<!-- Source: https://docs.cupy.dev/en/stable/reference/ndarray.html -->
# The N-dimensional array ([`ndarray`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray"))[#](#the-n-dimensional-array-ndarray "Permalink to this heading")
[`cupy.ndarray`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray") is the CuPy counterpart of NumPy [`numpy.ndarray`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)").
It provides an intuitive interface for a fixed-size multidimensional array which resides
in a CUDA device.
For the basic concept of `ndarray`s, please refer to the [NumPy documentation](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/arrays.ndarray.html).
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupy.ndarray`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray")(shape[, dtype, memptr, ...]) | Multi-dimensional array on a CUDA device. |
## Conversion to/from NumPy arrays[#](#conversion-to-from-numpy-arrays "Permalink to this heading")
[`cupy.ndarray`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray") and [`numpy.ndarray`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)") are not implicitly convertible to each other.
That means, NumPy functions cannot take [`cupy.ndarray`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray")s as inputs, and vice versa.
* To convert [`numpy.ndarray`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)") to [`cupy.ndarray`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray"), use [`cupy.array()`](generated/cupy.array.html#cupy.array "cupy.array") or [`cupy.asarray()`](generated/cupy.asarray.html#cupy.asarray "cupy.asarray").
* To convert [`cupy.ndarray`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray") to [`numpy.ndarray`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)"), use [`cupy.asnumpy()`](generated/cupy.asnumpy.html#cupy.asnumpy "cupy.asnumpy") or [`cupy.ndarray.get()`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray.get "cupy.ndarray.get").
Note that converting between [`cupy.ndarray`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray") and [`numpy.ndarray`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)") incurs data transfer between
the host (CPU) device and the GPU device, which is costly in terms of performance.
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupy.array`](generated/cupy.array.html#cupy.array "cupy.array")(obj[, dtype, copy, order, subok, ...]) | Creates an array on the current device. |
| [`cupy.asarray`](generated/cupy.asarray.html#cupy.asarray "cupy.asarray")(a[, dtype, order, copy, blocking]) | Converts an object to array. |
| [`cupy.asnumpy`](generated/cupy.asnumpy.html#cupy.asnumpy "cupy.asnumpy")(a[, stream, order, out, blocking]) | Returns an array on the host memory from an arbitrary source array. |
## Code compatibility features[#](#code-compatibility-features "Permalink to this heading")
[`cupy.ndarray`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray") is designed to be interchangeable with [`numpy.ndarray`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)") in terms of code compatibility as much as possible.
But occasionally, you will need to know whether the arrays you’re handling are [`cupy.ndarray`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray") or [`numpy.ndarray`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)").
One example is when invoking module-level functions such as [`cupy.sum()`](generated/cupy.sum.html#cupy.sum "cupy.sum") or [`numpy.sum()`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.sum.html#numpy.sum "(in NumPy v2.4)").
In such situations, [`cupy.get_array_module()`](generated/cupy.get_array_module.html#cupy.get_array_module "cupy.get_array_module") can be used.
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupy.get_array_module`](generated/cupy.get_array_module.html#cupy.get_array_module "cupy.get_array_module")(\*args) | Returns the array module for arguments. |
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupyx.scipy.get_array_module`](generated/cupyx.scipy.get_array_module.html#cupyx.scipy.get_array_module "cupyx.scipy.get_array_module")(\*args) | Returns the array module for arguments. |
[previous
API Reference](index.html "previous page")
[next
cupy.ndarray](generated/cupy.ndarray.html "next page")
On this page
* [Conversion to/from NumPy arrays](#conversion-to-from-numpy-arrays)
* [Code compatibility features](#code-compatibility-features)

<!-- Source: https://docs.cupy.dev/en/stable/reference/generated/cupy.ndarray.html -->
# cupy.ndarray[#](#cupy-ndarray "Permalink to this heading")
*class* cupy.ndarray(*shape*, *dtype=float*, *memptr=None*, *strides=None*, *order='C'*)[[source]](https://github.com/cupy/cupy/blob/v14.1.1/cupy/_core/core.pyx)[#](#cupy.ndarray "Permalink to this definition")
:   Multi-dimensional array on a CUDA device.
    This class implements a subset of methods of [`numpy.ndarray`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)").
    The difference is that this class allocates the array content on the
    current GPU device.
    Parameters:
    :   * **shape** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *ints*) – Length of axes.
        * **dtype** – Data type. It must be an argument of [`numpy.dtype`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.dtype.html#numpy.dtype "(in NumPy v2.4)").
        * **memptr** ([*cupy.cuda.MemoryPointer*](cupy.cuda.MemoryPointer.html#cupy.cuda.MemoryPointer "cupy.cuda.MemoryPointer")) – Pointer to the array content head.
        * **strides** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *ints* *or* *None*) – Strides of data in memory.
        * **order** (*{'C'**,* *'F'}*) – Row-major (C-style) or column-major
          (Fortran-style) order.
    Variables:
    :   * **base** (*None* *or* [*cupy.ndarray*](#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray")) – Base array from which this array is
          created as a view.
        * **data** ([*cupy.cuda.MemoryPointer*](cupy.cuda.MemoryPointer.html#cupy.cuda.MemoryPointer "cupy.cuda.MemoryPointer")) – Pointer to the array content head.
        * **~ndarray.dtype** ([*numpy.dtype*](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.dtype.html#numpy.dtype "(in NumPy v2.4)")) –
          Dtype object of element type.
          See also
          [Data type objects (dtype)](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/arrays.dtypes.html)
        * **~ndarray.size** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) –
          Number of elements this array holds.
          This is equivalent to product over the shape tuple.
          See also
          [`numpy.ndarray.size`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.size.html#numpy.ndarray.size "(in NumPy v2.4)")
    Methods
    \_\_getitem\_\_(*self*, *slices*)[#](#cupy.ndarray.__getitem__ "Permalink to this definition")
    :   x.\_\_getitem\_\_(y) <==> x[y]
        Supports both basic and advanced indexing.
        Note
        Currently, it does not support `slices` that consists of more
        than one boolean arrays
        Note
        CuPy handles out-of-bounds indices differently from NumPy.
        NumPy handles them by raising an error, but CuPy wraps around them.
        Example
        ```
        >>> a = cupy.arange(3)
        >>> a[[1, 3]]
        array([1, 0])
        ```
    \_\_setitem\_\_(*self*, *slices*, *value*)[#](#cupy.ndarray.__setitem__ "Permalink to this definition")
    :   x.\_\_setitem\_\_(slices, y) <==> x[slices] = y
        Supports both basic and advanced indexing.
        Note
        Currently, it does not support `slices` that consists of more
        than one boolean arrays
        Note
        CuPy handles out-of-bounds indices differently from NumPy when
        using integer array indexing.
        NumPy handles them by raising an error, but CuPy wraps around them.
        ```
        >>> import cupy
        >>> x = cupy.arange(3)
        >>> x[[1, 3]] = 10
        >>> x
        array([10, 10,  2])
        ```
        Note
        The behavior differs from NumPy when integer arrays in `slices`
        reference the same location multiple times.
        In that case, the value that is actually stored is undefined.
        ```
        >>> import cupy
        >>> a = cupy.zeros((2,))
        >>> i = cupy.arange(10000) % 2
        >>> v = cupy.arange(10000).astype(cupy.float64)
        >>> a[i] = v
        >>> a  
        array([9150., 9151.])
        ```
        On the other hand, NumPy stores the value corresponding to the
        last index among the indices referencing duplicate locations.
        ```
        >>> import numpy
        >>> a_cpu = numpy.zeros((2,))
        >>> i_cpu = numpy.arange(10000) % 2
        >>> v_cpu = numpy.arange(10000).astype(numpy.float64)
        >>> a_cpu[i_cpu] = v_cpu
        >>> a_cpu
        array([9998., 9999.])
        ```
    \_\_len\_\_()[#](#cupy.ndarray.__len__ "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Return len(self).
    \_\_iter\_\_()[#](#cupy.ndarray.__iter__ "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Implement iter(self).
    \_\_copy\_\_(*self*)[#](#cupy.ndarray.__copy__ "Permalink to this definition")
    all(*self*, *axis=None*, *out=None*, *keepdims=False*) → [ndarray](#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray")[#](#cupy.ndarray.all "Permalink to this definition")
    any(*self*, *axis=None*, *out=None*, *keepdims=False*) → [ndarray](#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray")[#](#cupy.ndarray.any "Permalink to this definition")
    argmax(*self*, *axis=None*, *out=None*, *dtype=None*, *keepdims=False*) → [ndarray](#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray")[#](#cupy.ndarray.argmax "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Returns the indices of the maximum along a given axis.
        Note
        `dtype` and `keepdim` arguments are specific to CuPy. They are
        not in NumPy.
        Note
        `axis` argument accepts a tuple of ints, but this is specific to
        CuPy. NumPy does not support it.
        See also
        [`cupy.argmax()`](cupy.argmax.html#cupy.argmax "cupy.argmax") for full documentation,
        [`numpy.ndarray.argmax()`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.argmax.html#numpy.ndarray.argmax "(in NumPy v2.4)")
    argmin(*self*, *axis=None*, *out=None*, *dtype=None*, *keepdims=False*) → [ndarray](#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray")[#](#cupy.ndarray.argmin "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Returns the indices of the minimum along a given axis.
        Note
        `dtype` and `keepdim` arguments are specific to CuPy. They are
        not in NumPy.
        Note
        `axis` argument accepts a tuple of ints, but this is specific to
        CuPy. NumPy does not support it.
        See also
        [`cupy.argmin()`](cupy.argmin.html#cupy.argmin "cupy.argmin") for full documentation,
        [`numpy.ndarray.argmin()`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.argmin.html#numpy.ndarray.argmin "(in NumPy v2.4)")
    argpartition(*self*, *kth*, *axis=-1*) → [ndarray](#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray")[#](#cupy.ndarray.argpartition "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Returns the indices that would partially sort an array.
        Parameters:
        :   * **kth** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") *or* *sequence* *of* *ints*) – Element index to partition by. If
              supplied with a sequence of k-th it will partition all elements
              indexed by k-th of them into their sorted position at once.
            * **axis** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") *or* *None*) – Axis along which to sort. Default is -1, which
              means sort along the last axis. If None is supplied, the array
              is flattened before sorting.
        Returns:
        :   Array of the same type and shape as `a`.
        Return type:
        :   [cupy.ndarray](#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray")
        See also
        [`cupy.argpartition()`](cupy.argpartition.html#cupy.argpartition "cupy.argpartition") for full documentation,
        [`numpy.ndarray.argpartition()`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.argpartition.html#numpy.ndarray.argpartition "(in NumPy v2.4)")
    argsort(*self*, *axis=-1*, *kind=None*) → [ndarray](#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray")[#](#cupy.ndarray.argsort "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Returns the indices that would sort an array with stable sorting
        Parameters:
        :   * **axis** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") *or* *None*) – Axis along which to sort. Default is -1, which
              means sort along the last axis. If None is supplied, the array
              is flattened before sorting.
            * **kind** – Default is None, which is equivalent to ‘stable’. Unlike in
              NumPy any other options are not accepted here.
        Returns:
        :   Array of indices that sort the array.
        Return type:
        :   [cupy.ndarray](#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray")
        See also
        [`cupy.argsort()`](cupy.argsort.html#cupy.argsort "cupy.argsort") for full documentation,
        [`numpy.ndarray.argsort()`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.argsort.html#numpy.ndarray.argsort "(in NumPy v2.4)")
    astype(*self*, *dtype*, *order='K'*, *casting=None*, *subok=None*, *copy=True*) → [ndarray](#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray")[#](#cupy.ndarray.astype "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Casts the array to given data type.
        Parameters:
        :   * **dtype** – Type specifier.
            * **order** (*{'C'**,* *'F'**,* *'A'**,* *'K'}*) – Row-major (C-style) or column-major
              (Fortran-style) order.
              When `order` is ‘A’, it uses ‘F’ if `a` is column-major and
              uses ‘C’ otherwise.
              And when `order` is ‘K’, it keeps strides as closely as
              possible.
            * **copy** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If it is False and no cast happens, then this method
              returns the array itself. Otherwise, a copy is returned.
        Returns:
        :   If `copy` is False and no cast is required, then the array itself
            is returned. Otherwise, it returns a (possibly casted) copy of the
            array.
        Note
        This method currently does not support `casting`, and `subok`
        arguments.
        See also
        [`numpy.ndarray.astype()`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.astype.html#numpy.ndarray.astype "(in NumPy v2.4)")
    byteswap(*self*, *inplace=False*) → [ndarray](#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray")[#](#cupy.ndarray.byteswap "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Swap the bytes of the array elements.
        Toggle between low-endian and big-endian data representation by
        returning a byteswapped array, optionally swapped in-place.
        Parameters:
        :   **inplace** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If `True`, swap bytes in-place. Default is
            `False`.
        Returns:
        :   The byteswapped array. If `inplace` is `True`,
            this is a view to self.
        Return type:
        :   [cupy.ndarray](#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray")
        See also
        [`numpy.ndarray.byteswap()`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.byteswap.html#numpy.ndarray.byteswap "(in NumPy v2.4)")

    copy(*self*, *order='C'*) → [ndarray](#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray")[#](#cupy.ndarray.copy "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Returns a copy of the array.
        This method makes a copy of a given array in the current device.
        Even when a given array is located in another device, you can copy it
        to the current device.
        Parameters:
        :   **order** (*{'C'**,* *'F'**,* *'A'**,* *'K'}*) – Row-major (C-style) or column-major
            (Fortran-style) order.
            When `order` is ‘A’, it uses ‘F’ if `a` is column-major and
            uses ‘C’ otherwise.
            And when order is ‘K’, it keeps strides as closely as
            possible.
        See also
        [`cupy.copy()`](cupy.copy.html#cupy.copy "cupy.copy") for full documentation,
        [`numpy.ndarray.copy()`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.copy.html#numpy.ndarray.copy "(in NumPy v2.4)")

    flatten(*self*, *order='C'*) → [ndarray](#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray")[#](#cupy.ndarray.flatten "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Returns a copy of the array flatten into one dimension.
        Parameters:
        :   **order** (*{'C'**,* *'F'**,* *'A'**,* *'K'}*) – ‘C’ means to flatten in row-major (C-style) order.
            ‘F’ means to flatten in column-major (Fortran-
            style) order. ‘A’ means to flatten in column-major
            order if self is Fortran *contiguous* in memory,
            row-major order otherwise. ‘K’ means to flatten
            self in the order the elements occur in memory.
            The default is ‘C’.
        Returns:
        :   A copy of the array with one dimension.
        Return type:
        :   [cupy.ndarray](#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray")
        See also
        [`numpy.ndarray.flatten()`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.flatten.html#numpy.ndarray.flatten "(in NumPy v2.4)")
    get(*self*, *stream=None*, *order='C'*, *out=None*, *blocking=True*)[#](#cupy.ndarray.get "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Returns a copy of the array on host memory.
        Parameters:
        :   * **stream** ([*cupy.cuda.Stream*](cupy.cuda.Stream.html#cupy.cuda.Stream "cupy.cuda.Stream")) – CUDA stream object. If given, the
              stream is used to perform the copy. Otherwise, the current
              stream is used.
            * **order** (*{'C'**,* *'F'**,* *'A'}*) – The desired memory layout of the host
              array. When `order` is ‘A’, it uses ‘F’ if the array is
              fortran-contiguous and ‘C’ otherwise. The `order` will be
              ignored if `out` is specified.
            * **out** ([*numpy.ndarray*](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")) – Output array. In order to enable asynchronous
              copy, the underlying memory should be a pinned memory.
            * **blocking** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If set to `False`, the copy runs asynchronously
              on the given (if given) or current stream, and users are
              responsible for ensuring the stream order. Default is `True`,
              so the copy is synchronous (with respect to the host).
        Returns:
        :   Copy of the array on host memory.
        Return type:
        :   [numpy.ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")

        See also
        [`cupy.amax()`](cupy.amax.html#cupy.amax "cupy.amax") for full documentation,
        [`numpy.ndarray.max()`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.max.html#numpy.ndarray.max "(in NumPy v2.4)")
    mdspan(*self*, *\**, *index\_type*, *allow\_unsafe=False*)[#](#cupy.ndarray.mdspan "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Returns an mdspan view of the array for use in CUDA kernels.
        This method creates a view of the CuPy array that is compatible with
        `cuda::std::mdspan` for use in custom CUDA kernels.
        Parameters:
        :   * **index\_type** (*dtype*) – The data type for extent and stride indices.
              Must be either `cupy.int32` or `cupy.int64`. If
              `cupy.int32` is specified, the array size must not exceed
              `INT32_MAX`.
            * **allow\_unsafe** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If True, allows creating an mdspan for arrays
              that have either zero or negative strides, or one or more
              dimensions of size zero. Depending on the access pattern such
              mdspan may lead to undefined behavior when described with
              either a left-, right, or stride- layout\_stride as per C++
              standard. For example, `mdspan.required_span_size()` might
              become negative. Default is False.
        Returns:
        :   An mdspan view of the array that can be passed to CUDA
            kernels as a kernel argument.
        Return type:
        :   mdspan
        Raises:
        :   [**ValueError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError "(in Python v3.14)") – If `index_type` is not `cupy.int32` or
            `cupy.int64`, or if the array size exceeds the range of
            the specified `index_type`.
        Note
        The returned mdspan can work with either `layout_stride`,
        `layout_left`, or `layout_right`, but your kernel must declare
        the mdspan type with all extents being **dynamic**:
        ```
        template<typename T, typename IndexType>
        __global__ void my_kernel(
            cuda::std::mdspan<
                T,
                cuda::std::extents<
                    IndexType,
                    cuda::std::dynamic_extent,
                    cuda::std::dynamic_extent>,
                cuda::std::layout_stride> arr
        ) {
            // arr is a 2D mdspan
            // Access: arr(i, j)
        }
        ```
        **Static extents are NOT supported.** For example, using
        `extents<int, 4, 8>` will result in undefined behavior.
        Example
        ```
        >>> import cupy
        >>> a = cupy.arange(12, dtype=cupy.float32).reshape(3, 4)
        >>> a_mdspan = a.mdspan(index_type=cupy.int64)
        >>> # Pass a_mdspan to a cupy.RawKernel expecting:
        >>> # mdspan<float, extents<int64_t, dyn, dyn>, layout_stride>
        ```
    mean(*self*, *axis=None*, *dtype=None*, *out=None*, *keepdims=False*) → [ndarray](#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray")[#](#cupy.ndarray.mean "Permalink to this definition")

              stream is used to perform the copy. Otherwise, the current
              stream is used.
    sort(*self*, *int axis=-1*, *kind=None*)[#](#cupy.ndarray.sort "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Sort an array, in-place with a stable sorting algorithm.
        Parameters:
        :   * **axis** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – Axis along which to sort. Default is -1, which means
              sort along the last axis.
            * **kind** – Default is None, which is equivalent to ‘stable’. Unlike in
              NumPy any other options are not accepted here.
        Note
        For its implementation reason, `ndarray.sort` currently supports
        only arrays with their own data, and does not fully support `kind`
        and `order` parameters that `numpy.ndarray.sort` does support.
        See also
        [`cupy.sort()`](cupy.sort.html#cupy.sort "cupy.sort") for full documentation,
        [`numpy.ndarray.sort()`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.sort.html#numpy.ndarray.sort "(in NumPy v2.4)")
    squeeze(*self*, *axis=None*) → [ndarray](#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray")[#](#cupy.ndarray.squeeze "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Returns a view with size-one axes removed.
        See also
        [`cupy.squeeze()`](cupy.squeeze.html#cupy.squeeze "cupy.squeeze") for full documentation,
        [`numpy.ndarray.squeeze()`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.squeeze.html#numpy.ndarray.squeeze "(in NumPy v2.4)")
    std(*self*, *axis=None*, *dtype=None*, *out=None*, *ddof=0*, *keepdims=False*) → [ndarray](#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray")[#](#cupy.ndarray.std "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Returns the standard deviation along a given axis.
        See also
        [`cupy.std()`](cupy.std.html#cupy.std "cupy.std") for full documentation,
        [`numpy.ndarray.std()`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.std.html#numpy.ndarray.std "(in NumPy v2.4)")
    sum(*self*, *axis=None*, *dtype=None*, *out=None*, *keepdims=False*) → [ndarray](#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray")[#](#cupy.ndarray.sum "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Returns the sum along a given axis.
        See also
        [`cupy.sum()`](cupy.sum.html#cupy.sum "cupy.sum") for full documentation,
        [`numpy.ndarray.sum()`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.sum.html#numpy.ndarray.sum "(in NumPy v2.4)")
    swapaxes(*self*, *Py\_ssize\_t axis1*, *Py\_ssize\_t axis2*) → [ndarray](#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray")[#](#cupy.ndarray.swapaxes "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Returns a view of the array with two axes swapped.
        See also
        [`cupy.swapaxes()`](cupy.swapaxes.html#cupy.swapaxes "cupy.swapaxes") for full documentation,
        [`numpy.ndarray.swapaxes()`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.swapaxes.html#numpy.ndarray.swapaxes "(in NumPy v2.4)")
    take(*self*, *indices*, *axis=None*, *out=None*) → [ndarray](#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray")[#](#cupy.ndarray.take "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Returns an array of elements at given indices along the axis.
        See also
        [`cupy.take()`](cupy.take.html#cupy.take "cupy.take") for full documentation,
        [`numpy.ndarray.take()`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.take.html#numpy.ndarray.take "(in NumPy v2.4)")
    toDlpack(*self*)[#](#cupy.ndarray.toDlpack "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Zero-copy conversion to a DLPack tensor.
        DLPack is a open in memory tensor structure proposed in this
        repository: [dmlc/dlpack](https://github.com/dmlc/dlpack).
        This function returns a `PyCapsule` object which contains a
        pointer to a DLPack tensor converted from the own ndarray. This
        function does not copy the own data to the output DLpack tensor
        but it shares the pointer which is pointing to the same memory region
        for the data.
        Returns:
        :   Output DLPack tensor which is
            encapsulated in a `PyCapsule` object.
        Return type:
        :   dltensor (`PyCapsule`)
        Warning
        This method is deprecated and will be removed in a future release.
        In particular, we discourage explicit user management of any
        `PyCapsule` objects. Use the [`from_dlpack()`](cupy.from_dlpack.html#cupy.from_dlpack "cupy.from_dlpack")
        array constructor instead.
    tobytes(*self*, *order='C'*) → [bytes](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#bytes "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#cupy.ndarray.tobytes "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Turns the array into a Python bytes object.
    tofile(*self*, *fid*, *sep=''*, *format='%s'*)[#](#cupy.ndarray.tofile "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Writes the array to a file.
        See also
        [`numpy.ndarray.tofile()`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.tofile.html#numpy.ndarray.tofile "(in NumPy v2.4)")
    tolist(*self*)[#](#cupy.ndarray.tolist "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Converts the array to a (possibly nested) Python list.
        Returns:
        :   The possibly nested Python list of array elements.
        Return type:
        :   [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")
        See also
        [`numpy.ndarray.tolist()`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.tolist.html#numpy.ndarray.tolist "(in NumPy v2.4)")
    trace(*self*, *offset=0*, *axis1=0*, *axis2=1*, *dtype=None*, *out=None*) → [ndarray](#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray")[#](#cupy.ndarray.trace "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Returns the sum along diagonals of the array.
        See also
        [`cupy.trace()`](cupy.trace.html#cupy.trace "cupy.trace") for full documentation,
        [`numpy.ndarray.trace()`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.trace.html#numpy.ndarray.trace "(in NumPy v2.4)")
    transpose(*self*, *\*axes*)[#](#cupy.ndarray.transpose "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Returns a view of the array with axes permuted.
        See also
        [`cupy.transpose()`](cupy.transpose.html#cupy.transpose "cupy.transpose") for full documentation,
        [`numpy.ndarray.reshape()`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.reshape.html#numpy.ndarray.reshape "(in NumPy v2.4)")
    var(*self*, *axis=None*, *dtype=None*, *out=None*, *ddof=0*, *keepdims=False*) → [ndarray](#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray")[#](#cupy.ndarray.var "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Returns the variance along a given axis.
        See also
        [`cupy.var()`](cupy.var.html#cupy.var "cupy.var") for full documentation,
        [`numpy.ndarray.var()`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.var.html#numpy.ndarray.var "(in NumPy v2.4)")
    view(*self*, *dtype=None*, *type=None*)[#](#cupy.ndarray.view "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Returns a view of the array.
        Parameters:
        :   **dtype** – If this is different from the data type of the array, the
            returned view reinterpret the memory sequence as an array of
            this type.
        Returns:
        :   A view of the array. A reference to the original
            array is stored at the [`base`](#cupy.ndarray.base "cupy.ndarray.base") attribute.
        Return type:
        :   [cupy.ndarray](#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray")
        See also
        [`numpy.ndarray.view()`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.view.html#numpy.ndarray.view "(in NumPy v2.4)")
    \_\_eq\_\_(*value*, */*)[#](#cupy.ndarray.__eq__ "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Return self==value.
    \_\_ne\_\_(*value*, */*)[#](#cupy.ndarray.__ne__ "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Return self!=value.
    \_\_lt\_\_(*value*, */*)[#](#cupy.ndarray.__lt__ "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Return self<value.
    \_\_le\_\_(*value*, */*)[#](#cupy.ndarray.__le__ "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Return self<=value.
    \_\_gt\_\_(*value*, */*)[#](#cupy.ndarray.__gt__ "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Return self>value.
    \_\_ge\_\_(*value*, */*)[#](#cupy.ndarray.__ge__ "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Return self>=value.
    \_\_bool\_\_()[#](#cupy.ndarray.__bool__ "Permalink to this definition")
    :   True if self else False
    Attributes
    T[#](#cupy.ndarray.T "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Shape-reversed view of the array.
        If ndim < 2, then this is just a reference to the array itself.
    base[#](#cupy.ndarray.base "Permalink to this definition")
    cstruct[#](#cupy.ndarray.cstruct "Permalink to this definition")
    :   C representation of the array.
        This property is used for sending an array to CUDA kernels. The type of
        returned C structure is different for different dtypes and ndims. The
        definition of C type is written in `cupy/carray.cuh`.
    data[#](#cupy.ndarray.data "Permalink to this definition")
    device[#](#cupy.ndarray.device "Permalink to this definition")
    :   CUDA device on which this array resides.
    dtype[#](#cupy.ndarray.dtype "Permalink to this definition")
    flags[#](#cupy.ndarray.flags "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Object containing memory-layout information.
        It only contains `c_contiguous`, `f_contiguous`, and `owndata`
        attributes. All of these are read-only. Accessing by indexes is also
        supported.
        See also
        [`numpy.ndarray.flags`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.flags.html#numpy.ndarray.flags "(in NumPy v2.4)")
    flat[#](#cupy.ndarray.flat "Permalink to this definition")
    imag[#](#cupy.ndarray.imag "Permalink to this definition")
    itemsize[#](#cupy.ndarray.itemsize "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Size of each element in bytes.
        See also
        [`numpy.ndarray.itemsize`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.itemsize.html#numpy.ndarray.itemsize "(in NumPy v2.4)")
    mT[#](#cupy.ndarray.mT "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Matrix-transpose view of the array.
        If ndim < 2, raise a ValueError.
    nbytes[#](#cupy.ndarray.nbytes "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Total size of all elements in bytes.
        It does not count skips between elements.
        See also
        [`numpy.ndarray.nbytes`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.nbytes.html#numpy.ndarray.nbytes "(in NumPy v2.4)")
    ndim[#](#cupy.ndarray.ndim "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Number of dimensions.
        `a.ndim` is equivalent to `len(a.shape)`.
        See also
        [`numpy.ndarray.ndim`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.ndim.html#numpy.ndarray.ndim "(in NumPy v2.4)")
    real[#](#cupy.ndarray.real "Permalink to this definition")
    shape[#](#cupy.ndarray.shape "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Lengths of axes.
        Setter of this property involves reshaping without copy. If the array
        cannot be reshaped without copy, it raises an exception.
    size[#](#cupy.ndarray.size "Permalink to this definition")
    strides[#](#cupy.ndarray.strides "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Strides of axes in bytes.
        See also
        [`numpy.ndarray.strides`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.strides.html#numpy.ndarray.strides "(in NumPy v2.4)")

<!-- Source: https://docs.cupy.dev/en/stable/reference/ndarray.html#conversion-to-from-numpy-arrays -->
# The N-dimensional array ([`ndarray`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray"))[#](#the-n-dimensional-array-ndarray "Permalink to this heading")
[`cupy.ndarray`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray") is the CuPy counterpart of NumPy [`numpy.ndarray`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)").
It provides an intuitive interface for a fixed-size multidimensional array which resides
in a CUDA device.
For the basic concept of `ndarray`s, please refer to the [NumPy documentation](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/arrays.ndarray.html).
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupy.ndarray`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray")(shape[, dtype, memptr, ...]) | Multi-dimensional array on a CUDA device. |
## Conversion to/from NumPy arrays[#](#conversion-to-from-numpy-arrays "Permalink to this heading")
[`cupy.ndarray`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray") and [`numpy.ndarray`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)") are not implicitly convertible to each other.
That means, NumPy functions cannot take [`cupy.ndarray`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray")s as inputs, and vice versa.
* To convert [`numpy.ndarray`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)") to [`cupy.ndarray`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray"), use [`cupy.array()`](generated/cupy.array.html#cupy.array "cupy.array") or [`cupy.asarray()`](generated/cupy.asarray.html#cupy.asarray "cupy.asarray").
* To convert [`cupy.ndarray`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray") to [`numpy.ndarray`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)"), use [`cupy.asnumpy()`](generated/cupy.asnumpy.html#cupy.asnumpy "cupy.asnumpy") or [`cupy.ndarray.get()`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray.get "cupy.ndarray.get").
Note that converting between [`cupy.ndarray`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray") and [`numpy.ndarray`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)") incurs data transfer between
the host (CPU) device and the GPU device, which is costly in terms of performance.
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupy.array`](generated/cupy.array.html#cupy.array "cupy.array")(obj[, dtype, copy, order, subok, ...]) | Creates an array on the current device. |
| [`cupy.asarray`](generated/cupy.asarray.html#cupy.asarray "cupy.asarray")(a[, dtype, order, copy, blocking]) | Converts an object to array. |
| [`cupy.asnumpy`](generated/cupy.asnumpy.html#cupy.asnumpy "cupy.asnumpy")(a[, stream, order, out, blocking]) | Returns an array on the host memory from an arbitrary source array. |
## Code compatibility features[#](#code-compatibility-features "Permalink to this heading")
[`cupy.ndarray`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray") is designed to be interchangeable with [`numpy.ndarray`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)") in terms of code compatibility as much as possible.
But occasionally, you will need to know whether the arrays you’re handling are [`cupy.ndarray`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray") or [`numpy.ndarray`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)").
One example is when invoking module-level functions such as [`cupy.sum()`](generated/cupy.sum.html#cupy.sum "cupy.sum") or [`numpy.sum()`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.sum.html#numpy.sum "(in NumPy v2.4)").
In such situations, [`cupy.get_array_module()`](generated/cupy.get_array_module.html#cupy.get_array_module "cupy.get_array_module") can be used.
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupy.get_array_module`](generated/cupy.get_array_module.html#cupy.get_array_module "cupy.get_array_module")(\*args) | Returns the array module for arguments. |
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupyx.scipy.get_array_module`](generated/cupyx.scipy.get_array_module.html#cupyx.scipy.get_array_module "cupyx.scipy.get_array_module")(\*args) | Returns the array module for arguments. |
[previous
API Reference](index.html "previous page")
[next
cupy.ndarray](generated/cupy.ndarray.html "next page")
On this page
* [Conversion to/from NumPy arrays](#conversion-to-from-numpy-arrays)
* [Code compatibility features](#code-compatibility-features)
---

# The N-dimensional array ([`ndarray`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray"))[#](#the-n-dimensional-array-ndarray "Permalink to this heading")
[`cupy.ndarray`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray") is the CuPy counterpart of NumPy [`numpy.ndarray`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)").
It provides an intuitive interface for a fixed-size multidimensional array which resides
in a CUDA device.
For the basic concept of `ndarray`s, please refer to the [NumPy documentation](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/arrays.ndarray.html).
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupy.ndarray`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray")(shape[, dtype, memptr, ...]) | Multi-dimensional array on a CUDA device. |
## Conversion to/from NumPy arrays[#](#conversion-to-from-numpy-arrays "Permalink to this heading")
[`cupy.ndarray`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray") and [`numpy.ndarray`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)") are not implicitly convertible to each other.
That means, NumPy functions cannot take [`cupy.ndarray`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray")s as inputs, and vice versa.
* To convert [`numpy.ndarray`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)") to [`cupy.ndarray`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray"), use [`cupy.array()`](generated/cupy.array.html#cupy.array "cupy.array") or [`cupy.asarray()`](generated/cupy.asarray.html#cupy.asarray "cupy.asarray").
* To convert [`cupy.ndarray`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray") to [`numpy.ndarray`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)"), use [`cupy.asnumpy()`](generated/cupy.asnumpy.html#cupy.asnumpy "cupy.asnumpy") or [`cupy.ndarray.get()`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray.get "cupy.ndarray.get").
Note that converting between [`cupy.ndarray`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray") and [`numpy.ndarray`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)") incurs data transfer between
the host (CPU) device and the GPU device, which is costly in terms of performance.
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupy.array`](generated/cupy.array.html#cupy.array "cupy.array")(obj[, dtype, copy, order, subok, ...]) | Creates an array on the current device. |
| [`cupy.asarray`](generated/cupy.asarray.html#cupy.asarray "cupy.asarray")(a[, dtype, order, copy, blocking]) | Converts an object to array. |
| [`cupy.asnumpy`](generated/cupy.asnumpy.html#cupy.asnumpy "cupy.asnumpy")(a[, stream, order, out, blocking]) | Returns an array on the host memory from an arbitrary source array. |
## Code compatibility features[#](#code-compatibility-features "Permalink to this heading")
[`cupy.ndarray`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray") is designed to be interchangeable with [`numpy.ndarray`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)") in terms of code compatibility as much as possible.
But occasionally, you will need to know whether the arrays you’re handling are [`cupy.ndarray`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray") or [`numpy.ndarray`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)").
One example is when invoking module-level functions such as [`cupy.sum()`](generated/cupy.sum.html#cupy.sum "cupy.sum") or [`numpy.sum()`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.sum.html#numpy.sum "(in NumPy v2.4)").
In such situations, [`cupy.get_array_module()`](generated/cupy.get_array_module.html#cupy.get_array_module "cupy.get_array_module") can be used.
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupy.get_array_module`](generated/cupy.get_array_module.html#cupy.get_array_module "cupy.get_array_module")(\*args) | Returns the array module for arguments. |
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupyx.scipy.get_array_module`](generated/cupyx.scipy.get_array_module.html#cupyx.scipy.get_array_module "cupyx.scipy.get_array_module")(\*args) | Returns the array module for arguments. |

API Reference](index.html "previous page")

cupy.ndarray](generated/cupy.ndarray.html "next page")
On this page
* [Conversion to/from NumPy arrays](#conversion-to-from-numpy-arrays)
* [Code compatibility features](#code-compatibility-features)

<!-- Source: https://docs.cupy.dev/en/stable/reference/ufunc.html -->
# Universal functions ([`cupy.ufunc`](generated/cupy.ufunc.html#cupy.ufunc "cupy.ufunc"))[#](#universal-functions-cupy-ufunc "Permalink to this heading")
Hint
[NumPy API Reference: Universal functions (numpy.ufunc)](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/ufuncs.html)
CuPy provides universal functions (a.k.a. ufuncs) to support various elementwise operations.
CuPy’s ufunc supports following features of NumPy’s one:
* Broadcasting
* Output type determination
* Casting rules
## ufunc[#](#ufunc "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`ufunc`](generated/cupy.ufunc.html#cupy.ufunc "cupy.ufunc")(name, nin, nout, \_Ops ops[, preamble, ...]) | Universal function. |
### Methods[#](#methods "Permalink to this heading")
These methods are only available for selected ufuncs.
* [`ufunc.reduce`](generated/cupy.ufunc.html#cupy.ufunc.reduce "cupy.ufunc.reduce"): [`add()`](generated/cupy.add.html#cupy.add "cupy.add"), [`multiply()`](generated/cupy.multiply.html#cupy.multiply "cupy.multiply")
* [`ufunc.accumulate`](generated/cupy.ufunc.html#cupy.ufunc.accumulate "cupy.ufunc.accumulate"): [`add()`](generated/cupy.add.html#cupy.add "cupy.add"), [`multiply()`](generated/cupy.multiply.html#cupy.multiply "cupy.multiply")
* [`ufunc.reduceat`](generated/cupy.ufunc.html#cupy.ufunc.reduceat "cupy.ufunc.reduceat"): [`add()`](generated/cupy.add.html#cupy.add "cupy.add")
* [`ufunc.outer`](generated/cupy.ufunc.html#cupy.ufunc.outer "cupy.ufunc.outer"): All ufuncs
* [`ufunc.at`](generated/cupy.ufunc.html#cupy.ufunc.at "cupy.ufunc.at"): [`add()`](generated/cupy.add.html#cupy.add "cupy.add"), [`subtract()`](generated/cupy.subtract.html#cupy.subtract "cupy.subtract"), [`maximum()`](generated/cupy.maximum.html#cupy.maximum "cupy.maximum"), [`minimum()`](generated/cupy.minimum.html#cupy.minimum "cupy.minimum"), [`bitwise_and()`](generated/cupy.bitwise_and.html#cupy.bitwise_and "cupy.bitwise_and"), [`bitwise_or()`](generated/cupy.bitwise_or.html#cupy.bitwise_or "cupy.bitwise_or"), [`bitwise_xor()`](generated/cupy.bitwise_xor.html#cupy.bitwise_xor "cupy.bitwise_xor")
Hint
In case you need support for other ufuncs, submit a feature request along with your use-case in [the tracker issue](https://github.com/cupy/cupy/issues/7082).
## Available ufuncs[#](#available-ufuncs "Permalink to this heading")

| [`nextafter`](generated/cupy.nextafter.html#cupy.nextafter "cupy.nextafter")(x1, x2, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Computes the nearest neighbor float values towards the second argument. |
| [`modf`](generated/cupy.modf.html#cupy.modf "cupy.modf")(x[, out1, out2], / [[, out, casting, dtype]) | Extracts the fractional and integral parts of an array elementwise. |
| [`ldexp`](generated/cupy.ldexp.html#cupy.ldexp "cupy.ldexp")(x1, x2, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Computes `x1 * 2 ** x2` elementwise. |
| [`frexp`](generated/cupy.frexp.html#cupy.frexp "cupy.frexp")(x[, out1, out2], / [[, out, casting, ...]) | Decomposes each element to mantissa and two's exponent. |
| [`fmod`](generated/cupy.fmod.html#cupy.fmod "cupy.fmod")(x1, x2, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Computes the remainder of C division elementwise. |
| [`floor`](generated/cupy.floor.html#cupy.floor "cupy.floor")(x, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Rounds each element of an array to its floor integer. |
| [`ceil`](generated/cupy.ceil.html#cupy.ceil "cupy.ceil")(x, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Rounds each element of an array to its ceiling integer. |
| [`trunc`](generated/cupy.trunc.html#cupy.trunc "cupy.trunc")(x, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Rounds each element of an array towards zero. |
## Generalized Universal Functions[#](#generalized-universal-functions "Permalink to this heading")
In addition to regular ufuncs, CuPy also provides a wrapper class to convert
regular cupy functions into Generalized Universal Functions as in NumPy <https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/c-api/generalized-ufuncs.html>.
This allows to automatically use keyword arguments such as `axes`, `order`, `dtype`
without needing to explicitly implement them in the wrapped function.
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`GeneralizedUFunc`](generated/cupyx.GeneralizedUFunc.html#cupyx.GeneralizedUFunc "cupyx.GeneralizedUFunc")(func, signature, \*[, name, ...]) | Creates a Generalized Universal Function by wrapping a user provided function with the signature. |

# Universal functions ([`cupy.ufunc`](generated/cupy.ufunc.html#cupy.ufunc "cupy.ufunc"))[#](#universal-functions-cupy-ufunc "Permalink to this heading")
Hint
[NumPy API Reference: Universal functions (numpy.ufunc)](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/ufuncs.html)
CuPy provides universal functions (a.k.a. ufuncs) to support various elementwise operations.
CuPy’s ufunc supports following features of NumPy’s one:
* Broadcasting
* Output type determination
* Casting rules
## ufunc[#](#ufunc "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`ufunc`](generated/cupy.ufunc.html#cupy.ufunc "cupy.ufunc")(name, nin, nout, \_Ops ops[, preamble, ...]) | Universal function. |
### Methods[#](#methods "Permalink to this heading")
These methods are only available for selected ufuncs.
* [`ufunc.reduce`](generated/cupy.ufunc.html#cupy.ufunc.reduce "cupy.ufunc.reduce"): [`add()`](generated/cupy.add.html#cupy.add "cupy.add"), [`multiply()`](generated/cupy.multiply.html#cupy.multiply "cupy.multiply")
* [`ufunc.accumulate`](generated/cupy.ufunc.html#cupy.ufunc.accumulate "cupy.ufunc.accumulate"): [`add()`](generated/cupy.add.html#cupy.add "cupy.add"), [`multiply()`](generated/cupy.multiply.html#cupy.multiply "cupy.multiply")
* [`ufunc.reduceat`](generated/cupy.ufunc.html#cupy.ufunc.reduceat "cupy.ufunc.reduceat"): [`add()`](generated/cupy.add.html#cupy.add "cupy.add")
* [`ufunc.outer`](generated/cupy.ufunc.html#cupy.ufunc.outer "cupy.ufunc.outer"): All ufuncs
* [`ufunc.at`](generated/cupy.ufunc.html#cupy.ufunc.at "cupy.ufunc.at"): [`add()`](generated/cupy.add.html#cupy.add "cupy.add"), [`subtract()`](generated/cupy.subtract.html#cupy.subtract "cupy.subtract"), [`maximum()`](generated/cupy.maximum.html#cupy.maximum "cupy.maximum"), [`minimum()`](generated/cupy.minimum.html#cupy.minimum "cupy.minimum"), [`bitwise_and()`](generated/cupy.bitwise_and.html#cupy.bitwise_and "cupy.bitwise_and"), [`bitwise_or()`](generated/cupy.bitwise_or.html#cupy.bitwise_or "cupy.bitwise_or"), [`bitwise_xor()`](generated/cupy.bitwise_xor.html#cupy.bitwise_xor "cupy.bitwise_xor")
Hint
In case you need support for other ufuncs, submit a feature request along with your use-case in [the tracker issue](https://github.com/cupy/cupy/issues/7082).

## Generalized Universal Functions[#](#generalized-universal-functions "Permalink to this heading")
In addition to regular ufuncs, CuPy also provides a wrapper class to convert
regular cupy functions into Generalized Universal Functions as in NumPy <https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/c-api/generalized-ufuncs.html>.
This allows to automatically use keyword arguments such as `axes`, `order`, `dtype`
without needing to explicitly implement them in the wrapped function.
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`GeneralizedUFunc`](generated/cupyx.GeneralizedUFunc.html#cupyx.GeneralizedUFunc "cupyx.GeneralizedUFunc")(func, signature, \*[, name, ...]) | Creates a Generalized Universal Function by wrapping a user provided function with the signature. |

<!-- Source: https://docs.cupy.dev/en/stable/reference/ufunc.html#available-ufuncs -->
# Universal functions ([`cupy.ufunc`](generated/cupy.ufunc.html#cupy.ufunc "cupy.ufunc"))[#](#universal-functions-cupy-ufunc "Permalink to this heading")
Hint
[NumPy API Reference: Universal functions (numpy.ufunc)](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/ufuncs.html)
CuPy provides universal functions (a.k.a. ufuncs) to support various elementwise operations.
CuPy’s ufunc supports following features of NumPy’s one:
* Broadcasting
* Output type determination
* Casting rules
## ufunc[#](#ufunc "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`ufunc`](generated/cupy.ufunc.html#cupy.ufunc "cupy.ufunc")(name, nin, nout, \_Ops ops[, preamble, ...]) | Universal function. |
### Methods[#](#methods "Permalink to this heading")
These methods are only available for selected ufuncs.
* [`ufunc.reduce`](generated/cupy.ufunc.html#cupy.ufunc.reduce "cupy.ufunc.reduce"): [`add()`](generated/cupy.add.html#cupy.add "cupy.add"), [`multiply()`](generated/cupy.multiply.html#cupy.multiply "cupy.multiply")
* [`ufunc.accumulate`](generated/cupy.ufunc.html#cupy.ufunc.accumulate "cupy.ufunc.accumulate"): [`add()`](generated/cupy.add.html#cupy.add "cupy.add"), [`multiply()`](generated/cupy.multiply.html#cupy.multiply "cupy.multiply")
* [`ufunc.reduceat`](generated/cupy.ufunc.html#cupy.ufunc.reduceat "cupy.ufunc.reduceat"): [`add()`](generated/cupy.add.html#cupy.add "cupy.add")
* [`ufunc.outer`](generated/cupy.ufunc.html#cupy.ufunc.outer "cupy.ufunc.outer"): All ufuncs
* [`ufunc.at`](generated/cupy.ufunc.html#cupy.ufunc.at "cupy.ufunc.at"): [`add()`](generated/cupy.add.html#cupy.add "cupy.add"), [`subtract()`](generated/cupy.subtract.html#cupy.subtract "cupy.subtract"), [`maximum()`](generated/cupy.maximum.html#cupy.maximum "cupy.maximum"), [`minimum()`](generated/cupy.minimum.html#cupy.minimum "cupy.minimum"), [`bitwise_and()`](generated/cupy.bitwise_and.html#cupy.bitwise_and "cupy.bitwise_and"), [`bitwise_or()`](generated/cupy.bitwise_or.html#cupy.bitwise_or "cupy.bitwise_or"), [`bitwise_xor()`](generated/cupy.bitwise_xor.html#cupy.bitwise_xor "cupy.bitwise_xor")
Hint
In case you need support for other ufuncs, submit a feature request along with your use-case in [the tracker issue](https://github.com/cupy/cupy/issues/7082).

| [`negative`](generated/cupy.negative.html#cupy.negative "cupy.negative")(x, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Takes numerical negative elementwise. |
| [`positive`](generated/cupy.positive.html#cupy.positive "cupy.positive")(x, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Takes numerical positive elementwise. |

## Generalized Universal Functions[#](#generalized-universal-functions "Permalink to this heading")
In addition to regular ufuncs, CuPy also provides a wrapper class to convert
regular cupy functions into Generalized Universal Functions as in NumPy <https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/c-api/generalized-ufuncs.html>.
This allows to automatically use keyword arguments such as `axes`, `order`, `dtype`
without needing to explicitly implement them in the wrapped function.
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`GeneralizedUFunc`](generated/cupyx.GeneralizedUFunc.html#cupyx.GeneralizedUFunc "cupyx.GeneralizedUFunc")(func, signature, \*[, name, ...]) | Creates a Generalized Universal Function by wrapping a user provided function with the signature. |

# Universal functions ([`cupy.ufunc`](generated/cupy.ufunc.html#cupy.ufunc "cupy.ufunc"))[#](#universal-functions-cupy-ufunc "Permalink to this heading")
Hint
[NumPy API Reference: Universal functions (numpy.ufunc)](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/ufuncs.html)
CuPy provides universal functions (a.k.a. ufuncs) to support various elementwise operations.
CuPy’s ufunc supports following features of NumPy’s one:
* Broadcasting
* Output type determination
* Casting rules
## ufunc[#](#ufunc "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`ufunc`](generated/cupy.ufunc.html#cupy.ufunc "cupy.ufunc")(name, nin, nout, \_Ops ops[, preamble, ...]) | Universal function. |
### Methods[#](#methods "Permalink to this heading")
These methods are only available for selected ufuncs.
* [`ufunc.reduce`](generated/cupy.ufunc.html#cupy.ufunc.reduce "cupy.ufunc.reduce"): [`add()`](generated/cupy.add.html#cupy.add "cupy.add"), [`multiply()`](generated/cupy.multiply.html#cupy.multiply "cupy.multiply")
* [`ufunc.accumulate`](generated/cupy.ufunc.html#cupy.ufunc.accumulate "cupy.ufunc.accumulate"): [`add()`](generated/cupy.add.html#cupy.add "cupy.add"), [`multiply()`](generated/cupy.multiply.html#cupy.multiply "cupy.multiply")
* [`ufunc.reduceat`](generated/cupy.ufunc.html#cupy.ufunc.reduceat "cupy.ufunc.reduceat"): [`add()`](generated/cupy.add.html#cupy.add "cupy.add")
* [`ufunc.outer`](generated/cupy.ufunc.html#cupy.ufunc.outer "cupy.ufunc.outer"): All ufuncs
* [`ufunc.at`](generated/cupy.ufunc.html#cupy.ufunc.at "cupy.ufunc.at"): [`add()`](generated/cupy.add.html#cupy.add "cupy.add"), [`subtract()`](generated/cupy.subtract.html#cupy.subtract "cupy.subtract"), [`maximum()`](generated/cupy.maximum.html#cupy.maximum "cupy.maximum"), [`minimum()`](generated/cupy.minimum.html#cupy.minimum "cupy.minimum"), [`bitwise_and()`](generated/cupy.bitwise_and.html#cupy.bitwise_and "cupy.bitwise_and"), [`bitwise_or()`](generated/cupy.bitwise_or.html#cupy.bitwise_or "cupy.bitwise_or"), [`bitwise_xor()`](generated/cupy.bitwise_xor.html#cupy.bitwise_xor "cupy.bitwise_xor")
Hint
In case you need support for other ufuncs, submit a feature request along with your use-case in [the tracker issue](https://github.com/cupy/cupy/issues/7082).

| [`cbrt`](generated/cupy.cbrt.html#cupy.cbrt "cupy.cbrt")(x, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Elementwise cube root function. |
| [`reciprocal`](generated/cupy.reciprocal.html#cupy.reciprocal "cupy.reciprocal")(x, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Computes `1 / x` elementwise. |
| [`gcd`](generated/cupy.gcd.html#cupy.gcd "cupy.gcd")(x1, x2, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Computes gcd of `x1` and `x2` elementwise. |

| [`nextafter`](generated/cupy.nextafter.html#cupy.nextafter "cupy.nextafter")(x1, x2, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Computes the nearest neighbor float values towards the second argument. |
| [`modf`](generated/cupy.modf.html#cupy.modf "cupy.modf")(x[, out1, out2], / [[, out, casting, dtype]) | Extracts the fractional and integral parts of an array elementwise. |
| [`ldexp`](generated/cupy.ldexp.html#cupy.ldexp "cupy.ldexp")(x1, x2, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Computes `x1 * 2 ** x2` elementwise. |
| [`frexp`](generated/cupy.frexp.html#cupy.frexp "cupy.frexp")(x[, out1, out2], / [[, out, casting, ...]) | Decomposes each element to mantissa and two's exponent. |
| [`fmod`](generated/cupy.fmod.html#cupy.fmod "cupy.fmod")(x1, x2, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Computes the remainder of C division elementwise. |
| [`floor`](generated/cupy.floor.html#cupy.floor "cupy.floor")(x, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Rounds each element of an array to its floor integer. |
| [`ceil`](generated/cupy.ceil.html#cupy.ceil "cupy.ceil")(x, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Rounds each element of an array to its ceiling integer. |
| [`trunc`](generated/cupy.trunc.html#cupy.trunc "cupy.trunc")(x, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Rounds each element of an array towards zero. |
## Generalized Universal Functions[#](#generalized-universal-functions "Permalink to this heading")
In addition to regular ufuncs, CuPy also provides a wrapper class to convert
regular cupy functions into Generalized Universal Functions as in NumPy <https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/c-api/generalized-ufuncs.html>.
This allows to automatically use keyword arguments such as `axes`, `order`, `dtype`
without needing to explicitly implement them in the wrapped function.
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`GeneralizedUFunc`](generated/cupyx.GeneralizedUFunc.html#cupyx.GeneralizedUFunc "cupyx.GeneralizedUFunc")(func, signature, \*[, name, ...]) | Creates a Generalized Universal Function by wrapping a user provided function with the signature. |

* [Generalized Universal Functions](#generalized-universal-functions)

# Routines (NumPy)[#](#routines-numpy "Permalink to this heading")
The following pages describe NumPy-compatible routines.
These functions cover a subset of
[NumPy routines](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/routines.html).
* [Array creation routines](creation.html)

* [Array manipulation routines](manipulation.html)
  + [Basic operations](manipulation.html#basic-operations)
  + [Changing array shape](manipulation.html#changing-array-shape)
  + [Transpose-like operations](manipulation.html#transpose-like-operations)
  + [Changing number of dimensions](manipulation.html#changing-number-of-dimensions)
  + [Changing kind of array](manipulation.html#changing-kind-of-array)
  + [Joining arrays](manipulation.html#joining-arrays)
  + [Splitting arrays](manipulation.html#splitting-arrays)
  + [Tiling arrays](manipulation.html#tiling-arrays)
  + [Adding and removing elements](manipulation.html#adding-and-removing-elements)
  + [Rearranging elements](manipulation.html#rearranging-elements)
* [Bit-wise operations](binary.html)
  + [Elementwise bit operations](binary.html#elementwise-bit-operations)
  + [Bit packing](binary.html#bit-packing)
  + [Output formatting](binary.html#output-formatting)
* [Data type routines](dtype.html)
  + [cupy.can\_cast](generated/cupy.can_cast.html)

<!-- Source: https://docs.cupy.dev/en/stable/reference/creation.html -->
# Array creation routines[#](#array-creation-routines "Permalink to this heading")
Hint
[NumPy API Reference: Array creation routines](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/routines.array-creation.html)
## From shape or value[#](#from-shape-or-value "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`empty`](generated/cupy.empty.html#cupy.empty "cupy.empty")(shape[, dtype, order]) | Returns an array without initializing the elements. |
| [`empty_like`](generated/cupy.empty_like.html#cupy.empty_like "cupy.empty_like")(prototype[, dtype, order, subok, ...]) | Returns a new array with same shape and dtype of a given array. |
| [`eye`](generated/cupy.eye.html#cupy.eye "cupy.eye")(N[, M, k, dtype, order]) | Returns a 2-D array with ones on the diagonals and zeros elsewhere. |
| [`identity`](generated/cupy.identity.html#cupy.identity "cupy.identity")(n[, dtype]) | Returns a 2-D identity array. |
| [`ones`](generated/cupy.ones.html#cupy.ones "cupy.ones")(shape[, dtype, order]) | Returns a new array of given shape and dtype, filled with ones. |
| [`ones_like`](generated/cupy.ones_like.html#cupy.ones_like "cupy.ones_like")(a[, dtype, order, subok, shape]) | Returns an array of ones with same shape and dtype as a given array. |
| [`zeros`](generated/cupy.zeros.html#cupy.zeros "cupy.zeros")(shape[, dtype, order]) | Returns a new array of given shape and dtype, filled with zeros. |
| [`zeros_like`](generated/cupy.zeros_like.html#cupy.zeros_like "cupy.zeros_like")(a[, dtype, order, subok, shape]) | Returns an array of zeros with same shape and dtype as a given array. |
| [`full`](generated/cupy.full.html#cupy.full "cupy.full")(shape, fill\_value[, dtype, order]) | Returns a new array of given shape and dtype, filled with a given value. |
| [`full_like`](generated/cupy.full_like.html#cupy.full_like "cupy.full_like")(a, fill\_value[, dtype, order, ...]) | Returns a full array with same shape and dtype as a given array. |
## From existing data[#](#from-existing-data "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`array`](generated/cupy.array.html#cupy.array "cupy.array")(obj[, dtype, copy, order, subok, ...]) | Creates an array on the current device. |
| [`asarray`](generated/cupy.asarray.html#cupy.asarray "cupy.asarray")(a[, dtype, order, copy, blocking]) | Converts an object to array. |
| [`asanyarray`](generated/cupy.asanyarray.html#cupy.asanyarray "cupy.asanyarray")(a[, dtype, order, copy, blocking]) | Converts an object to array. |
| [`ascontiguousarray`](generated/cupy.ascontiguousarray.html#cupy.ascontiguousarray "cupy.ascontiguousarray")(a[, dtype]) | Returns a C-contiguous array. |
| [`astype`](generated/cupy.astype.html#cupy.astype "cupy.astype")(x, dtype, /, \*[, copy]) | Copies an array to a specified data type. |
| [`copy`](generated/cupy.copy.html#cupy.copy "cupy.copy")(a[, order]) | Creates a copy of a given array on the current device. |
| [`frombuffer`](generated/cupy.frombuffer.html#cupy.frombuffer "cupy.frombuffer")(\*args, \*\*kwargs) | Interpret a buffer as a 1-dimensional array. |
| [`from_dlpack`](generated/cupy.from_dlpack.html#cupy.from_dlpack "cupy.from_dlpack")(array, \*[, device, copy]) | Zero-copy conversion between array objects compliant with the DLPack data exchange protocol. |
| [`fromfile`](generated/cupy.fromfile.html#cupy.fromfile "cupy.fromfile")(\*args, \*\*kwargs) | Reads an array from a file. |
| [`fromfunction`](generated/cupy.fromfunction.html#cupy.fromfunction "cupy.fromfunction")(\*args, \*\*kwargs) | Construct an array by executing a function over each coordinate. |
| [`fromiter`](generated/cupy.fromiter.html#cupy.fromiter "cupy.fromiter")(\*args, \*\*kwargs) | Create a new 1-dimensional array from an iterable object. |
| [`fromstring`](generated/cupy.fromstring.html#cupy.fromstring "cupy.fromstring")(\*args, \*\*kwargs) | A new 1-D array initialized from text data in a string. |
| [`loadtxt`](generated/cupy.loadtxt.html#cupy.loadtxt "cupy.loadtxt")(\*args, \*\*kwargs) | Load data from a text file. |
## Numerical ranges[#](#numerical-ranges "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`arange`](generated/cupy.arange.html#cupy.arange "cupy.arange")(start[, stop, step, dtype]) | Returns an array with evenly spaced values within a given interval. |
| [`linspace`](generated/cupy.linspace.html#cupy.linspace "cupy.linspace")(start, stop[, num, endpoint, ...]) | Returns an array with evenly-spaced values within a given interval. |
| [`logspace`](generated/cupy.logspace.html#cupy.logspace "cupy.logspace")(start, stop[, num, endpoint, base, ...]) | Returns an array with evenly-spaced values on a log-scale. |
| [`meshgrid`](generated/cupy.meshgrid.html#cupy.meshgrid "cupy.meshgrid")(\*xi, \*\*kwargs) | Return coordinate matrices from coordinate vectors. |
| [`mgrid`](generated/cupy.mgrid.html#cupy.mgrid "cupy.mgrid") | Construct a multi-dimensional "meshgrid". |
| [`ogrid`](generated/cupy.ogrid.html#cupy.ogrid "cupy.ogrid") | Construct a multi-dimensional "meshgrid". |
## Building matrices[#](#building-matrices "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`diag`](generated/cupy.diag.html#cupy.diag "cupy.diag")(v[, k]) | Returns a diagonal or a diagonal array. |
| [`diagflat`](generated/cupy.diagflat.html#cupy.diagflat "cupy.diagflat")(v[, k]) | Creates a diagonal array from the flattened input. |
| [`tri`](generated/cupy.tri.html#cupy.tri "cupy.tri")(N[, M, k, dtype]) | Creates an array with ones at and below the given diagonal. |
| [`tril`](generated/cupy.tril.html#cupy.tril "cupy.tril")(m[, k]) | Returns a lower triangle of an array. |
| [`triu`](generated/cupy.triu.html#cupy.triu "cupy.triu")(m[, k]) | Returns an upper triangle of an array. |
| [`vander`](generated/cupy.vander.html#cupy.vander "cupy.vander")(x[, N, increasing]) | Returns a Vandermonde matrix. |
[previous
Routines (NumPy)](routines.html "previous page")
[next
cupy.empty](generated/cupy.empty.html "next page")

<!-- Source: https://docs.cupy.dev/en/stable/reference/manipulation.html -->
# Array manipulation routines[#](#array-manipulation-routines "Permalink to this heading")
Hint
[NumPy API Reference: Array manipulation routines](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/routines.array-manipulation.html)
## Basic operations[#](#basic-operations "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`copyto`](generated/cupy.copyto.html#cupy.copyto "cupy.copyto")(dst, src[, casting, where]) | Copies values from one array to another with broadcasting. |
| [`ndim`](generated/cupy.ndim.html#cupy.ndim "cupy.ndim")(a) | Returns the number of dimensions of an array. |
| [`shape`](generated/cupy.shape.html#cupy.shape "cupy.shape")(a) | Returns the shape of an array |
| [`size`](generated/cupy.size.html#cupy.size "cupy.size")(ndarray a[, axis]) | Returns the number of elements along a given axis. |
## Changing array shape[#](#changing-array-shape "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`reshape`](generated/cupy.reshape.html#cupy.reshape "cupy.reshape")(a, newshape[, order]) | Returns an array with new shape and same elements. |
| [`ravel`](generated/cupy.ravel.html#cupy.ravel "cupy.ravel")(a[, order]) | Returns a flattened array. |
See also
[`cupy.ndarray.flat`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray.flat "cupy.ndarray.flat") and [`cupy.ndarray.flatten()`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray.flatten "cupy.ndarray.flatten")
## Transpose-like operations[#](#transpose-like-operations "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`moveaxis`](generated/cupy.moveaxis.html#cupy.moveaxis "cupy.moveaxis")(a, source, destination) | Moves axes of an array to new positions. |
| [`rollaxis`](generated/cupy.rollaxis.html#cupy.rollaxis "cupy.rollaxis")(a, axis[, start]) | Moves the specified axis backwards to the given place. |
| [`swapaxes`](generated/cupy.swapaxes.html#cupy.swapaxes "cupy.swapaxes")(a, axis1, axis2) | Swaps the two axes. |
| [`transpose`](generated/cupy.transpose.html#cupy.transpose "cupy.transpose")(a[, axes]) | Permutes the dimensions of an array. |
| [`permute_dims`](generated/cupy.permute_dims.html#cupy.permute_dims "cupy.permute_dims")(a[, axes]) | Permutes the dimensions of an array. |
| [`matrix_transpose`](generated/cupy.matrix_transpose.html#cupy.matrix_transpose "cupy.matrix_transpose")(x, /) | Transposes a matrix (or a stack of matrices) `x`. |
See also
[`cupy.ndarray.T`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray.T "cupy.ndarray.T")
## Changing number of dimensions[#](#changing-number-of-dimensions "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`atleast_1d`](generated/cupy.atleast_1d.html#cupy.atleast_1d "cupy.atleast_1d")(\*arys) | Converts arrays to arrays with dimensions >= 1. |
| [`atleast_2d`](generated/cupy.atleast_2d.html#cupy.atleast_2d "cupy.atleast_2d")(\*arys) | Converts arrays to arrays with dimensions >= 2. |
| [`atleast_3d`](generated/cupy.atleast_3d.html#cupy.atleast_3d "cupy.atleast_3d")(\*arys) | Converts arrays to arrays with dimensions >= 3. |
| [`broadcast`](generated/cupy.broadcast.html#cupy.broadcast "cupy.broadcast")(\*arrays) | Object that performs broadcasting. |
| [`broadcast_to`](generated/cupy.broadcast_to.html#cupy.broadcast_to "cupy.broadcast_to")(array, shape) | Broadcast an array to a given shape. |
| [`broadcast_arrays`](generated/cupy.broadcast_arrays.html#cupy.broadcast_arrays "cupy.broadcast_arrays")(\*args) | Broadcasts given arrays. |
| [`expand_dims`](generated/cupy.expand_dims.html#cupy.expand_dims "cupy.expand_dims")(a, axis) | Expands given arrays. |
| [`squeeze`](generated/cupy.squeeze.html#cupy.squeeze "cupy.squeeze")(a[, axis]) | Removes size-one axes from the shape of an array. |
## Changing kind of array[#](#changing-kind-of-array "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`asarray`](generated/cupy.asarray.html#cupy.asarray "cupy.asarray")(a[, dtype, order, copy, blocking]) | Converts an object to array. |
| [`asanyarray`](generated/cupy.asanyarray.html#cupy.asanyarray "cupy.asanyarray")(a[, dtype, order, copy, blocking]) | Converts an object to array. |
| [`asfortranarray`](generated/cupy.asfortranarray.html#cupy.asfortranarray "cupy.asfortranarray")(a[, dtype]) | Return an array laid out in Fortran order in memory. |
| [`ascontiguousarray`](generated/cupy.ascontiguousarray.html#cupy.ascontiguousarray "cupy.ascontiguousarray")(a[, dtype]) | Returns a C-contiguous array. |
| [`asarray_chkfinite`](generated/cupy.asarray_chkfinite.html#cupy.asarray_chkfinite "cupy.asarray_chkfinite")(a[, dtype, order]) | Converts the given input to an array, and raises an error if the input contains NaNs or Infs. |
| [`require`](generated/cupy.require.html#cupy.require "cupy.require")(a[, dtype, requirements]) | Return an array which satisfies the requirements. |
## Joining arrays[#](#joining-arrays "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`concatenate`](generated/cupy.concatenate.html#cupy.concatenate "cupy.concatenate")(tup[, axis, out, dtype, casting]) | Joins arrays along an axis. |
| [`concat`](generated/cupy.concat.html#cupy.concat "cupy.concat")(tup[, axis, out, dtype, casting]) | Joins arrays along an axis. |
| [`stack`](generated/cupy.stack.html#cupy.stack "cupy.stack")(tup[, axis, out, dtype, casting]) | Stacks arrays along a new axis. |
| [`vstack`](generated/cupy.vstack.html#cupy.vstack "cupy.vstack")(tup, \*[, dtype, casting]) | Stacks arrays vertically. |
| [`hstack`](generated/cupy.hstack.html#cupy.hstack "cupy.hstack")(tup, \*[, dtype, casting]) | Stacks arrays horizontally. |
| [`dstack`](generated/cupy.dstack.html#cupy.dstack "cupy.dstack")(tup) | Stacks arrays along the third axis. |
| [`column_stack`](generated/cupy.column_stack.html#cupy.column_stack "cupy.column_stack")(tup) | Stacks 1-D and 2-D arrays as columns into a 2-D array. |
## Splitting arrays[#](#splitting-arrays "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`split`](generated/cupy.split.html#cupy.split "cupy.split")(ary, indices\_or\_sections[, axis]) | Splits an array into multiple sub arrays along a given axis. |
| [`array_split`](generated/cupy.array_split.html#cupy.array_split "cupy.array_split")(ary, indices\_or\_sections[, axis]) | Splits an array into multiple sub arrays along a given axis. |
| [`dsplit`](generated/cupy.dsplit.html#cupy.dsplit "cupy.dsplit")(ary, indices\_or\_sections) | Splits an array into multiple sub arrays along the third axis. |
| [`hsplit`](generated/cupy.hsplit.html#cupy.hsplit "cupy.hsplit")(ary, indices\_or\_sections) | Splits an array into multiple sub arrays horizontally. |
| [`vsplit`](generated/cupy.vsplit.html#cupy.vsplit "cupy.vsplit")(ary, indices\_or\_sections) | Splits an array into multiple sub arrays along the first axis. |
## Tiling arrays[#](#tiling-arrays "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`tile`](generated/cupy.tile.html#cupy.tile "cupy.tile")(A, reps) | Construct an array by repeating A the number of times given by reps. |
| [`repeat`](generated/cupy.repeat.html#cupy.repeat "cupy.repeat")(a, repeats[, axis]) | Repeat arrays along an axis. |
## Adding and removing elements[#](#adding-and-removing-elements "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`delete`](generated/cupy.delete.html#cupy.delete "cupy.delete")(arr, indices[, axis]) | Delete values from an array along the specified axis. |
| [`append`](generated/cupy.append.html#cupy.append "cupy.append")(arr, values[, axis]) | Append values to the end of an array. |
| [`resize`](generated/cupy.resize.html#cupy.resize "cupy.resize")(a, new\_shape) | Return a new array with the specified shape. |
| [`trim_zeros`](generated/cupy.trim_zeros.html#cupy.trim_zeros "cupy.trim_zeros")(filt[, trim]) | Trim the leading and/or trailing zeros from a 1-D array or sequence. |
| [`unique`](generated/cupy.unique.html#cupy.unique "cupy.unique")(ar[, return\_index, return\_inverse, ...]) | Find the unique elements of an array. |
| [`pad`](generated/cupy.pad.html#cupy.pad "cupy.pad")(array, pad\_width[, mode]) | Pads an array with specified widths and values. |
## Rearranging elements[#](#rearranging-elements "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`flip`](generated/cupy.flip.html#cupy.flip "cupy.flip")(a[, axis]) | Reverse the order of elements in an array along the given axis. |
| [`fliplr`](generated/cupy.fliplr.html#cupy.fliplr "cupy.fliplr")(a) | Flip array in the left/right direction. |
| [`flipud`](generated/cupy.flipud.html#cupy.flipud "cupy.flipud")(a) | Flip array in the up/down direction. |
| [`roll`](generated/cupy.roll.html#cupy.roll "cupy.roll")(a, shift[, axis]) | Roll array elements along a given axis. |
| [`rot90`](generated/cupy.rot90.html#cupy.rot90 "cupy.rot90")(a[, k, axes]) | Rotate an array by 90 degrees in the plane specified by axes. |

# Bit-wise operations[#](#bit-wise-operations "Permalink to this heading")
Hint
[NumPy API Reference: Bit-wise operations](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/routines.bitwise.html)
## Elementwise bit operations[#](#elementwise-bit-operations "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`bitwise_and`](generated/cupy.bitwise_and.html#cupy.bitwise_and "cupy.bitwise_and")(x1, x2, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Computes the bitwise AND of two arrays elementwise. |
| [`bitwise_or`](generated/cupy.bitwise_or.html#cupy.bitwise_or "cupy.bitwise_or")(x1, x2, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Computes the bitwise OR of two arrays elementwise. |
| [`bitwise_xor`](generated/cupy.bitwise_xor.html#cupy.bitwise_xor "cupy.bitwise_xor")(x1, x2, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Computes the bitwise XOR of two arrays elementwise. |
| [`invert`](generated/cupy.invert.html#cupy.invert "cupy.invert") | bitwise\_invert(x, /, out=None, \*, casting='same\_kind', dtype=None) |
| [`bitwise_invert`](generated/cupy.bitwise_invert.html#cupy.bitwise_invert "cupy.bitwise_invert")(x, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Computes the bitwise NOT of an array elementwise. |
| [`left_shift`](generated/cupy.left_shift.html#cupy.left_shift "cupy.left_shift") | bitwise\_left\_shift(x1, x2, /, out=None, \*, casting='same\_kind', dtype=None) |
| [`bitwise_left_shift`](generated/cupy.bitwise_left_shift.html#cupy.bitwise_left_shift "cupy.bitwise_left_shift")(x1, x2, /[, out, ...]) | Shifts the bits of each integer element to the left. |
| [`right_shift`](generated/cupy.right_shift.html#cupy.right_shift "cupy.right_shift") | bitwise\_right\_shift(x1, x2, /, out=None, \*, casting='same\_kind', dtype=None) |
| [`bitwise_right_shift`](generated/cupy.bitwise_right_shift.html#cupy.bitwise_right_shift "cupy.bitwise_right_shift")(x1, x2, /[, out, ...]) | Shifts the bits of each integer element to the right. |
| [`bitwise_count`](generated/cupy.bitwise_count.html#cupy.bitwise_count "cupy.bitwise_count")(x, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Computes the number of 1-bits in each element. |
## Bit packing[#](#bit-packing "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`packbits`](generated/cupy.packbits.html#cupy.packbits "cupy.packbits")(a[, axis, bitorder]) | Packs the elements of a binary-valued array into bits in a uint8 array. |
| [`unpackbits`](generated/cupy.unpackbits.html#cupy.unpackbits "cupy.unpackbits")(a[, axis, bitorder]) | Unpacks elements of a uint8 array into a binary-valued output array. |
## Output formatting[#](#output-formatting "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`binary_repr`](generated/cupy.binary_repr.html#cupy.binary_repr "cupy.binary_repr")(num[, width]) | Return the binary representation of the input number as a string. |

# Data type routines[#](#data-type-routines "Permalink to this heading")
Hint
[NumPy API Reference: Data type routines](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/routines.dtype.html)
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`can_cast`](generated/cupy.can_cast.html#cupy.can_cast "cupy.can_cast")(from\_, to[, casting]) | Returns True if cast between data types can occur according to the casting rule. |
| [`min_scalar_type`](generated/cupy.min_scalar_type.html#cupy.min_scalar_type "cupy.min_scalar_type")(a) | For scalar `a`, returns the data type with the smallest size and smallest scalar kind which can hold its value. |
| [`make_aligned_dtype`](generated/cupy.make_aligned_dtype.html#cupy.make_aligned_dtype "cupy.make_aligned_dtype")(dtype, \*, ...) | Create a new structured dtype from a NumPy dtype or dtype-like with sufficient alignment for GPU use. |
| [`result_type`](generated/cupy.result_type.html#cupy.result_type "cupy.result_type")(\*arrays\_and\_dtypes) | Returns the type that results from applying the NumPy type promotion rules to the arguments. |
| [`common_type`](generated/cupy.common_type.html#cupy.common_type "cupy.common_type")(\*arrays) | Return a scalar type which is common to the input arrays. |
|  |
| --- |
| `promote_types` (alias of [`numpy.promote_types()`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.promote_types.html#numpy.promote_types "(in NumPy v2.4)")) |

| `make_aligned_dtype` CuPy utility to create structured dtypes with sufficient alignment for GPU use. |
