<!-- Source: https://docs.cupy.dev/en/stable/reference/fft.html -->
# Discrete Fourier Transform ([`cupy.fft`](#module-cupy.fft "cupy.fft"))[#](#discrete-fourier-transform-cupy-fft "Permalink to this heading")
Hint
[NumPy API Reference: Discrete Fourier Transform (numpy.fft)](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/routines.fft.html)
See also
[Discrete Fourier transforms (cupyx.scipy.fft)](scipy_fft.html), [Fast Fourier Transform with CuPy](../user_guide/fft.html)
## Standard FFTs[#](#standard-ffts "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`fft`](generated/cupy.fft.fft.html#cupy.fft.fft "cupy.fft.fft")(a[, n, axis, norm]) | Compute the one-dimensional FFT. |
| [`ifft`](generated/cupy.fft.ifft.html#cupy.fft.ifft "cupy.fft.ifft")(a[, n, axis, norm]) | Compute the one-dimensional inverse FFT. |
| [`fft2`](generated/cupy.fft.fft2.html#cupy.fft.fft2 "cupy.fft.fft2")(a[, s, axes, norm]) | Compute the two-dimensional FFT. |
| [`ifft2`](generated/cupy.fft.ifft2.html#cupy.fft.ifft2 "cupy.fft.ifft2")(a[, s, axes, norm]) | Compute the two-dimensional inverse FFT. |
| [`fftn`](generated/cupy.fft.fftn.html#cupy.fft.fftn "cupy.fft.fftn")(a[, s, axes, norm]) | Compute the N-dimensional FFT. |
| [`ifftn`](generated/cupy.fft.ifftn.html#cupy.fft.ifftn "cupy.fft.ifftn")(a[, s, axes, norm]) | Compute the N-dimensional inverse FFT. |
## Real FFTs[#](#real-ffts "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`rfft`](generated/cupy.fft.rfft.html#cupy.fft.rfft "cupy.fft.rfft")(a[, n, axis, norm]) | Compute the one-dimensional FFT for real input. |
| [`irfft`](generated/cupy.fft.irfft.html#cupy.fft.irfft "cupy.fft.irfft")(a[, n, axis, norm]) | Compute the one-dimensional inverse FFT for real input. |
| [`rfft2`](generated/cupy.fft.rfft2.html#cupy.fft.rfft2 "cupy.fft.rfft2")(a[, s, axes, norm]) | Compute the two-dimensional FFT for real input. |
| [`irfft2`](generated/cupy.fft.irfft2.html#cupy.fft.irfft2 "cupy.fft.irfft2")(a[, s, axes, norm]) | Compute the two-dimensional inverse FFT for real input. |
| [`rfftn`](generated/cupy.fft.rfftn.html#cupy.fft.rfftn "cupy.fft.rfftn")(a[, s, axes, norm]) | Compute the N-dimensional FFT for real input. |
| [`irfftn`](generated/cupy.fft.irfftn.html#cupy.fft.irfftn "cupy.fft.irfftn")(a[, s, axes, norm]) | Compute the N-dimensional inverse FFT for real input. |
## Hermitian FFTs[#](#hermitian-ffts "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`hfft`](generated/cupy.fft.hfft.html#cupy.fft.hfft "cupy.fft.hfft")(a[, n, axis, norm]) | Compute the FFT of a signal that has Hermitian symmetry. |
| [`ihfft`](generated/cupy.fft.ihfft.html#cupy.fft.ihfft "cupy.fft.ihfft")(a[, n, axis, norm]) | Compute the FFT of a signal that has Hermitian symmetry. |
## Helper routines[#](#helper-routines "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`fftfreq`](generated/cupy.fft.fftfreq.html#cupy.fft.fftfreq "cupy.fft.fftfreq")(n[, d]) | Return the FFT sample frequencies. |
| [`rfftfreq`](generated/cupy.fft.rfftfreq.html#cupy.fft.rfftfreq "cupy.fft.rfftfreq")(n[, d]) | Return the FFT sample frequencies for real input. |
| [`fftshift`](generated/cupy.fft.fftshift.html#cupy.fft.fftshift "cupy.fft.fftshift")(x[, axes]) | Shift the zero-frequency component to the center of the spectrum. |
| [`ifftshift`](generated/cupy.fft.ifftshift.html#cupy.fft.ifftshift "cupy.fft.ifftshift")(x[, axes]) | The inverse of [`fftshift()`](generated/cupy.fft.fftshift.html#cupy.fft.fftshift "cupy.fft.fftshift"). |
## CuPy-specific APIs[#](#cupy-specific-apis "Permalink to this heading")
CuPy has some additional configuration options available:
* [`cupy.fft.config`](fft.config.html)
  + [`config.set_cufft_callbacks`](fft.config.html#cupy.fft.config.set_cufft_callbacks)
  + [`config.set_cufft_gpus()`](fft.config.html#cupy.fft.config.set_cufft_gpus)
  + [`config.get_plan_cache()`](fft.config.html#cupy.fft.config.get_plan_cache)
  + [`config.show_plan_cache_info()`](fft.config.html#cupy.fft.config.show_plan_cache_info)
## Normalization[#](#normalization "Permalink to this heading")
The default normalization (`norm` is `"backward"` or `None`) has the direct transforms unscaled and the inverse transforms scaled by \(1/n\).
If the keyword argument `norm` is `"forward"`, it is the exact opposite of `"backward"`:
the direct transforms are scaled by \(1/n\) and the inverse transforms are unscaled.
Finally, if the keyword argument `norm` is `"ortho"`, both transforms are scaled by \(1/\sqrt{n}\).
## Code compatibility features[#](#code-compatibility-features "Permalink to this heading")
FFT functions of NumPy always return numpy.ndarray which type is `numpy.complex128` or `numpy.float64`.
CuPy functions do not follow the behavior, they will return `numpy.complex64` or `numpy.float32` if the type of the input is `numpy.float16`, `numpy.float32`, or `numpy.complex64`.
Internally, `cupy.fft` always generates a *cuFFT plan* (see the [cuFFT documentation](https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cufft/index.html) for detail) corresponding to the desired transform. When possible, an n-dimensional plan will be used, as opposed to applying separate 1D plans for each axis to be transformed. Using n-dimensional planning can provide better performance for multidimensional transforms, but requires more GPU memory than separable 1D planning.
Moreover, the automatic plan generation can be suppressed by using an existing plan returned by [`cupyx.scipy.fftpack.get_fft_plan()`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fftpack.get_fft_plan.html#cupyx.scipy.fftpack.get_fft_plan "cupyx.scipy.fftpack.get_fft_plan") as a context manager. This is again a deviation from NumPy.
Finally, when using the high-level NumPy-like FFT APIs as listed above, internally the cuFFT plans are cached for possible reuse. The plan cache can be retrieved by `get_plan_cache()`, and its current status can be queried by [`show_plan_cache_info()`](fft.config.html#cupy.fft.config.show_plan_cache_info "cupy.fft.config.show_plan_cache_info"). For finer control of the plan cache, see [`PlanCache`](generated/cupy.fft._cache.PlanCache.html#cupy.fft._cache.PlanCache "cupy.fft._cache.PlanCache").
## Multi-GPU FFT[#](#multi-gpu-fft "Permalink to this heading")
[`cupy.fft`](#module-cupy.fft "cupy.fft") can use multiple GPUs. To enable (disable) this feature, set `cupy.fft::config.use_multi_gpus` to `True` (`False`). Next, to set the number of GPUs or the participating GPU IDs, use the function [`config.set_cufft_gpus()`](fft.config.html#cupy.fft.config.set_cufft_gpus "cupy.fft.config.set_cufft_gpus"). All of the limitations listed in the [cuFFT documentation](https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cufft/index.html) apply here. In particular, using more than one GPU does not guarantee better performance.

cupy.common\_type](generated/cupy.common_type.html "previous page")
[next
cupy.fft.fft](generated/cupy.fft.fft.html "next page")

# Linear algebra ([`cupy.linalg`](#module-cupy.linalg "cupy.linalg"))[#](#linear-algebra-cupy-linalg "Permalink to this heading")
Hint
[NumPy API Reference: Linear algebra (numpy.linalg)](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/routines.linalg.html)
See also
[Linear algebra (cupyx.scipy.linalg)](scipy_linalg.html)
## The `@` operator[#](#the-operator "Permalink to this heading")
The `@` operator is preferable to other methods when computing the matrix product between 2d arrays.
The [`cupy.matmul()`](generated/cupy.matmul.html#cupy.matmul "cupy.matmul") function implements the `@` operator.
## Matrix and vector products[#](#matrix-and-vector-products "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`dot`](generated/cupy.dot.html#cupy.dot "cupy.dot")(a, b[, out]) | Returns a dot product of two arrays. |
| [`vdot`](generated/cupy.vdot.html#cupy.vdot "cupy.vdot")(a, b) | Returns the dot product of two vectors. |
| [`inner`](generated/cupy.inner.html#cupy.inner "cupy.inner")(a, b) | Returns the inner product of two arrays. |
| [`outer`](generated/cupy.outer.html#cupy.outer "cupy.outer")(a, b[, out]) | Returns the outer product of two vectors. |
| [`matmul`](generated/cupy.matmul.html#cupy.matmul "cupy.matmul") | matmul(x1, x2, /, out=None, \*\*kwargs) |
| [`linalg.matmul`](generated/cupy.linalg.matmul.html#cupy.linalg.matmul "cupy.linalg.matmul") | matmul(x1, x2, /, out=None, \*\*kwargs) |
| [`tensordot`](generated/cupy.tensordot.html#cupy.tensordot "cupy.tensordot")(a, b[, axes]) | Returns the tensor dot product of two arrays along specified axes. |
| [`einsum`](generated/cupy.einsum.html#cupy.einsum "cupy.einsum")(subscripts, \*operands[, dtype, optimize]) | Evaluates the Einstein summation convention on the operands. |
| [`linalg.matrix_power`](generated/cupy.linalg.matrix_power.html#cupy.linalg.matrix_power "cupy.linalg.matrix_power")(M, n) | Raise a square matrix to the (integer) power n. |
| [`kron`](generated/cupy.kron.html#cupy.kron "cupy.kron")(a, b) | Returns the kronecker product of two arrays. |
| [`linalg.cross`](generated/cupy.linalg.cross.html#cupy.linalg.cross "cupy.linalg.cross")(x1, x2, /, \*[, axis]) | Returns the cross product of 3-element vectors. |
## Decompositions[#](#decompositions "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`linalg.cholesky`](generated/cupy.linalg.cholesky.html#cupy.linalg.cholesky "cupy.linalg.cholesky")(a) | Cholesky decomposition. |
| [`linalg.qr`](generated/cupy.linalg.qr.html#cupy.linalg.qr "cupy.linalg.qr")(a[, mode]) | QR decomposition. |
| [`linalg.svd`](generated/cupy.linalg.svd.html#cupy.linalg.svd "cupy.linalg.svd")(a[, full\_matrices, compute\_uv]) | Singular Value Decomposition. |

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`linalg.eig`](generated/cupy.linalg.eig.html#cupy.linalg.eig "cupy.linalg.eig")(a) | Return the eigenvalues and eigenvectors of a matrix. |
| [`linalg.eigh`](generated/cupy.linalg.eigh.html#cupy.linalg.eigh "cupy.linalg.eigh")(a[, UPLO]) | Return the eigenvalues and eigenvectors of a complex Hermitian (conjugate symmetric) or a real symmetric matrix. |
| [`linalg.eigvals`](generated/cupy.linalg.eigvals.html#cupy.linalg.eigvals "cupy.linalg.eigvals")(a) | Compute the eigenvalues of a matrix. |
| [`linalg.eigvalsh`](generated/cupy.linalg.eigvalsh.html#cupy.linalg.eigvalsh "cupy.linalg.eigvalsh")(a[, UPLO]) | Compute the eigenvalues of a complex Hermitian or real symmetric matrix. |

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`linalg.norm`](generated/cupy.linalg.norm.html#cupy.linalg.norm "cupy.linalg.norm")(x[, ord, axis, keepdims]) | Returns one of matrix norms specified by `ord` parameter. |
| [`linalg.cond`](generated/cupy.linalg.cond.html#cupy.linalg.cond "cupy.linalg.cond")(x[, p]) | Returns the condition number of a matrix. |
| [`linalg.det`](generated/cupy.linalg.det.html#cupy.linalg.det "cupy.linalg.det")(a) | Returns the determinant of an array. |
| [`linalg.matrix_rank`](generated/cupy.linalg.matrix_rank.html#cupy.linalg.matrix_rank "cupy.linalg.matrix_rank")(M[, tol]) | Return matrix rank of array using SVD method |
| [`linalg.slogdet`](generated/cupy.linalg.slogdet.html#cupy.linalg.slogdet "cupy.linalg.slogdet")(a) | Returns sign and logarithm of the determinant of an array. |
| [`trace`](generated/cupy.trace.html#cupy.trace "cupy.trace")(a[, offset, axis1, axis2, dtype, out]) | Returns the sum along the diagonals of an array. |

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`linalg.solve`](generated/cupy.linalg.solve.html#cupy.linalg.solve "cupy.linalg.solve")(a, b) | Solves a linear matrix equation. |

| [`linalg.lstsq`](generated/cupy.linalg.lstsq.html#cupy.linalg.lstsq "cupy.linalg.lstsq")(a, b[, rcond]) | Return the least-squares solution to a linear matrix equation. |
| [`linalg.inv`](generated/cupy.linalg.inv.html#cupy.linalg.inv "cupy.linalg.inv")(a) | Computes the inverse of a matrix. |
| [`linalg.pinv`](generated/cupy.linalg.pinv.html#cupy.linalg.pinv "cupy.linalg.pinv")(a[, rcond]) | Compute the Moore-Penrose pseudoinverse of a matrix. |
| [`linalg.tensorinv`](generated/cupy.linalg.tensorinv.html#cupy.linalg.tensorinv "cupy.linalg.tensorinv")(a[, ind]) | Computes the inverse of a tensor. |

| --- | --- |
| [`isfinite`](generated/cupy.isfinite.html#cupy.isfinite "cupy.isfinite")(x, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Tests finiteness elementwise. |
| [`isinf`](generated/cupy.isinf.html#cupy.isinf "cupy.isinf")(x, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Tests if each element is the positive or negative infinity. |
| [`isnan`](generated/cupy.isnan.html#cupy.isnan "cupy.isnan")(x, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Tests if each element is a NaN. |
| [`isneginf`](generated/cupy.isneginf.html#cupy.isneginf "cupy.isneginf")(x[, out]) | Test element-wise for negative infinity, return result as bool array. |
| [`isposinf`](generated/cupy.isposinf.html#cupy.isposinf "cupy.isposinf")(x[, out]) | Test element-wise for positive infinity, return result as bool array. |
## Array type testing[#](#array-type-testing "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`iscomplex`](generated/cupy.iscomplex.html#cupy.iscomplex "cupy.iscomplex")(x) | Returns a bool array, where True if input element is complex. |
| [`iscomplexobj`](generated/cupy.iscomplexobj.html#cupy.iscomplexobj "cupy.iscomplexobj")(x) | Check for a complex type or an array of complex numbers. |
| [`isfortran`](generated/cupy.isfortran.html#cupy.isfortran "cupy.isfortran")(a) | Returns True if the array is Fortran contiguous but *not* C contiguous. |
| [`isreal`](generated/cupy.isreal.html#cupy.isreal "cupy.isreal")(x) | Returns a bool array, where True if input element is real. |
| [`isrealobj`](generated/cupy.isrealobj.html#cupy.isrealobj "cupy.isrealobj")(x) | Return True if x is a not complex type or an array of complex numbers. |
| [`isscalar`](generated/cupy.isscalar.html#cupy.isscalar "cupy.isscalar")(element) | Returns True if the type of num is a scalar type. |
## Logic operations[#](#logic-operations "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`logical_and`](generated/cupy.logical_and.html#cupy.logical_and "cupy.logical_and")(x1, x2, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Computes the logical AND of two arrays. |
| [`logical_or`](generated/cupy.logical_or.html#cupy.logical_or "cupy.logical_or")(x1, x2, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Computes the logical OR of two arrays. |
| [`logical_not`](generated/cupy.logical_not.html#cupy.logical_not "cupy.logical_not")(x, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Computes the logical NOT of an array. |
| [`logical_xor`](generated/cupy.logical_xor.html#cupy.logical_xor "cupy.logical_xor")(x1, x2, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Computes the logical XOR of two arrays. |
## Comparison[#](#comparison "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`allclose`](generated/cupy.allclose.html#cupy.allclose "cupy.allclose")(a, b[, rtol, atol, equal\_nan]) | Returns True if two arrays are element-wise equal within a tolerance. |
| [`isclose`](generated/cupy.isclose.html#cupy.isclose "cupy.isclose")(a, b[, rtol, atol, equal\_nan]) | Returns a boolean array where two arrays are equal within a tolerance. |
| [`array_equal`](generated/cupy.array_equal.html#cupy.array_equal "cupy.array_equal")(a1, a2[, equal\_nan]) | Returns `True` if two arrays are element-wise exactly equal. |
| [`array_equiv`](generated/cupy.array_equiv.html#cupy.array_equiv "cupy.array_equiv")(a1, a2) | Returns `True` if all elements are equal or shape consistent, i.e., one input array can be broadcasted to create the same shape as the other. |
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`greater`](generated/cupy.greater.html#cupy.greater "cupy.greater")(x1, x2, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Tests elementwise if `x1 > x2`. |
| [`greater_equal`](generated/cupy.greater_equal.html#cupy.greater_equal "cupy.greater_equal")(x1, x2, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Tests elementwise if `x1 >= x2`. |
| [`less`](generated/cupy.less.html#cupy.less "cupy.less")(x1, x2, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Tests elementwise if `x1 < x2`. |
| [`less_equal`](generated/cupy.less_equal.html#cupy.less_equal "cupy.less_equal")(x1, x2, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Tests elementwise if `x1 <= x2`. |
| [`equal`](generated/cupy.equal.html#cupy.equal "cupy.equal")(x1, x2, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Tests elementwise if `x1 == x2`. |
| [`not_equal`](generated/cupy.not_equal.html#cupy.not_equal "cupy.not_equal")(x1, x2, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Tests elementwise if `x1 != x2`. |

| [`arctan2`](generated/cupy.arctan2.html#cupy.arctan2 "cupy.arctan2") | atan2(x1, x2, /, out=None, \*, casting='same\_kind', dtype=None) |

| [`degrees`](generated/cupy.degrees.html#cupy.degrees "cupy.degrees") | rad2deg(x, /, out=None, \*, casting='same\_kind', dtype=None) |

| [`rad2deg`](generated/cupy.rad2deg.html#cupy.rad2deg "cupy.rad2deg")(x, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Converts angles from radians to degrees elementwise. |

## Rounding[#](#rounding "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`round`](generated/cupy.round.html#cupy.round "cupy.round")(a[, decimals, out]) |  |
| [`around`](generated/cupy.around.html#cupy.around "cupy.around")(a[, decimals, out]) | Rounds to the given number of decimals. |
| [`rint`](generated/cupy.rint.html#cupy.rint "cupy.rint")(x, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Rounds each element of an array to the nearest integer. |
| [`fix`](generated/cupy.fix.html#cupy.fix "cupy.fix")(x, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | If given value x is positive, it return floor(x). |
| [`floor`](generated/cupy.floor.html#cupy.floor "cupy.floor")(x, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Rounds each element of an array to its floor integer. |
| [`ceil`](generated/cupy.ceil.html#cupy.ceil "cupy.ceil")(x, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Rounds each element of an array to its ceiling integer. |
| [`trunc`](generated/cupy.trunc.html#cupy.trunc "cupy.trunc")(x, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Rounds each element of an array towards zero. |
## Sums, products, differences[#](#sums-products-differences "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`prod`](generated/cupy.prod.html#cupy.prod "cupy.prod")(a[, axis, dtype, out, keepdims]) | Returns the product of an array along given axes. |
| [`sum`](generated/cupy.sum.html#cupy.sum "cupy.sum")(a[, axis, dtype, out, keepdims]) | Returns the sum of an array along given axes. |
| [`nanprod`](generated/cupy.nanprod.html#cupy.nanprod "cupy.nanprod")(a[, axis, dtype, out, keepdims]) | Returns the product of an array along given axes treating Not a Numbers (NaNs) as zero. |
| [`nansum`](generated/cupy.nansum.html#cupy.nansum "cupy.nansum")(a[, axis, dtype, out, keepdims]) | Returns the sum of an array along given axes treating Not a Numbers (NaNs) as zero. |
| [`cumprod`](generated/cupy.cumprod.html#cupy.cumprod "cupy.cumprod")(a[, axis, dtype, out]) | Returns the cumulative product of an array along a given axis. |
| [`cumsum`](generated/cupy.cumsum.html#cupy.cumsum "cupy.cumsum")(a[, axis, dtype, out]) | Returns the cumulative sum of an array along a given axis. |
| [`nancumprod`](generated/cupy.nancumprod.html#cupy.nancumprod "cupy.nancumprod")(a[, axis, dtype, out]) | Returns the cumulative product of an array along a given axis treating Not a Numbers (NaNs) as one. |
| [`nancumsum`](generated/cupy.nancumsum.html#cupy.nancumsum "cupy.nancumsum")(a[, axis, dtype, out]) | Returns the cumulative sum of an array along a given axis treating Not a Numbers (NaNs) as zero. |
| [`diff`](generated/cupy.diff.html#cupy.diff "cupy.diff")(a[, n, axis, prepend, append]) | Calculate the n-th discrete difference along the given axis. |
| [`ediff1d`](generated/cupy.ediff1d.html#cupy.ediff1d "cupy.ediff1d")(arr[, to\_end, to\_begin]) | Calculates the difference between consecutive elements of an array. |
| [`gradient`](generated/cupy.gradient.html#cupy.gradient "cupy.gradient")(f, \*varargs[, axis, edge\_order]) | Return the gradient of an N-dimensional array. |
| [`cross`](generated/cupy.cross.html#cupy.cross "cupy.cross")(a, b[, axisa, axisb, axisc, axis]) | Returns the cross product of two vectors. |
| [`trapezoid`](generated/cupy.trapezoid.html#cupy.trapezoid "cupy.trapezoid")(y[, x, dx, axis]) | Integrate along the given axis using the composite trapezoidal rule. |
## Exponents and logarithms[#](#exponents-and-logarithms "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`exp`](generated/cupy.exp.html#cupy.exp "cupy.exp")(x, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Elementwise exponential function. |
| [`expm1`](generated/cupy.expm1.html#cupy.expm1 "cupy.expm1")(x, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Computes `exp(x) - 1` elementwise. |

| [`log`](generated/cupy.log.html#cupy.log "cupy.log")(x, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Elementwise natural logarithm function. |
| [`log10`](generated/cupy.log10.html#cupy.log10 "cupy.log10")(x, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Elementwise common logarithm function. |
| [`log2`](generated/cupy.log2.html#cupy.log2 "cupy.log2")(x, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Elementwise binary logarithm function. |
| [`log1p`](generated/cupy.log1p.html#cupy.log1p "cupy.log1p")(x, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Computes `log(1 + x)` elementwise. |
| [`logaddexp`](generated/cupy.logaddexp.html#cupy.logaddexp "cupy.logaddexp")(x1, x2, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Computes `log(exp(x1) + exp(x2))` elementwise. |
| [`logaddexp2`](generated/cupy.logaddexp2.html#cupy.logaddexp2 "cupy.logaddexp2")(x1, x2, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Computes `log2(exp2(x1) + exp2(x2))` elementwise. |

| [`amax`](generated/cupy.amax.html#cupy.amax "cupy.amax")(a[, axis, out, keepdims]) | Returns the maximum of an array or the maximum along an axis. |

| [`nanmax`](generated/cupy.nanmax.html#cupy.nanmax "cupy.nanmax")(a[, axis, out, keepdims]) | Returns the maximum of an array along an axis ignoring NaN. |
| [`minimum`](generated/cupy.minimum.html#cupy.minimum "cupy.minimum")(x1, x2, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Takes the minimum of two arrays elementwise. |
| [`min`](generated/cupy.min.html#cupy.min "cupy.min")(a[, axis, out, keepdims]) | Returns the minimum of an array or the minimum along an axis. |
| [`amin`](generated/cupy.amin.html#cupy.amin "cupy.amin")(a[, axis, out, keepdims]) | Returns the minimum of an array or the minimum along an axis. |
| [`fmin`](generated/cupy.fmin.html#cupy.fmin "cupy.fmin")(x1, x2, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Takes the minimum of two arrays elementwise. |
| [`nanmin`](generated/cupy.nanmin.html#cupy.nanmin "cupy.nanmin")(a[, axis, out, keepdims]) | Returns the minimum of an array along an axis ignoring NaN. |

| [`cbrt`](generated/cupy.cbrt.html#cupy.cbrt "cupy.cbrt")(x, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Elementwise cube root function. |
| [`square`](generated/cupy.square.html#cupy.square "cupy.square")(x, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Elementwise square function. |
| [`absolute`](generated/cupy.absolute.html#cupy.absolute "cupy.absolute")(x, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Elementwise absolute value function. |
| [`fabs`](generated/cupy.fabs.html#cupy.fabs "cupy.fabs")(x, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Calculates absolute values element-wise. |
| [`sign`](generated/cupy.sign.html#cupy.sign "cupy.sign")(x, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Elementwise sign function. |
| [`heaviside`](generated/cupy.heaviside.html#cupy.heaviside "cupy.heaviside")(x1, x2, /[, out, casting, dtype]) | Compute the Heaviside step function. |
| [`nan_to_num`](generated/cupy.nan_to_num.html#cupy.nan_to_num "cupy.nan_to_num")(x[, copy, nan, posinf, neginf]) | Replace NaN with zero and infinity with large finite numbers (default behaviour) or with the numbers defined by the user using the nan, posinf and/or neginf keywords. |
| [`real_if_close`](generated/cupy.real_if_close.html#cupy.real_if_close "cupy.real_if_close")(a[, tol]) | If input is complex with all imaginary parts close to zero, return real parts. |
| [`interp`](generated/cupy.interp.html#cupy.interp "cupy.interp")(x, xp, fp[, left, right, period]) | One-dimensional linear interpolation. |

<!-- Source: https://docs.cupy.dev/en/stable/reference/random.html -->
# Random sampling ([`cupy.random`](#module-cupy.random "cupy.random"))[#](#random-sampling-cupy-random "Permalink to this heading")
Differences between [`cupy.random`](#module-cupy.random "cupy.random") and [`numpy.random`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/random/index.html#module-numpy.random "(in NumPy v2.4)"):
* Most functions under [`cupy.random`](#module-cupy.random "cupy.random") support the `dtype` option, which do not exist in the corresponding NumPy APIs.
  This option enables generation of float32 values directly without any space overhead.
* [`cupy.random.default_rng()`](generated/cupy.random.default_rng.html#cupy.random.default_rng "cupy.random.default_rng") uses XORWOW bit generator by default.
* Random states cannot be serialized. See the description below for details.
* CuPy does not guarantee that the same number generator is used across major versions.
  This means that numbers generated by [`cupy.random`](#module-cupy.random "cupy.random") by new major version may not be the same as the previous one, even if the same seed and distribution are used.
## New Random Generator API[#](#new-random-generator-api "Permalink to this heading")
Hint
[NumPy API Reference: Random sampling (numpy.random)](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/random/)
### Random Generator[#](#random-generator "Permalink to this heading")
Hint
[NumPy API Reference: Random Generator](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/random/generator.html)
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`default_rng`](generated/cupy.random.default_rng.html#cupy.random.default_rng "cupy.random.default_rng")([seed]) | Construct a new Generator with the default BitGenerator (XORWOW). |
| [`Generator`](generated/cupy.random.Generator.html#cupy.random.Generator "cupy.random.Generator")(bit\_generator) | Container for the BitGenerators. |
### Bit Generators[#](#bit-generators "Permalink to this heading")
Hint
[NumPy API Reference: Bit Generators](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/random/bit_generators/index.html)
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`BitGenerator`](generated/cupy.random.BitGenerator.html#cupy.random.BitGenerator "cupy.random.BitGenerator")([seed]) | Generic BitGenerator. |
CuPy provides the following bit generator implementations:
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`XORWOW`](generated/cupy.random.XORWOW.html#cupy.random.XORWOW "cupy.random.XORWOW")([seed, size]) | BitGenerator that uses cuRAND XORWOW device generator. |
| [`MRG32k3a`](generated/cupy.random.MRG32k3a.html#cupy.random.MRG32k3a "cupy.random.MRG32k3a")([seed, size]) | BitGenerator that uses cuRAND MRG32k3a device generator. |
| [`Philox4x3210`](generated/cupy.random.Philox4x3210.html#cupy.random.Philox4x3210 "cupy.random.Philox4x3210")([seed, size]) | BitGenerator that uses cuRAND Philox4x3210 device generator. |
## Legacy Random Generation[#](#legacy-random-generation "Permalink to this heading")
Hint
* [NumPy API Reference: Legacy Random Generation](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/random/legacy.html)
* [NumPy 1.16 Reference](https://numpy.org/doc/1.16/reference/routines.random.html)
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`RandomState`](generated/cupy.random.RandomState.html#cupy.random.RandomState "cupy.random.RandomState")([seed, method]) | Portable container of a pseudo-random number generator. |

| [`standard_normal`](generated/cupy.random.standard_normal.html#cupy.random.standard_normal "cupy.random.standard_normal")([size, dtype]) | Returns an array of samples drawn from the standard normal distribution. |
| [`standard_t`](generated/cupy.random.standard_t.html#cupy.random.standard_t "cupy.random.standard_t")(df[, size, dtype]) | Standard Student's t distribution. |
| [`triangular`](generated/cupy.random.triangular.html#cupy.random.triangular "cupy.random.triangular")(left, mode, right[, size, dtype]) | Triangular distribution. |
| [`uniform`](generated/cupy.random.uniform.html#cupy.random.uniform "cupy.random.uniform")([low, high, size, dtype]) | Returns an array of uniformly-distributed samples over an interval. |
| [`vonmises`](generated/cupy.random.vonmises.html#cupy.random.vonmises "cupy.random.vonmises")(mu, kappa[, size, dtype]) | von Mises distribution. |
| [`wald`](generated/cupy.random.wald.html#cupy.random.wald "cupy.random.wald")(mean, scale[, size, dtype]) | Wald distribution. |
| [`weibull`](generated/cupy.random.weibull.html#cupy.random.weibull "cupy.random.weibull")(a[, size, dtype]) | weibull distribution. |
| [`zipf`](generated/cupy.random.zipf.html#cupy.random.zipf "cupy.random.zipf")(a[, size, dtype]) | Zipf distribution. |
CuPy does not provide `cupy.random.get_state` nor `cupy.random.set_state` at this time.
Use the following CuPy-specific APIs instead.
Note that these functions use [`cupy.random.RandomState`](generated/cupy.random.RandomState.html#cupy.random.RandomState "cupy.random.RandomState") instance to represent the internal state, which cannot be serialized.
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`get_random_state`](generated/cupy.random.get_random_state.html#cupy.random.get_random_state "cupy.random.get_random_state")() | Gets the state of the random number generator for the current device. |
| [`set_random_state`](generated/cupy.random.set_random_state.html#cupy.random.set_random_state "cupy.random.set_random_state")(rs) | Sets the state of the random number generator for the current device. |

Hint
[NumPy API Reference: Set routines](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/routines.set.html)
## Making proper sets[#](#making-proper-sets "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`unique`](generated/cupy.unique.html#cupy.unique "cupy.unique")(ar[, return\_index, return\_inverse, ...]) | Find the unique elements of an array. |
| [`unique_all`](generated/cupy.unique_all.html#cupy.unique_all "cupy.unique_all")(x) | Find the unique elements of an array, and counts, inverse and indices. |
| [`unique_counts`](generated/cupy.unique_counts.html#cupy.unique_counts "cupy.unique_counts")(x) | Find the unique elements and counts of an input array x. |
| [`unique_inverse`](generated/cupy.unique_inverse.html#cupy.unique_inverse "cupy.unique_inverse")(x) | Find the unique elements of x and indices to reconstruct x. |
| [`unique_values`](generated/cupy.unique_values.html#cupy.unique_values "cupy.unique_values")(x) | Returns the unique elements of an input array x. |
## Boolean operations[#](#boolean-operations "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`in1d`](generated/cupy.in1d.html#cupy.in1d "cupy.in1d")(ar1, ar2[, assume\_unique, invert]) | Tests whether each element of a 1-D array is also present in a second array. |
| [`intersect1d`](generated/cupy.intersect1d.html#cupy.intersect1d "cupy.intersect1d")(arr1, arr2[, assume\_unique, ...]) | Find the intersection of two arrays. |
| [`isin`](generated/cupy.isin.html#cupy.isin "cupy.isin")(element, test\_elements[, ...]) | Calculates element in `test_elements`, broadcasting over `element` only. |
| [`setdiff1d`](generated/cupy.setdiff1d.html#cupy.setdiff1d "cupy.setdiff1d")(ar1, ar2[, assume\_unique]) | Find the set difference of two arrays. |
| [`setxor1d`](generated/cupy.setxor1d.html#cupy.setxor1d "cupy.setxor1d")(ar1, ar2[, assume\_unique]) | Find the set exclusive-or of two arrays. |
| [`union1d`](generated/cupy.union1d.html#cupy.union1d "cupy.union1d")(arr1, arr2) | Find the union of two arrays. |
[previous
cupy.random.set\_random\_state](generated/cupy.random.set_random_state.html "previous page")
[next
cupy.unique\_all](generated/cupy.unique_all.html "next page")
On this page
* [Making proper sets](#making-proper-sets)
* [Boolean operations](#boolean-operations)

<!-- Source: https://docs.cupy.dev/en/stable/reference/sorting.html -->
# Sorting, searching, and counting[#](#sorting-searching-and-counting "Permalink to this heading")
Hint
[NumPy API Reference: Sorting, searching, and counting](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/routines.sort.html)
## Sorting[#](#sorting "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`sort`](generated/cupy.sort.html#cupy.sort "cupy.sort")(a[, axis, kind]) | Returns a sorted copy of an array with a stable sorting algorithm. |
| [`lexsort`](generated/cupy.lexsort.html#cupy.lexsort "cupy.lexsort")(keys) | Perform an indirect sort using an array of keys. |
| [`argsort`](generated/cupy.argsort.html#cupy.argsort "cupy.argsort")(a[, axis, kind]) | Returns the indices that would sort an array with a stable sorting. |
| [`msort`](generated/cupy.msort.html#cupy.msort "cupy.msort")(a) | Returns a copy of an array sorted along the first axis. |
| [`sort_complex`](generated/cupy.sort_complex.html#cupy.sort_complex "cupy.sort_complex")(a) | Sort a complex array using the real part first, then the imaginary part. |
| [`partition`](generated/cupy.partition.html#cupy.partition "cupy.partition")(a, kth[, axis]) | Returns a partitioned copy of an array. |
| [`argpartition`](generated/cupy.argpartition.html#cupy.argpartition "cupy.argpartition")(a, kth[, axis]) | Returns the indices that would partially sort an array. |
See also
[`cupy.ndarray.sort()`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray.sort "cupy.ndarray.sort")
## Searching[#](#searching "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`argmax`](generated/cupy.argmax.html#cupy.argmax "cupy.argmax")(a[, axis, dtype, out, keepdims]) | Returns the indices of the maximum along an axis. |
| [`nanargmax`](generated/cupy.nanargmax.html#cupy.nanargmax "cupy.nanargmax")(a[, axis, dtype, out, keepdims]) | Return the indices of the maximum values in the specified axis ignoring NaNs. |
| [`argmin`](generated/cupy.argmin.html#cupy.argmin "cupy.argmin")(a[, axis, dtype, out, keepdims]) | Returns the indices of the minimum along an axis. |
| [`nanargmin`](generated/cupy.nanargmin.html#cupy.nanargmin "cupy.nanargmin")(a[, axis, dtype, out, keepdims]) | Return the indices of the minimum values in the specified axis ignoring NaNs. |
| [`argwhere`](generated/cupy.argwhere.html#cupy.argwhere "cupy.argwhere")(a) | Return the indices of the elements that are non-zero. |
| [`nonzero`](generated/cupy.nonzero.html#cupy.nonzero "cupy.nonzero")(a) | Return the indices of the elements that are non-zero. |
| [`flatnonzero`](generated/cupy.flatnonzero.html#cupy.flatnonzero "cupy.flatnonzero")(a) | Return indices that are non-zero in the flattened version of a. |
| [`where`](generated/cupy.where.html#cupy.where "cupy.where")(condition[, x, y]) | Return elements, either from x or y, depending on condition. |
| [`searchsorted`](generated/cupy.searchsorted.html#cupy.searchsorted "cupy.searchsorted")(a, v[, side, sorter]) | Finds indices where elements should be inserted to maintain order. |
| [`extract`](generated/cupy.extract.html#cupy.extract "cupy.extract")(condition, a) | Return the elements of an array that satisfy some condition. |
## Counting[#](#counting "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`count_nonzero`](generated/cupy.count_nonzero.html#cupy.count_nonzero "cupy.count_nonzero")(a[, axis]) | Counts the number of non-zero values in the array. |
[previous
cupy.union1d](generated/cupy.union1d.html "previous page")
[next
cupy.sort](generated/cupy.sort.html "next page")
On this page
* [Sorting](#sorting)
* [Searching](#searching)
* [Counting](#counting)

<!-- Source: https://docs.cupy.dev/en/stable/reference/statistics.html -->
# Statistics[#](#statistics "Permalink to this heading")
Hint
[NumPy API Reference: Statistics](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/routines.statistics.html)
## Order statistics[#](#order-statistics "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`ptp`](generated/cupy.ptp.html#cupy.ptp "cupy.ptp")(a[, axis, out, keepdims]) | Returns the range of values (maximum - minimum) along an axis. |
| [`percentile`](generated/cupy.percentile.html#cupy.percentile "cupy.percentile")(a, q[, axis, out, ...]) | Computes the q-th percentile of the data along the specified axis. |
| [`quantile`](generated/cupy.quantile.html#cupy.quantile "cupy.quantile")(a, q[, axis, out, overwrite\_input, ...]) | Computes the q-th quantile of the data along the specified axis. |
## Averages and variances[#](#averages-and-variances "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`median`](generated/cupy.median.html#cupy.median "cupy.median")(a[, axis, out, overwrite\_input, keepdims]) | Compute the median along the specified axis. |
| [`average`](generated/cupy.average.html#cupy.average "cupy.average")(a[, axis, weights, returned, keepdims]) | Returns the weighted average along an axis. |
| [`mean`](generated/cupy.mean.html#cupy.mean "cupy.mean")(a[, axis, dtype, out, keepdims]) | Returns the arithmetic mean along an axis. |
| [`std`](generated/cupy.std.html#cupy.std "cupy.std")(a[, axis, dtype, out, ddof, keepdims]) | Returns the standard deviation along an axis. |
| [`var`](generated/cupy.var.html#cupy.var "cupy.var")(a[, axis, dtype, out, ddof, keepdims]) | Returns the variance along an axis. |
| [`nanmedian`](generated/cupy.nanmedian.html#cupy.nanmedian "cupy.nanmedian")(a[, axis, out, overwrite\_input, ...]) | Compute the median along the specified axis, while ignoring NaNs. |
| [`nanmean`](generated/cupy.nanmean.html#cupy.nanmean "cupy.nanmean")(a[, axis, dtype, out, keepdims]) | Returns the arithmetic mean along an axis ignoring NaN values. |
| [`nanstd`](generated/cupy.nanstd.html#cupy.nanstd "cupy.nanstd")(a[, axis, dtype, out, ddof, keepdims]) | Returns the standard deviation along an axis ignoring NaN values. |
| [`nanvar`](generated/cupy.nanvar.html#cupy.nanvar "cupy.nanvar")(a[, axis, dtype, out, ddof, keepdims]) | Returns the variance along an axis ignoring NaN values. |
## Correlating[#](#correlating "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`corrcoef`](generated/cupy.corrcoef.html#cupy.corrcoef "cupy.corrcoef")(a[, y, rowvar, bias, ddof, dtype]) | Returns the Pearson product-moment correlation coefficients of an array. |
| [`correlate`](generated/cupy.correlate.html#cupy.correlate "cupy.correlate")(a, v[, mode]) | Returns the cross-correlation of two 1-dimensional sequences. |
| [`cov`](generated/cupy.cov.html#cupy.cov "cupy.cov")(a[, y, rowvar, bias, ddof, fweights, ...]) | Returns the covariance matrix of an array. |
## Histograms[#](#histograms "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`histogram`](generated/cupy.histogram.html#cupy.histogram "cupy.histogram")(x[, bins, range, density, weights]) | Computes the histogram of a set of data. |
| [`histogram2d`](generated/cupy.histogram2d.html#cupy.histogram2d "cupy.histogram2d")(x, y[, bins, range, weights, ...]) | Compute the bi-dimensional histogram of two data samples. |
| [`histogramdd`](generated/cupy.histogramdd.html#cupy.histogramdd "cupy.histogramdd")(sample[, bins, range, weights, ...]) | Compute the multidimensional histogram of some data. |
| [`bincount`](generated/cupy.bincount.html#cupy.bincount "cupy.bincount")(x[, weights, minlength]) | Count number of occurrences of each value in array of non-negative ints. |
| [`digitize`](generated/cupy.digitize.html#cupy.digitize "cupy.digitize")(x, bins[, right]) | Finds the indices of the bins to which each value in input array belongs. |

[next

<!-- Source: https://docs.cupy.dev/en/stable/reference/testing.html -->
# Test support ([`cupy.testing`](#module-cupy.testing "cupy.testing"))[#](#test-support-cupy-testing "Permalink to this heading")
Hint
[NumPy API Reference: Test support (numpy.testing)](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/routines.testing.html)
## Asserts[#](#asserts "Permalink to this heading")
Hint
These APIs can accept both [`numpy.ndarray`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)") and [`cupy.ndarray`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray").
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`assert_array_almost_equal`](generated/cupy.testing.assert_array_almost_equal.html#cupy.testing.assert_array_almost_equal "cupy.testing.assert_array_almost_equal")(actual, desired[, ...]) | Raises an AssertionError if objects are not equal up to desired precision. |
| [`assert_allclose`](generated/cupy.testing.assert_allclose.html#cupy.testing.assert_allclose "cupy.testing.assert_allclose")(actual, desired[, rtol, ...]) | Raises an AssertionError if objects are not equal up to desired tolerance. |
| [`assert_array_almost_equal_nulp`](generated/cupy.testing.assert_array_almost_equal_nulp.html#cupy.testing.assert_array_almost_equal_nulp "cupy.testing.assert_array_almost_equal_nulp")(x, y[, nulp]) | Compare two arrays relatively to their spacing. |
| [`assert_array_max_ulp`](generated/cupy.testing.assert_array_max_ulp.html#cupy.testing.assert_array_max_ulp "cupy.testing.assert_array_max_ulp")(a, b[, maxulp, dtype]) | Check that all items of arrays differ in at most N Units in the Last Place. |
| [`assert_array_equal`](generated/cupy.testing.assert_array_equal.html#cupy.testing.assert_array_equal "cupy.testing.assert_array_equal")(actual, desired[, ...]) | Raises an AssertionError if two array\_like objects are not equal. |
| [`assert_array_less`](generated/cupy.testing.assert_array_less.html#cupy.testing.assert_array_less "cupy.testing.assert_array_less")(x, y[, err\_msg, verbose, ...]) | Raises an AssertionError if array\_like objects are not ordered by less than. |
## CuPy-specific APIs[#](#cupy-specific-apis "Permalink to this heading")
### Asserts[#](#id1 "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`assert_array_list_equal`](generated/cupy.testing.assert_array_list_equal.html#cupy.testing.assert_array_list_equal "cupy.testing.assert_array_list_equal")(xlist, ylist[, ...]) | Compares lists of arrays pairwise with `assert_array_equal`. |
### NumPy-CuPy Consistency Check[#](#numpy-cupy-consistency-check "Permalink to this heading")
The following decorators are for testing consistency
between CuPy’s functions and corresponding NumPy’s ones.
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`numpy_cupy_allclose`](generated/cupy.testing.numpy_cupy_allclose.html#cupy.testing.numpy_cupy_allclose "cupy.testing.numpy_cupy_allclose")([rtol, atol, err\_msg, ...]) | Decorator that checks NumPy results and CuPy ones are close. |
| [`numpy_cupy_array_almost_equal`](generated/cupy.testing.numpy_cupy_array_almost_equal.html#cupy.testing.numpy_cupy_array_almost_equal "cupy.testing.numpy_cupy_array_almost_equal")([decimal, ...]) | Decorator that checks NumPy results and CuPy ones are almost equal. |
| [`numpy_cupy_array_almost_equal_nulp`](generated/cupy.testing.numpy_cupy_array_almost_equal_nulp.html#cupy.testing.numpy_cupy_array_almost_equal_nulp "cupy.testing.numpy_cupy_array_almost_equal_nulp")([nulp, ...]) | Decorator that checks results of NumPy and CuPy are equal w.r.t. |
| [`numpy_cupy_array_max_ulp`](generated/cupy.testing.numpy_cupy_array_max_ulp.html#cupy.testing.numpy_cupy_array_max_ulp "cupy.testing.numpy_cupy_array_max_ulp")([maxulp, dtype, ...]) | Decorator that checks results of NumPy and CuPy ones are equal w.r.t. |
| [`numpy_cupy_array_equal`](generated/cupy.testing.numpy_cupy_array_equal.html#cupy.testing.numpy_cupy_array_equal "cupy.testing.numpy_cupy_array_equal")([err\_msg, verbose, ...]) | Decorator that checks NumPy results and CuPy ones are equal. |
| [`numpy_cupy_array_list_equal`](generated/cupy.testing.numpy_cupy_array_list_equal.html#cupy.testing.numpy_cupy_array_list_equal "cupy.testing.numpy_cupy_array_list_equal")([err\_msg, ...]) | Decorator that checks the resulting lists of NumPy and CuPy's one are equal. |
| [`numpy_cupy_array_less`](generated/cupy.testing.numpy_cupy_array_less.html#cupy.testing.numpy_cupy_array_less "cupy.testing.numpy_cupy_array_less")([err\_msg, verbose, ...]) | Decorator that checks the CuPy result is less than NumPy result. |
### Parameterized dtype Test[#](#parameterized-dtype-test "Permalink to this heading")
The following decorators offer the standard way for
parameterized test with respect to single or the
combination of dtype(s).
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`for_dtypes`](generated/cupy.testing.for_dtypes.html#cupy.testing.for_dtypes "cupy.testing.for_dtypes")(dtypes[, name]) | Decorator for parameterized dtype test. |
| [`for_all_dtypes`](generated/cupy.testing.for_all_dtypes.html#cupy.testing.for_all_dtypes "cupy.testing.for_all_dtypes")([name, no\_float16, no\_bool, ...]) | Decorator that checks the fixture with all dtypes. |
| [`for_float_dtypes`](generated/cupy.testing.for_float_dtypes.html#cupy.testing.for_float_dtypes "cupy.testing.for_float_dtypes")([name, no\_float16]) | Decorator that checks the fixture with float dtypes. |
| [`for_signed_dtypes`](generated/cupy.testing.for_signed_dtypes.html#cupy.testing.for_signed_dtypes "cupy.testing.for_signed_dtypes")([name]) | Decorator that checks the fixture with signed dtypes. |
| [`for_unsigned_dtypes`](generated/cupy.testing.for_unsigned_dtypes.html#cupy.testing.for_unsigned_dtypes "cupy.testing.for_unsigned_dtypes")([name]) | Decorator that checks the fixture with unsigned dtypes. |
| [`for_int_dtypes`](generated/cupy.testing.for_int_dtypes.html#cupy.testing.for_int_dtypes "cupy.testing.for_int_dtypes")([name, no\_bool]) | Decorator that checks the fixture with integer and optionally bool dtypes. |
| [`for_complex_dtypes`](generated/cupy.testing.for_complex_dtypes.html#cupy.testing.for_complex_dtypes "cupy.testing.for_complex_dtypes")([name]) | Decorator that checks the fixture with complex dtypes. |
| [`for_dtypes_combination`](generated/cupy.testing.for_dtypes_combination.html#cupy.testing.for_dtypes_combination "cupy.testing.for_dtypes_combination")(types[, names, full]) | Decorator that checks the fixture with a product set of dtypes. |
| [`for_all_dtypes_combination`](generated/cupy.testing.for_all_dtypes_combination.html#cupy.testing.for_all_dtypes_combination "cupy.testing.for_all_dtypes_combination")([names, ...]) | Decorator that checks the fixture with a product set of all dtypes. |
| [`for_signed_dtypes_combination`](generated/cupy.testing.for_signed_dtypes_combination.html#cupy.testing.for_signed_dtypes_combination "cupy.testing.for_signed_dtypes_combination")([names, full]) | Decorator for parameterized test w.r.t. |
| [`for_unsigned_dtypes_combination`](generated/cupy.testing.for_unsigned_dtypes_combination.html#cupy.testing.for_unsigned_dtypes_combination "cupy.testing.for_unsigned_dtypes_combination")([names, full]) | Decorator for parameterized test w.r.t. |
| [`for_int_dtypes_combination`](generated/cupy.testing.for_int_dtypes_combination.html#cupy.testing.for_int_dtypes_combination "cupy.testing.for_int_dtypes_combination")([names, no\_bool, ...]) | Decorator for parameterized test w.r.t. |
### Parameterized order Test[#](#parameterized-order-test "Permalink to this heading")
The following decorators offer the standard way to parameterize tests with
orders.
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`for_orders`](generated/cupy.testing.for_orders.html#cupy.testing.for_orders "cupy.testing.for_orders")(orders[, name]) | Decorator to parameterize tests with order. |
| [`for_CF_orders`](generated/cupy.testing.for_CF_orders.html#cupy.testing.for_CF_orders "cupy.testing.for_CF_orders")([name]) | Decorator that checks the fixture with orders 'C' and 'F'. |
[previous
cupy.digitize](generated/cupy.digitize.html "previous page")
[next
cupy.testing.assert\_array\_almost\_equal](generated/cupy.testing.assert_array_almost_equal.html "next page")
On this page

* [CuPy-specific APIs](#cupy-specific-apis)
  + [Asserts](#id1)
  + [NumPy-CuPy Consistency Check](#numpy-cupy-consistency-check)
  + [Parameterized dtype Test](#parameterized-dtype-test)

# Window functions[#](#window-functions "Permalink to this heading")
Hint

## Various windows[#](#various-windows "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`bartlett`](generated/cupy.bartlett.html#cupy.bartlett "cupy.bartlett")(M) | Returns the Bartlett window. |

| [`hamming`](generated/cupy.hamming.html#cupy.hamming "cupy.hamming")(M) | Returns the Hamming window. |
| [`hanning`](generated/cupy.hanning.html#cupy.hanning "cupy.hanning")(M) | Returns the Hanning window. |
| [`kaiser`](generated/cupy.kaiser.html#cupy.kaiser "cupy.kaiser")(M, beta) | Return the Kaiser window. |

<!-- Source: https://docs.cupy.dev/en/stable/reference/scipy.html -->
# Routines (SciPy)[#](#routines-scipy "Permalink to this heading")
The following pages describe SciPy-compatible routines.
These functions cover a subset of
[SciPy routines](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/#api-reference).
* [Discrete Fourier transforms (`cupyx.scipy.fft`)](scipy_fft.html)
  + [Fast Fourier Transforms (FFTs)](scipy_fft.html#fast-fourier-transforms-ffts)
  + [Discrete Cosine and Sine Transforms (DST and DCT)](scipy_fft.html#discrete-cosine-and-sine-transforms-dst-and-dct)
  + [Fast Hankel Transforms](scipy_fft.html#fast-hankel-transforms)
  + [Helper functions](scipy_fft.html#helper-functions)
  + [Code compatibility features](scipy_fft.html#code-compatibility-features)
* [Legacy discrete fourier transforms (`cupyx.scipy.fftpack`)](scipy_fftpack.html)
  + [Fast Fourier Transforms (FFTs)](scipy_fftpack.html#fast-fourier-transforms-ffts)
  + [Code compatibility features](scipy_fftpack.html#code-compatibility-features)
* [Interpolation (`cupyx.scipy.interpolate`)](scipy_interpolate.html)
  + [Univariate interpolation](scipy_interpolate.html#univariate-interpolation)
  + [1-D Splines](scipy_interpolate.html#d-splines)
  + [Smoothing Splines](scipy_interpolate.html#smoothing-splines)
  + [Multivariate interpolation](scipy_interpolate.html#multivariate-interpolation)
* [Linear algebra (`cupyx.scipy.linalg`)](scipy_linalg.html)
  + [Basics](scipy_linalg.html#basics)
  + [Matrix Functions](scipy_linalg.html#matrix-functions)
  + [Decompositions](scipy_linalg.html#decompositions)
  + [Special Matrices](scipy_linalg.html#special-matrices)
* [Multidimensional image processing (`cupyx.scipy.ndimage`)](scipy_ndimage.html)
  + [Filters](scipy_ndimage.html#filters)
  + [Fourier filters](scipy_ndimage.html#fourier-filters)
  + [Interpolation](scipy_ndimage.html#interpolation)
  + [Measurements](scipy_ndimage.html#measurements)
  + [Morphology](scipy_ndimage.html#morphology)
  + [OpenCV mode](scipy_ndimage.html#opencv-mode)
* [Signal processing (`cupyx.scipy.signal`)](scipy_signal.html)
  + [Convolution](scipy_signal.html#convolution)
  + [B-Splines](scipy_signal.html#b-splines)
  + [Filtering](scipy_signal.html#filtering)
  + [Filter design](scipy_signal.html#filter-design)
  + [Matlab-style IIR filter design](scipy_signal.html#matlab-style-iir-filter-design)
  + [Low-level filter design functions](scipy_signal.html#low-level-filter-design-functions)
  + [LTI representations](scipy_signal.html#lti-representations)
  + [Continuous-time linear systems](scipy_signal.html#continuous-time-linear-systems)
  + [Discrete-time linear systems](scipy_signal.html#discrete-time-linear-systems)
  + [Waveforms](scipy_signal.html#waveforms)
  + [Window functions](scipy_signal.html#window-functions)
  + [Wavelets](scipy_signal.html#wavelets)
  + [Peak finding](scipy_signal.html#peak-finding)
  + [Spectral analysis](scipy_signal.html#spectral-analysis)
  + [Chirp Z-transform and Zoom FFT](scipy_signal.html#chirp-z-transform-and-zoom-fft)
* [Signal processing windows (`cupyx.scipy.signal.windows`)](scipy_signal_windows.html)
  + [cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.get\_window](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.get_window.html)
  + [cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.barthann](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.barthann.html)
  + [cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.bartlett](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.bartlett.html)
  + [cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.blackman](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.blackman.html)
  + [cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.blackmanharris](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.blackmanharris.html)
  + [cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.bohman](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.bohman.html)
  + [cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.boxcar](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.boxcar.html)
  + [cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.chebwin](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.chebwin.html)
  + [cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.cosine](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.cosine.html)
  + [cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.exponential](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.exponential.html)
  + [cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.flattop](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.flattop.html)
  + [cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.gaussian](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.gaussian.html)
  + [cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.general\_cosine](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.general_cosine.html)

| [`ifft2`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fft.ifft2.html#cupyx.scipy.fft.ifft2 "cupyx.scipy.fft.ifft2")(x[, s, axes, norm, overwrite\_x, plan]) | Compute the two-dimensional inverse FFT. |

| [`ifftn`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fft.ifftn.html#cupyx.scipy.fft.ifftn "cupyx.scipy.fft.ifftn")(x[, s, axes, norm, overwrite\_x, plan]) | Compute the N-dimensional inverse FFT. |
| [`rfft`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fft.rfft.html#cupyx.scipy.fft.rfft "cupyx.scipy.fft.rfft")(x[, n, axis, norm, overwrite\_x, plan]) | Compute the one-dimensional FFT for real input. |
| [`irfft`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fft.irfft.html#cupyx.scipy.fft.irfft "cupyx.scipy.fft.irfft")(x[, n, axis, norm, overwrite\_x, plan]) | Compute the one-dimensional inverse FFT for real input. |
| [`rfft2`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fft.rfft2.html#cupyx.scipy.fft.rfft2 "cupyx.scipy.fft.rfft2")(x[, s, axes, norm, overwrite\_x, plan]) | Compute the two-dimensional FFT for real input. |
| [`irfft2`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fft.irfft2.html#cupyx.scipy.fft.irfft2 "cupyx.scipy.fft.irfft2")(x[, s, axes, norm, overwrite\_x, plan]) | Compute the two-dimensional inverse FFT for real input. |
| [`rfftn`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fft.rfftn.html#cupyx.scipy.fft.rfftn "cupyx.scipy.fft.rfftn")(x[, s, axes, norm, overwrite\_x, plan]) | Compute the N-dimensional FFT for real input. |
| [`irfftn`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fft.irfftn.html#cupyx.scipy.fft.irfftn "cupyx.scipy.fft.irfftn")(x[, s, axes, norm, overwrite\_x, plan]) | Compute the N-dimensional inverse FFT for real input. |
| [`hfft`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fft.hfft.html#cupyx.scipy.fft.hfft "cupyx.scipy.fft.hfft")(x[, n, axis, norm, overwrite\_x, plan]) | Compute the FFT of a signal that has Hermitian symmetry. |
| [`ihfft`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fft.ihfft.html#cupyx.scipy.fft.ihfft "cupyx.scipy.fft.ihfft")(x[, n, axis, norm, overwrite\_x, plan]) | Compute the FFT of a signal that has Hermitian symmetry. |
| [`hfft2`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fft.hfft2.html#cupyx.scipy.fft.hfft2 "cupyx.scipy.fft.hfft2")(x[, s, axes, norm, overwrite\_x, plan]) | Compute the FFT of a two-dimensional signal that has Hermitian symmetry. |

## Discrete Cosine and Sine Transforms (DST and DCT)[#](#discrete-cosine-and-sine-transforms-dst-and-dct "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`dct`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fft.dct.html#cupyx.scipy.fft.dct "cupyx.scipy.fft.dct")(x[, type, n, axis, norm, overwrite\_x]) | Return the Discrete Cosine Transform of an array, x. |
| [`idct`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fft.idct.html#cupyx.scipy.fft.idct "cupyx.scipy.fft.idct")(x[, type, n, axis, norm, overwrite\_x]) | Return the Inverse Discrete Cosine Transform of an array, x. |
| [`dctn`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fft.dctn.html#cupyx.scipy.fft.dctn "cupyx.scipy.fft.dctn")(x[, type, s, axes, norm, overwrite\_x]) | Compute a multidimensional Discrete Cosine Transform. |
| [`idctn`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fft.idctn.html#cupyx.scipy.fft.idctn "cupyx.scipy.fft.idctn")(x[, type, s, axes, norm, overwrite\_x]) | Compute a multidimensional Discrete Cosine Transform. |
| [`dst`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fft.dst.html#cupyx.scipy.fft.dst "cupyx.scipy.fft.dst")(x[, type, n, axis, norm, overwrite\_x]) | Return the Discrete Sine Transform of an array, x. |
| [`idst`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fft.idst.html#cupyx.scipy.fft.idst "cupyx.scipy.fft.idst")(x[, type, n, axis, norm, overwrite\_x]) | Return the Inverse Discrete Sine Transform of an array, x. |
| [`dstn`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fft.dstn.html#cupyx.scipy.fft.dstn "cupyx.scipy.fft.dstn")(x[, type, s, axes, norm, overwrite\_x]) | Compute a multidimensional Discrete Sine Transform. |
| [`idstn`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fft.idstn.html#cupyx.scipy.fft.idstn "cupyx.scipy.fft.idstn")(x[, type, s, axes, norm, overwrite\_x]) | Compute a multidimensional Discrete Sine Transform. |
## Fast Hankel Transforms[#](#fast-hankel-transforms "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`fht`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fft.fht.html#cupyx.scipy.fft.fht "cupyx.scipy.fft.fht")(a, dln, mu[, offset, bias]) | Compute the fast Hankel transform. |
| [`ifht`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fft.ifht.html#cupyx.scipy.fft.ifht "cupyx.scipy.fft.ifht")(A, dln, mu[, offset, bias]) | Compute the inverse fast Hankel transform. |
## Helper functions[#](#helper-functions "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`fftshift`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fft.fftshift.html#cupyx.scipy.fft.fftshift "cupyx.scipy.fft.fftshift")(x[, axes]) | Shift the zero-frequency component to the center of the spectrum. |
| [`ifftshift`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fft.ifftshift.html#cupyx.scipy.fft.ifftshift "cupyx.scipy.fft.ifftshift")(x[, axes]) | The inverse of [`fftshift()`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fft.fftshift.html#cupyx.scipy.fft.fftshift "cupyx.scipy.fft.fftshift"). |
| [`fftfreq`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fft.fftfreq.html#cupyx.scipy.fft.fftfreq "cupyx.scipy.fft.fftfreq")(n[, d]) | Return the FFT sample frequencies. |
| [`rfftfreq`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fft.rfftfreq.html#cupyx.scipy.fft.rfftfreq "cupyx.scipy.fft.rfftfreq")(n[, d]) | Return the FFT sample frequencies for real input. |
| [`next_fast_len`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fft.next_fast_len.html#cupyx.scipy.fft.next_fast_len "cupyx.scipy.fft.next_fast_len")(target[, real]) | Find the next fast size to `fft`. |
## Code compatibility features[#](#code-compatibility-features "Permalink to this heading")
1. As with other FFT modules in CuPy, FFT functions in this module can take advantage of an existing cuFFT plan (returned by [`get_fft_plan()`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fftpack.get_fft_plan.html#cupyx.scipy.fftpack.get_fft_plan "cupyx.scipy.fftpack.get_fft_plan")) to accelerate the computation. The plan can be either passed in explicitly via the keyword-only `plan` argument or used as a context manager. One exception to this are the DCT and DST transforms, which do not currently support a plan argument.
2. Like in `scipy.fft`, all FFT functions in this module have an optional argument `overwrite_x` (default is `False`), which has the same semantics as in `scipy.fft`: when it is set to `True`, the input array `x` *can* (not *will*) be overwritten arbitrarily. For this reason, when an in-place FFT is desired, the user should always reassign the input in the following manner: `x = cupyx.scipy.fftpack.fft(x, ..., overwrite_x=True, ...)`.
3. The `cupyx.scipy.fft` module can also be used as a backend for `scipy.fft` e.g. by installing with `scipy.fft.set_backend(cupyx.scipy.fft)`. This can allow `scipy.fft` to work with both `numpy` and `cupy` arrays. For more information, see [SciPy FFT backend](../user_guide/fft.html#scipy-fft-backend).
4. The boolean switch `cupy.fft.config.use_multi_gpus` also affects the FFT functions in this module, see [Discrete Fourier Transform (cupy.fft)](fft.html). Moreover, this switch is *honored* when planning manually using [`get_fft_plan()`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fftpack.get_fft_plan.html#cupyx.scipy.fftpack.get_fft_plan "cupyx.scipy.fftpack.get_fft_plan").
5. Both type II and III DCT and DST transforms are implemented. Type I and IV transforms are currently unavailable.
[previous
Routines (SciPy)](scipy.html "previous page")

cupyx.scipy.fft.fft](generated/cupyx.scipy.fft.fft.html "next page")

# Legacy discrete fourier transforms ([`cupyx.scipy.fftpack`](#module-cupyx.scipy.fftpack "cupyx.scipy.fftpack"))[#](#legacy-discrete-fourier-transforms-cupyx-scipy-fftpack "Permalink to this heading")
Note
As of SciPy version 1.4.0, [`scipy.fft`](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/fft.html#module-scipy.fft "(in SciPy v1.17.0)") is recommended over
[`scipy.fftpack`](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/fftpack.html#module-scipy.fftpack "(in SciPy v1.17.0)"). Consider using [`cupyx.scipy.fft`](scipy_fft.html#module-cupyx.scipy.fft "cupyx.scipy.fft") instead.
Hint
[SciPy API Reference: Legacy discrete Fourier transforms (scipy.fftpack)](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/fftpack.html)
## Fast Fourier Transforms (FFTs)[#](#fast-fourier-transforms-ffts "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`fft`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fftpack.fft.html#cupyx.scipy.fftpack.fft "cupyx.scipy.fftpack.fft")(x[, n, axis, overwrite\_x, plan]) | Compute the one-dimensional FFT. |
| [`ifft`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fftpack.ifft.html#cupyx.scipy.fftpack.ifft "cupyx.scipy.fftpack.ifft")(x[, n, axis, overwrite\_x, plan]) | Compute the one-dimensional inverse FFT. |
| [`fft2`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fftpack.fft2.html#cupyx.scipy.fftpack.fft2 "cupyx.scipy.fftpack.fft2")(x[, shape, axes, overwrite\_x, plan]) | Compute the two-dimensional FFT. |
| [`ifft2`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fftpack.ifft2.html#cupyx.scipy.fftpack.ifft2 "cupyx.scipy.fftpack.ifft2")(x[, shape, axes, overwrite\_x, plan]) | Compute the two-dimensional inverse FFT. |
| [`fftn`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fftpack.fftn.html#cupyx.scipy.fftpack.fftn "cupyx.scipy.fftpack.fftn")(x[, shape, axes, overwrite\_x, plan]) | Compute the N-dimensional FFT. |
| [`ifftn`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fftpack.ifftn.html#cupyx.scipy.fftpack.ifftn "cupyx.scipy.fftpack.ifftn")(x[, shape, axes, overwrite\_x, plan]) | Compute the N-dimensional inverse FFT. |
| [`rfft`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fftpack.rfft.html#cupyx.scipy.fftpack.rfft "cupyx.scipy.fftpack.rfft")(x[, n, axis, overwrite\_x, plan]) | Compute the one-dimensional FFT for real input. |
| [`irfft`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fftpack.irfft.html#cupyx.scipy.fftpack.irfft "cupyx.scipy.fftpack.irfft")(x[, n, axis, overwrite\_x]) | Compute the one-dimensional inverse FFT for real input. |
| [`get_fft_plan`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fftpack.get_fft_plan.html#cupyx.scipy.fftpack.get_fft_plan "cupyx.scipy.fftpack.get_fft_plan")(a[, shape, axes, value\_type]) | Generate a CUDA FFT plan for transforming up to three axes. |
## Code compatibility features[#](#code-compatibility-features "Permalink to this heading")
1. As with other FFT modules in CuPy, FFT functions in this module can take advantage of an existing cuFFT plan (returned by [`get_fft_plan()`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fftpack.get_fft_plan.html#cupyx.scipy.fftpack.get_fft_plan "cupyx.scipy.fftpack.get_fft_plan")) to accelerate the computation. The plan can be either passed in explicitly via the `plan` argument or used as a context manager. The argument `plan` is currently experimental and the interface may be changed in the future version. The [`get_fft_plan()`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fftpack.get_fft_plan.html#cupyx.scipy.fftpack.get_fft_plan "cupyx.scipy.fftpack.get_fft_plan") function has no counterpart in `scipy.fftpack`.
2. Like in `scipy.fftpack`, all FFT functions in this module have an optional argument `overwrite_x` (default is `False`), which has the same semantics as in `scipy.fftpack`: when it is set to `True`, the input array `x` *can* (not *will*) be overwritten arbitrarily. For this reason, when an in-place FFT is desired, the user should always reassign the input in the following manner: `x = cupyx.scipy.fftpack.fft(x, ..., overwrite_x=True, ...)`.
3. The boolean switch `cupy.fft.config.use_multi_gpus` also affects the FFT functions in this module, see [Discrete Fourier Transform (cupy.fft)](fft.html). Moreover, this switch is *honored* when planning manually using [`get_fft_plan()`](generated/cupyx.scipy.fftpack.get_fft_plan.html#cupyx.scipy.fftpack.get_fft_plan "cupyx.scipy.fftpack.get_fft_plan").

| [`pchip_interpolate`](generated/cupyx.scipy.interpolate.pchip_interpolate.html#cupyx.scipy.interpolate.pchip_interpolate "cupyx.scipy.interpolate.pchip_interpolate")(xi, yi, x[, der, axis]) | Convenience function for pchip interpolation. |
| [`CubicHermiteSpline`](generated/cupyx.scipy.interpolate.CubicHermiteSpline.html#cupyx.scipy.interpolate.CubicHermiteSpline "cupyx.scipy.interpolate.CubicHermiteSpline")(x, y, dydx[, axis, ...]) | Piecewise-cubic interpolator matching values and first derivatives. |
| [`PchipInterpolator`](generated/cupyx.scipy.interpolate.PchipInterpolator.html#cupyx.scipy.interpolate.PchipInterpolator "cupyx.scipy.interpolate.PchipInterpolator")(x, y[, axis, extrapolate]) | PCHIP 1-D monotonic cubic interpolation. |
| [`Akima1DInterpolator`](generated/cupyx.scipy.interpolate.Akima1DInterpolator.html#cupyx.scipy.interpolate.Akima1DInterpolator "cupyx.scipy.interpolate.Akima1DInterpolator")(x, y[, axis]) | Akima interpolator |
| [`PPoly`](generated/cupyx.scipy.interpolate.PPoly.html#cupyx.scipy.interpolate.PPoly "cupyx.scipy.interpolate.PPoly")(c, x[, extrapolate, axis]) | Piecewise polynomial in terms of coefficients and breakpoints The polynomial between `x[i]` and `x[i + 1]` is written in the local power basis. |
| [`BPoly`](generated/cupyx.scipy.interpolate.BPoly.html#cupyx.scipy.interpolate.BPoly "cupyx.scipy.interpolate.BPoly")(c, x[, extrapolate, axis]) | Piecewise polynomial in terms of coefficients and breakpoints. |
| [`CubicSpline`](generated/cupyx.scipy.interpolate.CubicSpline.html#cupyx.scipy.interpolate.CubicSpline "cupyx.scipy.interpolate.CubicSpline")(x, y[, axis, bc\_type, extrapolate]) | Cubic spline data interpolator. |
| [`interp1d`](generated/cupyx.scipy.interpolate.interp1d.html#cupyx.scipy.interpolate.interp1d "cupyx.scipy.interpolate.interp1d")(x, y[, kind, axis, copy, ...]) | Interpolate a 1-D function. |
## 1-D Splines[#](#d-splines "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`BSpline`](generated/cupyx.scipy.interpolate.BSpline.html#cupyx.scipy.interpolate.BSpline "cupyx.scipy.interpolate.BSpline")(t, c, k[, extrapolate, axis]) | Univariate spline in the B-spline basis. |
| [`make_interp_spline`](generated/cupyx.scipy.interpolate.make_interp_spline.html#cupyx.scipy.interpolate.make_interp_spline "cupyx.scipy.interpolate.make_interp_spline")(x, y[, k, t, bc\_type, ...]) | Compute the (coefficients of) interpolating B-spline. |
| [`make_lsq_spline`](generated/cupyx.scipy.interpolate.make_lsq_spline.html#cupyx.scipy.interpolate.make_lsq_spline "cupyx.scipy.interpolate.make_lsq_spline")(x, y, t[, k, w, axis, ...]) | Construct a BSpline via an LSQ (Least SQuared) fit. |
| [`splder`](generated/cupyx.scipy.interpolate.splder.html#cupyx.scipy.interpolate.splder "cupyx.scipy.interpolate.splder")(tck[, n]) | Compute the spline representation of the derivative of a given spline |
| [`splantider`](generated/cupyx.scipy.interpolate.splantider.html#cupyx.scipy.interpolate.splantider "cupyx.scipy.interpolate.splantider")(tck[, n]) | Compute the spline for the antiderivative (integral) of a given spline. |

|  |  |
| --- | --- |

| [`LSQUnivariateSpline`](generated/cupyx.scipy.interpolate.LSQUnivariateSpline.html#cupyx.scipy.interpolate.LSQUnivariateSpline "cupyx.scipy.interpolate.LSQUnivariateSpline")(x, y, t[, w, bbox, k, ext]) | 1-D spline with explicit internal knots. |

Unstructured data:
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`LinearNDInterpolator`](generated/cupyx.scipy.interpolate.LinearNDInterpolator.html#cupyx.scipy.interpolate.LinearNDInterpolator "cupyx.scipy.interpolate.LinearNDInterpolator")(points, values[, ...]) | Piecewise linear interpolant in N > 1 dimensions. |
| [`NearestNDInterpolator`](generated/cupyx.scipy.interpolate.NearestNDInterpolator.html#cupyx.scipy.interpolate.NearestNDInterpolator "cupyx.scipy.interpolate.NearestNDInterpolator")(x, y[, rescale, ...]) | NearestNDInterpolator(x, y). |
| [`CloughTocher2DInterpolator`](generated/cupyx.scipy.interpolate.CloughTocher2DInterpolator.html#cupyx.scipy.interpolate.CloughTocher2DInterpolator "cupyx.scipy.interpolate.CloughTocher2DInterpolator")(points, values[, ...]) | CloughTocher2DInterpolator(points, values, tol=1e-6). |
| [`RBFInterpolator`](generated/cupyx.scipy.interpolate.RBFInterpolator.html#cupyx.scipy.interpolate.RBFInterpolator "cupyx.scipy.interpolate.RBFInterpolator")(y, d[, neighbors, ...]) | Radial basis function (RBF) interpolation in N dimensions. |
For data on a grid:
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`interpn`](generated/cupyx.scipy.interpolate.interpn.html#cupyx.scipy.interpolate.interpn "cupyx.scipy.interpolate.interpn")(points, values, xi[, method, ...]) | Multidimensional interpolation on regular or rectilinear grids. |
| [`RegularGridInterpolator`](generated/cupyx.scipy.interpolate.RegularGridInterpolator.html#cupyx.scipy.interpolate.RegularGridInterpolator "cupyx.scipy.interpolate.RegularGridInterpolator")(points, values[, ...]) | Interpolator on a regular or rectilinear grid in arbitrary dimensions. |
Tensor product polynomials:
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`NdPPoly`](generated/cupyx.scipy.interpolate.NdPPoly.html#cupyx.scipy.interpolate.NdPPoly "cupyx.scipy.interpolate.NdPPoly")(c, x[, extrapolate]) | Piecewise tensor product polynomial |
| [`NdBSpline`](generated/cupyx.scipy.interpolate.NdBSpline.html#cupyx.scipy.interpolate.NdBSpline "cupyx.scipy.interpolate.NdBSpline")(t, c, k, \*[, extrapolate]) | Tensor product spline object. |

# Linear algebra ([`cupyx.scipy.linalg`](#module-cupyx.scipy.linalg "cupyx.scipy.linalg"))[#](#linear-algebra-cupyx-scipy-linalg "Permalink to this heading")

|  |  |
| --- | --- |

| [`kron`](generated/cupyx.scipy.linalg.kron.html#cupyx.scipy.linalg.kron "cupyx.scipy.linalg.kron")(a, b) | Kronecker product. |

| [`bandwidth`](generated/cupyx.scipy.linalg.bandwidth.html#cupyx.scipy.linalg.bandwidth "cupyx.scipy.linalg.bandwidth")(a) | Return the lower and upper bandwidth of a 2D numeric array. |

|  |  |
| --- | --- |

|  |  |

| [`circulant`](generated/cupyx.scipy.linalg.circulant.html#cupyx.scipy.linalg.circulant "cupyx.scipy.linalg.circulant")(c) | Construct a circulant matrix. |

| [`dft`](generated/cupyx.scipy.linalg.dft.html#cupyx.scipy.linalg.dft "cupyx.scipy.linalg.dft")(n[, scale]) | Discrete Fourier transform matrix. |
| [`fiedler`](generated/cupyx.scipy.linalg.fiedler.html#cupyx.scipy.linalg.fiedler "cupyx.scipy.linalg.fiedler")(a) | Returns a symmetric Fiedler matrix |
| [`fiedler_companion`](generated/cupyx.scipy.linalg.fiedler_companion.html#cupyx.scipy.linalg.fiedler_companion "cupyx.scipy.linalg.fiedler_companion")(a) | Returns a Fiedler companion matrix |
| [`hadamard`](generated/cupyx.scipy.linalg.hadamard.html#cupyx.scipy.linalg.hadamard "cupyx.scipy.linalg.hadamard")(n[, dtype]) | Construct an Hadamard matrix. |
| [`hankel`](generated/cupyx.scipy.linalg.hankel.html#cupyx.scipy.linalg.hankel "cupyx.scipy.linalg.hankel")(c[, r]) | Construct a Hankel matrix. |
| [`helmert`](generated/cupyx.scipy.linalg.helmert.html#cupyx.scipy.linalg.helmert "cupyx.scipy.linalg.helmert")(n[, full]) | Create an Helmert matrix of order `n`. |
| [`hilbert`](generated/cupyx.scipy.linalg.hilbert.html#cupyx.scipy.linalg.hilbert "cupyx.scipy.linalg.hilbert")(n) | Create a Hilbert matrix of order `n`. |
| [`leslie`](generated/cupyx.scipy.linalg.leslie.html#cupyx.scipy.linalg.leslie "cupyx.scipy.linalg.leslie")(f, s) | Create a Leslie matrix. |
| [`toeplitz`](generated/cupyx.scipy.linalg.toeplitz.html#cupyx.scipy.linalg.toeplitz "cupyx.scipy.linalg.toeplitz")(c[, r]) | Construct a Toeplitz matrix. |

# Multidimensional image processing ([`cupyx.scipy.ndimage`](#module-cupyx.scipy.ndimage "cupyx.scipy.ndimage"))[#](#multidimensional-image-processing-cupyx-scipy-ndimage "Permalink to this heading")

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`convolve`](generated/cupyx.scipy.ndimage.convolve.html#cupyx.scipy.ndimage.convolve "cupyx.scipy.ndimage.convolve")(input, weights[, output, mode, ...]) | Multi-dimensional convolution. |

| [`correlate1d`](generated/cupyx.scipy.ndimage.correlate1d.html#cupyx.scipy.ndimage.correlate1d "cupyx.scipy.ndimage.correlate1d")(input, weights[, axis, output, ...]) | One-dimensional correlate. |
| [`gaussian_filter`](generated/cupyx.scipy.ndimage.gaussian_filter.html#cupyx.scipy.ndimage.gaussian_filter "cupyx.scipy.ndimage.gaussian_filter")(input, sigma[, order, ...]) | Multi-dimensional Gaussian filter. |
| [`gaussian_filter1d`](generated/cupyx.scipy.ndimage.gaussian_filter1d.html#cupyx.scipy.ndimage.gaussian_filter1d "cupyx.scipy.ndimage.gaussian_filter1d")(input, sigma[, axis, ...]) | One-dimensional Gaussian filter along the given axis. |
| [`gaussian_gradient_magnitude`](generated/cupyx.scipy.ndimage.gaussian_gradient_magnitude.html#cupyx.scipy.ndimage.gaussian_gradient_magnitude "cupyx.scipy.ndimage.gaussian_gradient_magnitude")(input, sigma[, ...]) | Multi-dimensional gradient magnitude using Gaussian derivatives. |
| [`gaussian_laplace`](generated/cupyx.scipy.ndimage.gaussian_laplace.html#cupyx.scipy.ndimage.gaussian_laplace "cupyx.scipy.ndimage.gaussian_laplace")(input, sigma[, output, ...]) | Multi-dimensional Laplace filter using Gaussian second derivatives. |
| [`generic_filter`](generated/cupyx.scipy.ndimage.generic_filter.html#cupyx.scipy.ndimage.generic_filter "cupyx.scipy.ndimage.generic_filter")(input, function[, size, ...]) | Compute a multi-dimensional filter using the provided raw kernel or reduction kernel. |
| [`generic_filter1d`](generated/cupyx.scipy.ndimage.generic_filter1d.html#cupyx.scipy.ndimage.generic_filter1d "cupyx.scipy.ndimage.generic_filter1d")(input, function, filter\_size) | Compute a 1D filter along the given axis using the provided raw kernel. |
| [`generic_gradient_magnitude`](generated/cupyx.scipy.ndimage.generic_gradient_magnitude.html#cupyx.scipy.ndimage.generic_gradient_magnitude "cupyx.scipy.ndimage.generic_gradient_magnitude")(input, derivative) | Multi-dimensional gradient magnitude filter using a provided derivative function. |
| [`generic_laplace`](generated/cupyx.scipy.ndimage.generic_laplace.html#cupyx.scipy.ndimage.generic_laplace "cupyx.scipy.ndimage.generic_laplace")(input, derivative2[, ...]) | Multi-dimensional Laplace filter using a provided second derivative function. |
| [`laplace`](generated/cupyx.scipy.ndimage.laplace.html#cupyx.scipy.ndimage.laplace "cupyx.scipy.ndimage.laplace")(input[, output, mode, cval, axes]) | Multi-dimensional Laplace filter based on approximate second derivatives. |
| [`maximum_filter`](generated/cupyx.scipy.ndimage.maximum_filter.html#cupyx.scipy.ndimage.maximum_filter "cupyx.scipy.ndimage.maximum_filter")(input[, size, footprint, ...]) | Multi-dimensional maximum filter. |
| [`maximum_filter1d`](generated/cupyx.scipy.ndimage.maximum_filter1d.html#cupyx.scipy.ndimage.maximum_filter1d "cupyx.scipy.ndimage.maximum_filter1d")(input, size[, axis, ...]) | Compute the maximum filter along a single axis. |
| [`median_filter`](generated/cupyx.scipy.ndimage.median_filter.html#cupyx.scipy.ndimage.median_filter "cupyx.scipy.ndimage.median_filter")(input[, size, footprint, ...]) | Multi-dimensional median filter. |
| [`minimum_filter`](generated/cupyx.scipy.ndimage.minimum_filter.html#cupyx.scipy.ndimage.minimum_filter "cupyx.scipy.ndimage.minimum_filter")(input[, size, footprint, ...]) | Multi-dimensional minimum filter. |
| [`minimum_filter1d`](generated/cupyx.scipy.ndimage.minimum_filter1d.html#cupyx.scipy.ndimage.minimum_filter1d "cupyx.scipy.ndimage.minimum_filter1d")(input, size[, axis, ...]) | Compute the minimum filter along a single axis. |
| [`percentile_filter`](generated/cupyx.scipy.ndimage.percentile_filter.html#cupyx.scipy.ndimage.percentile_filter "cupyx.scipy.ndimage.percentile_filter")(input, percentile[, size, ...]) | Multi-dimensional percentile filter. |
| [`prewitt`](generated/cupyx.scipy.ndimage.prewitt.html#cupyx.scipy.ndimage.prewitt "cupyx.scipy.ndimage.prewitt")(input[, axis, output, mode, cval]) | Compute a Prewitt filter along the given axis. |
| [`rank_filter`](generated/cupyx.scipy.ndimage.rank_filter.html#cupyx.scipy.ndimage.rank_filter "cupyx.scipy.ndimage.rank_filter")(input, rank[, size, footprint, ...]) | Multi-dimensional rank filter. |
| [`sobel`](generated/cupyx.scipy.ndimage.sobel.html#cupyx.scipy.ndimage.sobel "cupyx.scipy.ndimage.sobel")(input[, axis, output, mode, cval]) | Compute a Sobel filter along the given axis. |
| [`uniform_filter`](generated/cupyx.scipy.ndimage.uniform_filter.html#cupyx.scipy.ndimage.uniform_filter "cupyx.scipy.ndimage.uniform_filter")(input[, size, output, mode, ...]) | Multi-dimensional uniform filter. |
| [`uniform_filter1d`](generated/cupyx.scipy.ndimage.uniform_filter1d.html#cupyx.scipy.ndimage.uniform_filter1d "cupyx.scipy.ndimage.uniform_filter1d")(input, size[, axis, ...]) | One-dimensional uniform filter along the given axis. |

| [`zoom`](generated/cupyx.scipy.ndimage.zoom.html#cupyx.scipy.ndimage.zoom "cupyx.scipy.ndimage.zoom")(input, zoom[, output, order, mode, ...]) | Zoom an array. |

|  |  |
| --- | --- |

## OpenCV mode[#](#opencv-mode "Permalink to this heading")
[`cupyx.scipy.ndimage`](#module-cupyx.scipy.ndimage "cupyx.scipy.ndimage") supports additional mode, `opencv`.
If it is given, the function performs like [cv2.warpAffine](https://docs.opencv.org/master/da/d54/group__imgproc__transform.html#ga0203d9ee5fcd28d40dbc4a1ea4451983) or [cv2.resize](https://docs.opencv.org/master/da/d54/group__imgproc__transform.html#ga47a974309e9102f5f08231edc7e7529d). Example:
```
import cupyx.scipy.ndimage
import cupy as cp
import cv2
im = cv2.imread('TODO') # pls fill in your image path
trans_mat = cp.eye(4)
trans_mat[0][0] = trans_mat[1][1] = 0.5
smaller_shape = (im.shape[0] // 2, im.shape[1] // 2, 3)
smaller = cp.zeros(smaller_shape) # preallocate memory for resized image
cupyx.scipy.ndimage.affine_transform(im, trans_mat, output_shape=smaller_shape,
                                     output=smaller, mode='opencv')
cv2.imwrite('smaller.jpg', cp.asnumpy(smaller)) # smaller image saved locally
```

| [`kaiser_beta`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.kaiser_beta.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.kaiser_beta "cupyx.scipy.signal.kaiser_beta")(a) | Compute the Kaiser parameter beta, given the attenuation a. |
| [`kaiserord`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.kaiserord.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.kaiserord "cupyx.scipy.signal.kaiserord")(ripple, width) | Determine the filter window parameters for the Kaiser window method. |
| [`unique_roots`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.unique_roots.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.unique_roots "cupyx.scipy.signal.unique_roots")(p[, tol, rtype]) | Determine unique roots and their multiplicities from a list of roots. |
| [`residue`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.residue.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.residue "cupyx.scipy.signal.residue")(b, a[, tol, rtype]) | Compute partial-fraction expansion of b(s) / a(s). |
| [`residuez`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.residuez.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.residuez "cupyx.scipy.signal.residuez")(b, a[, tol, rtype]) | Compute partial-fraction expansion of b(z) / a(z). |
| [`invres`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.invres.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.invres "cupyx.scipy.signal.invres")(r, p, k[, tol, rtype]) | Compute b(s) and a(s) from partial fraction expansion. |
| [`invresz`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.invresz.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.invresz "cupyx.scipy.signal.invresz")(r, p, k[, tol, rtype]) | Compute b(z) and a(z) from partial fraction expansion. |
| [`BadCoefficients`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.BadCoefficients.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.BadCoefficients "cupyx.scipy.signal.BadCoefficients") | Warning about badly conditioned filter coefficients |

| [`lp2lp_zpk`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.lp2lp_zpk.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.lp2lp_zpk "cupyx.scipy.signal.lp2lp_zpk")(z, p, k[, wo]) | Transform a lowpass filter prototype to a different frequency. |

| --- | --- |
