| [`zpk2sos`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.zpk2sos.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.zpk2sos "cupyx.scipy.signal.zpk2sos")(z, p, k[, pairing, analog]) | Return second-order sections from zeros, poles, and gain of a system |
| [`zpk2ss`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.zpk2ss.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.zpk2ss "cupyx.scipy.signal.zpk2ss")(z, p, k) | Zero-pole-gain representation to state-space representation |
| [`tf2zpk`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.tf2zpk.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.tf2zpk "cupyx.scipy.signal.tf2zpk")(b, a) | Return zero, pole, gain (z, p, k) representation from a numerator, denominator representation of a linear filter. |
| [`tf2sos`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.tf2sos.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.tf2sos "cupyx.scipy.signal.tf2sos")(b, a[, pairing, analog]) | Return second-order sections from transfer function representation |
| [`tf2ss`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.tf2ss.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.tf2ss "cupyx.scipy.signal.tf2ss")(num, den) | Transfer function to state-space representation. |
| [`ss2tf`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.ss2tf.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.ss2tf "cupyx.scipy.signal.ss2tf")(A, B, C, D[, input]) | State-space to transfer function. |
| [`ss2zpk`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.ss2zpk.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.ss2zpk "cupyx.scipy.signal.ss2zpk")(A, B, C, D[, input]) | State-space representation to zero-pole-gain representation. |
| [`sos2tf`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.sos2tf.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.sos2tf "cupyx.scipy.signal.sos2tf")(sos) | Return a single transfer function from a series of second-order sections |
| [`sos2zpk`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.sos2zpk.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.sos2zpk "cupyx.scipy.signal.sos2zpk")(sos) | Return zeros, poles, and gain of a series of second-order sections |
| [`cont2discrete`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.cont2discrete.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.cont2discrete "cupyx.scipy.signal.cont2discrete")(system, dt[, method, alpha]) | Transform a continuous to a discrete state-space system. |
| [`place_poles`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.place_poles.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.place_poles "cupyx.scipy.signal.place_poles")(A, B, poles[, method, rtol, maxiter]) | Compute K such that eigenvalues (A - dot(B, K))=poles. |

## Window functions[#](#window-functions "Permalink to this heading")
For window functions, see the [`cupyx.scipy.signal.windows`](scipy_signal_windows.html#module-cupyx.scipy.signal.windows "cupyx.scipy.signal.windows") namespace.
In the [`cupyx.scipy.signal`](#module-cupyx.scipy.signal "cupyx.scipy.signal") namespace, there is a convenience function
to obtain these windows by name:
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`get_window`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.get_window.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.get_window "cupyx.scipy.signal.get_window")(window, Nx[, fftbins]) | Return a window of a given length and type. |

| --- | --- |

| [`qmf`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.qmf.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.qmf "cupyx.scipy.signal.qmf")(hk) | Return high-pass qmf filter from low-pass |
| [`ricker`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.ricker.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.ricker "cupyx.scipy.signal.ricker")(points, a) | Return a Ricker wavelet, also known as the "Mexican hat wavelet". |
| [`morlet2`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.morlet2.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.morlet2 "cupyx.scipy.signal.morlet2")(M, s[, w]) | Complex Morlet wavelet, designed to work with cwt. Returns the complete version of morlet wavelet, normalised according to s::. |
| [`cwt`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.cwt.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.cwt "cupyx.scipy.signal.cwt")(data, wavelet, widths) | Continuous wavelet transform. |

| [`check_NOLA`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.check_NOLA.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.check_NOLA "cupyx.scipy.signal.check_NOLA")(window, nperseg, noverlap[, tol]) | Check whether the Nonzero Overlap Add (NOLA) constraint is met. |
## Chirp Z-transform and Zoom FFT[#](#chirp-z-transform-and-zoom-fft "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`czt`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.czt.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.czt "cupyx.scipy.signal.czt")(x[, m, w, a, axis]) | Compute the frequency response around a spiral in the Z plane. |
| [`zoom_fft`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.zoom_fft.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.zoom_fft "cupyx.scipy.signal.zoom_fft")(x, fn[, m, fs, endpoint, axis]) | Compute the DFT of x only for frequencies in range fn. |
| [`CZT`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.CZT.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.CZT "cupyx.scipy.signal.CZT")(n[, m, w, a]) | Create a callable chirp z-transform function. |
| [`ZoomFFT`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.ZoomFFT.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.ZoomFFT "cupyx.scipy.signal.ZoomFFT")(n, fn[, m, fs, endpoint]) | Create a callable zoom FFT transform function. |
| [`czt_points`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.czt_points.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.czt_points "cupyx.scipy.signal.czt_points")(m[, w, a]) | Return the points at which the chirp z-transform is computed. |

# Signal processing windows ([`cupyx.scipy.signal.windows`](#module-cupyx.scipy.signal.windows "cupyx.scipy.signal.windows"))[#](#signal-processing-windows-cupyx-scipy-signal-windows "Permalink to this heading")
The suite of window functions for filtering and spectral estimation.
Hint
[SciPy API Reference: Signal processing windows (scipy.signal.windows)](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/signal.windows.html)
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`get_window`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.get_window.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.get_window "cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.get_window")(window, Nx[, fftbins]) | Return a window of a given length and type. |
| [`barthann`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.barthann.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.barthann "cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.barthann")(M[, sym]) | Return a modified Bartlett-Hann window. |
| [`bartlett`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.bartlett.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.bartlett "cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.bartlett")(M[, sym]) | Return a Bartlett window. |
| [`blackman`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.blackman.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.blackman "cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.blackman")(M[, sym]) | Return a Blackman window. |
| [`blackmanharris`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.blackmanharris.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.blackmanharris "cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.blackmanharris")(M[, sym]) | Return a minimum 4-term Blackman-Harris window. |
| [`bohman`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.bohman.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.bohman "cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.bohman")(M[, sym]) | Return a Bohman window. |
| [`boxcar`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.boxcar.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.boxcar "cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.boxcar")(M[, sym]) | Return a boxcar or rectangular window. |
| [`chebwin`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.chebwin.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.chebwin "cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.chebwin")(M, at[, sym]) | Return a Dolph-Chebyshev window. |
| [`cosine`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.cosine.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.cosine "cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.cosine")(M[, sym]) | Return a window with a simple cosine shape. |
| [`exponential`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.exponential.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.exponential "cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.exponential")(M[, center, tau, sym]) | Return an exponential (or Poisson) window. |
| [`flattop`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.flattop.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.flattop "cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.flattop")(M[, sym]) | Return a flat top window. |
| [`gaussian`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.gaussian.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.gaussian "cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.gaussian")(M, std[, sym]) | Return a Gaussian window. |
| [`general_cosine`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.general_cosine.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.general_cosine "cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.general_cosine")(M, a[, sym]) | Generic weighted sum of cosine terms window |
| [`general_gaussian`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.general_gaussian.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.general_gaussian "cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.general_gaussian")(M, p, sig[, sym]) | Return a window with a generalized Gaussian shape. |
| [`general_hamming`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.general_hamming.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.general_hamming "cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.general_hamming")(M, alpha[, sym]) | Return a generalized Hamming window. |
| [`hamming`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.hamming.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.hamming "cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.hamming")(M[, sym]) | Return a Hamming window. |
| [`hann`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.hann.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.hann "cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.hann")(M[, sym]) | Return a Hann window. |
| [`kaiser`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.kaiser.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.kaiser "cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.kaiser")(M, beta[, sym]) | Return a Kaiser window. |
| [`kaiser_bessel_derived`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.kaiser_bessel_derived.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.kaiser_bessel_derived "cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.kaiser_bessel_derived")(M, beta[, sym]) | Return a Kaiser-Bessel derived window. |
| [`nuttall`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.nuttall.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.nuttall "cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.nuttall")(M[, sym]) | Return a minimum 4-term Blackman-Harris window according to Nuttall. |
| [`parzen`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.parzen.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.parzen "cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.parzen")(M[, sym]) | Return a Parzen window. |
| [`taylor`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.taylor.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.taylor "cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.taylor")(M[, nbar, sll, norm, sym]) | Return a Taylor window. |
| [`triang`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.triang.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.triang "cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.triang")(M[, sym]) | Return a triangular window. |
| [`lanczos`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.lanczos.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.lanczos "cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.lanczos")(M[, sym]) | Return a Lanczos window also known as a sinc window. |
| [`tukey`](generated/cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.tukey.html#cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.tukey "cupyx.scipy.signal.windows.tukey")(M[, alpha, sym]) | Return a Tukey window, also known as a tapered cosine window. |

# Sparse matrices ([`cupyx.scipy.sparse`](#module-cupyx.scipy.sparse "cupyx.scipy.sparse"))[#](#sparse-matrices-cupyx-scipy-sparse "Permalink to this heading")
Hint
[SciPy API Reference: Sparse matrices (scipy.sparse)](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/sparse.html)
CuPy supports sparse matrices using [cuSPARSE](https://developer.nvidia.com/cusparse).
These matrices have the same interfaces of [SciPy’s sparse matrices](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/sparse.html).
## Index dtype (int32 / int64)[#](#index-dtype-int32-int64 "Permalink to this heading")
Like SciPy, CuPy sparse matrices automatically choose the index
dtype (`indices`, `indptr`, `row`, `col`) based on the
matrix dimensions and index values:
* **int32** when all index values and dimensions fit in a 32-bit
  integer (the common case).
* **int64** when any dimension or index value exceeds
  `2**31 - 1`.
The dtype is chosen by `get_index_dtype` (mirroring SciPy’s logic)
and is preserved through format conversions, arithmetic, and indexing.
Operations that delegate to cuSPARSE use the native Generic API
(`SpMatDescr`) for int64 where available, with pure-CuPy
fallbacks for legacy int32-only APIs (e.g., `csr2cscEx2`,
`xcoo2csr`, `csrgeam2`).
### Known limitations[#](#known-limitations "Permalink to this heading")
The following operations are int32-only and raise `ValueError`
when called on a matrix with int64 indices:
* [`cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.spsolve()`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.spsolve.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.spsolve "cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.spsolve") – the underlying
  `cusolverSp<t>csrlsvqr` routine has no int64 overload.
* [`cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.spilu()`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.spilu.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.spilu "cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.spilu") – the underlying
  `cusparse<t>csrilu02` routine has no int64 overload.
* [`cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.spsolve_triangular()`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.spsolve_triangular.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.spsolve_triangular "cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.spsolve_triangular") on CUDA
  builds older than 12.0, where the dispatch falls back to
  `cusparse<t>csrsm2`. On CUDA 12.0+ it uses `cusparseSpSM`
  (Generic API), which supports int64.
## Conversion to/from SciPy sparse matrices[#](#conversion-to-from-scipy-sparse-matrices "Permalink to this heading")
`cupyx.scipy.sparse.*_matrix` and `scipy.sparse.*_matrix` are not implicitly convertible to each other.
That means, SciPy functions cannot take `cupyx.scipy.sparse.*_matrix` objects as inputs, and vice versa.
* To convert SciPy sparse matrices to CuPy, pass it to the constructor of each CuPy sparse matrix class.
* To convert CuPy sparse matrices to SciPy, use [`get`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.spmatrix.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.spmatrix.get "cupyx.scipy.sparse.spmatrix.get") method of each CuPy sparse matrix class.
Note that converting between CuPy and SciPy incurs data transfer between
the host (CPU) device and the GPU device, which is costly in terms of performance.
## Conversion to/from CuPy ndarrays[#](#conversion-to-from-cupy-ndarrays "Permalink to this heading")
* To convert CuPy ndarray to CuPy sparse matrices, pass it to the constructor of each CuPy sparse matrix class.
* To convert CuPy sparse matrices to CuPy ndarray, use `toarray` of each CuPy sparse matrix instance (e.g., [`cupyx.scipy.sparse.csr_matrix.toarray()`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.csr_matrix.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.csr_matrix.toarray "cupyx.scipy.sparse.csr_matrix.toarray")).
Converting between CuPy ndarray and CuPy sparse matrices does not incur data transfer; it is copied inside the GPU device.
## Contents[#](#contents "Permalink to this heading")
### Sparse matrix classes[#](#sparse-matrix-classes "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`coo_matrix`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.coo_matrix.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.coo_matrix "cupyx.scipy.sparse.coo_matrix")(arg1[, shape, dtype, copy]) | COOrdinate format sparse matrix. |
| [`csc_matrix`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.csc_matrix.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.csc_matrix "cupyx.scipy.sparse.csc_matrix")(arg1[, shape, dtype, copy]) | Compressed Sparse Column matrix. |
| [`csr_matrix`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.csr_matrix.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.csr_matrix "cupyx.scipy.sparse.csr_matrix")(arg1[, shape, dtype, copy]) | Compressed Sparse Row matrix. |
| [`dia_matrix`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.dia_matrix.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.dia_matrix "cupyx.scipy.sparse.dia_matrix")(arg1[, shape, dtype, copy]) | Sparse matrix with DIAgonal storage. |
| [`spmatrix`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.spmatrix.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.spmatrix "cupyx.scipy.sparse.spmatrix")([maxprint]) | Base class of all sparse matrixes. |
### Functions[#](#functions "Permalink to this heading")
Building sparse matrices:
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`eye`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.eye.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.eye "cupyx.scipy.sparse.eye")(m[, n, k, dtype, format]) | Creates a sparse matrix with ones on diagonal. |
| [`identity`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.identity.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.identity "cupyx.scipy.sparse.identity")(n[, dtype, format]) | Creates an identity matrix in sparse format. |
| [`kron`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.kron.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.kron "cupyx.scipy.sparse.kron")(A, B[, format]) | Kronecker product of sparse matrices A and B. |
| [`kronsum`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.kronsum.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.kronsum "cupyx.scipy.sparse.kronsum")(A, B[, format]) | Kronecker sum of sparse matrices A and B. |
| [`diags`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.diags.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.diags "cupyx.scipy.sparse.diags")(diagonals[, offsets, shape, format, dtype]) | Construct a sparse matrix from diagonals. |
| [`spdiags`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.spdiags.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.spdiags "cupyx.scipy.sparse.spdiags")(data, diags, m, n[, format]) | Creates a sparse matrix from diagonals. |
| [`tril`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.tril.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.tril "cupyx.scipy.sparse.tril")(A[, k, format]) | Returns the lower triangular portion of a matrix in sparse format |
| [`triu`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.triu.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.triu "cupyx.scipy.sparse.triu")(A[, k, format]) | Returns the upper triangular portion of a matrix in sparse format |
| [`bmat`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.bmat.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.bmat "cupyx.scipy.sparse.bmat")(blocks[, format, dtype]) | Builds a sparse matrix from sparse sub-blocks |
| [`hstack`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.hstack.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.hstack "cupyx.scipy.sparse.hstack")(blocks[, format, dtype]) | Stacks sparse matrices horizontally (column wise) |
| [`vstack`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.vstack.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.vstack "cupyx.scipy.sparse.vstack")(blocks[, format, dtype]) | Stacks sparse matrices vertically (row wise) |
| [`rand`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.rand.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.rand "cupyx.scipy.sparse.rand")(m, n[, density, format, dtype, ...]) | Generates a random sparse matrix. |
| [`random`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.random.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.random "cupyx.scipy.sparse.random")(m, n[, density, format, dtype, ...]) | Generates a random sparse matrix. |
Sparse matrix tools:
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`find`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.find.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.find "cupyx.scipy.sparse.find")(A) | Returns the indices and values of the nonzero elements of a matrix |
Identifying sparse matrices:
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`issparse`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.issparse.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.issparse "cupyx.scipy.sparse.issparse")(x) | Checks if a given matrix is a sparse matrix. |
| [`isspmatrix`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.isspmatrix.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.isspmatrix "cupyx.scipy.sparse.isspmatrix")(x) | Checks if a given matrix is a sparse matrix. |
| [`isspmatrix_csc`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.isspmatrix_csc.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.isspmatrix_csc "cupyx.scipy.sparse.isspmatrix_csc")(x) | Checks if a given matrix is of CSC format. |
| [`isspmatrix_csr`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.isspmatrix_csr.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.isspmatrix_csr "cupyx.scipy.sparse.isspmatrix_csr")(x) | Checks if a given matrix is of CSR format. |
| [`isspmatrix_coo`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.isspmatrix_coo.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.isspmatrix_coo "cupyx.scipy.sparse.isspmatrix_coo")(x) | Checks if a given matrix is of COO format. |
| [`isspmatrix_dia`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.isspmatrix_dia.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.isspmatrix_dia "cupyx.scipy.sparse.isspmatrix_dia")(x) | Checks if a given matrix is of DIA format. |

# Sparse linear algebra ([`cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg`](#module-cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg "cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg"))[#](#sparse-linear-algebra-cupyx-scipy-sparse-linalg "Permalink to this heading")
Hint
[SciPy API Reference: Sparse linear algebra (scipy.sparse.linalg)](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/sparse.linalg.html)
## Abstract linear operators[#](#abstract-linear-operators "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`LinearOperator`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.LinearOperator.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.LinearOperator "cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.LinearOperator")(shape, matvec[, rmatvec, ...]) | Common interface for performing matrix vector products |
| [`aslinearoperator`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.aslinearoperator.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.aslinearoperator "cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.aslinearoperator")(A) | Return A as a LinearOperator. |
## Matrix norms[#](#matrix-norms "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`norm`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.norm.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.norm "cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.norm")(x[, ord, axis]) | Norm of a cupy.scipy.spmatrix |
## Solving linear problems[#](#solving-linear-problems "Permalink to this heading")
Direct methods for linear equation systems:
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`spsolve`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.spsolve.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.spsolve "cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.spsolve")(A, b) | Solves a sparse linear system `A x = b` |
| [`spsolve_triangular`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.spsolve_triangular.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.spsolve_triangular "cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.spsolve_triangular")(A, b[, lower, ...]) | Solves a sparse triangular system `A x = b`. |
| [`factorized`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.factorized.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.factorized "cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.factorized")(A) | Return a function for solving a sparse linear system, with A pre-factorized. |
Iterative methods for linear equation systems:
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cg`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.cg.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.cg "cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.cg")(A, b[, x0, rtol, atol, maxiter, M, callback]) | Uses Conjugate Gradient iteration to solve `Ax = b`. |
| [`gmres`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.gmres.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.gmres "cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.gmres")(A, b[, x0, rtol, atol, restart, ...]) | Uses Generalized Minimal RESidual iteration to solve `Ax = b`. |
| [`cgs`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.cgs.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.cgs "cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.cgs")(A, b[, x0, rtol, atol, maxiter, M, callback]) | Use Conjugate Gradient Squared iteration to solve `Ax = b`. |
| [`minres`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.minres.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.minres "cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.minres")(A, b[, x0, shift, tol, maxiter, M, ...]) | Uses MINimum RESidual iteration to solve `Ax = b`. |
Iterative methods for least-squares problems:
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`lsqr`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.lsqr.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.lsqr "cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.lsqr")(A, b) | Solves linear system with QR decomposition. |
| [`lsmr`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.lsmr.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.lsmr "cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.lsmr")(A, b[, x0, damp, atol, btol, conlim, ...]) | Iterative solver for least-squares problems. |
## Matrix factorizations[#](#matrix-factorizations "Permalink to this heading")
Eigenvalue problems:
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`eigsh`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.eigsh.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.eigsh "cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.eigsh")(a[, k, which, v0, ncv, maxiter, tol, ...]) | Find `k` eigenvalues and eigenvectors of the real symmetric square matrix or complex Hermitian matrix `A`. |
| [`lobpcg`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.lobpcg.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.lobpcg "cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.lobpcg")(A, X[, B, M, Y, tol, maxiter, ...]) | Locally Optimal Block Preconditioned Conjugate Gradient Method (LOBPCG) |
Singular values problems:
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`svds`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.svds.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.svds "cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.svds")(a[, k, ncv, tol, which, maxiter, ...]) | Finds the largest `k` singular values/vectors for a sparse matrix. |
Complete or incomplete LU factorizations:
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`splu`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.splu.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.splu "cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.splu")(A[, permc\_spec, diag\_pivot\_thresh, ...]) | Computes the LU decomposition of a sparse square matrix. |
| [`spilu`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.spilu.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.spilu "cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.spilu")(A[, drop\_tol, fill\_factor, drop\_rule, ...]) | Computes the incomplete LU decomposition of a sparse square matrix. |
| [`SuperLU`](generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.SuperLU.html#cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.SuperLU "cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.SuperLU")(obj) |  |

# Compressed sparse graph routines ([`cupyx.scipy.sparse.csgraph`](#module-cupyx.scipy.sparse.csgraph "cupyx.scipy.sparse.csgraph"))[#](#compressed-sparse-graph-routines-cupyx-scipy-sparse-csgraph "Permalink to this heading")
Note
The `csgraph` module uses `pylibcugraph` as a backend.
You need to install pylibcugraph package <https://anaconda.org/rapidsai/pylibcugraph> from `rapidsai` Conda channel to use features listed on this page.
Note
Currently, the `csgraph` module is not supported on AMD ROCm platforms.
Hint
[SciPy API Reference: Compressed sparse graph routines (scipy.sparse.csgraph)](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/sparse.csgraph.html)

|  |  |
| --- | --- |

# Spatial algorithms and data structures ([`cupyx.scipy.spatial`](#module-cupyx.scipy.spatial "cupyx.scipy.spatial"))[#](#spatial-algorithms-and-data-structures-cupyx-scipy-spatial "Permalink to this heading")

[SciPy API Reference: Spatial (scipy.spatial)](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/spatial.html)
Note
The `spatial` module uses the `cuVS` library as a backend.
You need to install cuVS package <https://anaconda.org/rapidsai/cuvs> from `rapidsai` Conda channel to use features listed on this page.
Note
Currently, the `spatial` module is not supported on AMD ROCm platforms.

| [`Delaunay`](generated/cupyx.scipy.spatial.Delaunay.html#cupyx.scipy.spatial.Delaunay "cupyx.scipy.spatial.Delaunay")(points[, furthest\_site, incremental]) | Delaunay tessellation in 2 dimensions. |

# Distance computations ([`cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance`](#module-cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance "cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance"))[#](#distance-computations-cupyx-scipy-spatial-distance "Permalink to this heading")
Note
The `distance` module uses the `cuVS` library as a backend.
You need to install cuVS package <https://anaconda.org/rapidsai/cuvs> from `rapidsai` Conda channel to use features listed on this page.
Note
Currently, the `distance` module is not supported on AMD ROCm platforms.
Hint
[SciPy API Reference: Spatial distance routines (scipy.spatial.distance)](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/spatial.distance.html)
## Distance matrix computations[#](#distance-matrix-computations "Permalink to this heading")
Distance matrix computation from a collection of raw observation vectors stored in a rectangular array.
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`pdist`](generated/cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.pdist.html#cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.pdist "cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.pdist")(X[, metric, out]) | Compute distance between observations in n-dimensional space. |
| [`cdist`](generated/cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.cdist.html#cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.cdist "cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.cdist")(XA, XB[, metric, out]) | Compute distance between each pair of the two collections of inputs. |
| [`distance_matrix`](generated/cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.distance_matrix.html#cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.distance_matrix "cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.distance_matrix")(x, y[, p]) | Compute the distance matrix. |
## Distance functions[#](#distance-functions "Permalink to this heading")
Distance functions between two numeric vectors u and v. Computing distances over a large collection of vectors is inefficient for these functions. Use cdist for this purpose.
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`minkowski`](generated/cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.minkowski.html#cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.minkowski "cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.minkowski")(u, v, p) | Compute the Minkowski distance between two 1-D arrays. |
| [`canberra`](generated/cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.canberra.html#cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.canberra "cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.canberra")(u, v) | Compute the Canberra distance between two 1-D arrays. |
| [`chebyshev`](generated/cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.chebyshev.html#cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.chebyshev "cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.chebyshev")(u, v) | Compute the Chebyshev distance between two 1-D arrays. |
| [`cityblock`](generated/cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.cityblock.html#cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.cityblock "cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.cityblock")(u, v) | Compute the City Block (Manhattan) distance between two 1-D arrays. |
| [`correlation`](generated/cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.correlation.html#cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.correlation "cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.correlation")(u, v) | Compute the correlation distance between two 1-D arrays. |
| [`cosine`](generated/cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.cosine.html#cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.cosine "cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.cosine")(u, v) | Compute the Cosine distance between two 1-D arrays. |
| [`hamming`](generated/cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.hamming.html#cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.hamming "cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.hamming")(u, v) | Compute the Hamming distance between two 1-D arrays. |
| [`euclidean`](generated/cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.euclidean.html#cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.euclidean "cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.euclidean")(u, v) | Compute the Euclidean distance between two 1-D arrays. |
| [`jensenshannon`](generated/cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.jensenshannon.html#cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.jensenshannon "cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.jensenshannon")(u, v) | Compute the Jensen-Shannon distance between two 1-D arrays. |
| [`russellrao`](generated/cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.russellrao.html#cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.russellrao "cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.russellrao")(u, v) | Compute the Russell-Rao distance between two 1-D arrays. |
| [`sqeuclidean`](generated/cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.sqeuclidean.html#cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.sqeuclidean "cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.sqeuclidean")(u, v) | Compute the squared Euclidean distance between two 1-D arrays. |
| [`hellinger`](generated/cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.hellinger.html#cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.hellinger "cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.hellinger")(u, v) | Compute the Hellinger distance between two 1-D arrays. |
| [`kl_divergence`](generated/cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.kl_divergence.html#cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.kl_divergence "cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.kl_divergence")(u, v) | Compute the Kullback-Leibler divergence between two 1-D arrays. |
[previous

[next
cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.pdist](generated/cupyx.scipy.spatial.distance.pdist.html "next page")

* [Distance functions](#distance-functions)

| --- | --- |
| [`j0`](generated/cupyx.scipy.special.j0.html#cupyx.scipy.special.j0 "cupyx.scipy.special.j0") | Bessel function of the first kind of order 0. |

| [`k1`](generated/cupyx.scipy.special.k1.html#cupyx.scipy.special.k1 "cupyx.scipy.special.k1") | Modified Bessel function of the second kind of order 1. |
| [`k1e`](generated/cupyx.scipy.special.k1e.html#cupyx.scipy.special.k1e "cupyx.scipy.special.k1e") | Exponentially scaled modified Bessel function K of order 1 |
| [`y0`](generated/cupyx.scipy.special.y0.html#cupyx.scipy.special.y0 "cupyx.scipy.special.y0") | Bessel function of the second kind of order 0. |
| [`y1`](generated/cupyx.scipy.special.y1.html#cupyx.scipy.special.y1 "cupyx.scipy.special.y1") | Bessel function of the second kind of order 1. |
| [`yn`](generated/cupyx.scipy.special.yn.html#cupyx.scipy.special.yn "cupyx.scipy.special.yn") | Bessel function of the second kind of order n. |
| [`i0`](generated/cupyx.scipy.special.i0.html#cupyx.scipy.special.i0 "cupyx.scipy.special.i0") | Modified Bessel function of order 0. |
| [`i0e`](generated/cupyx.scipy.special.i0e.html#cupyx.scipy.special.i0e "cupyx.scipy.special.i0e") | Exponentially scaled modified Bessel function of order 0. |
| [`i1`](generated/cupyx.scipy.special.i1.html#cupyx.scipy.special.i1 "cupyx.scipy.special.i1") | Modified Bessel function of order 1. |
| [`i1e`](generated/cupyx.scipy.special.i1e.html#cupyx.scipy.special.i1e "cupyx.scipy.special.i1e") | Exponentially scaled modified Bessel function of order 1. |
| [`spherical_yn`](generated/cupyx.scipy.special.spherical_yn.html#cupyx.scipy.special.spherical_yn "cupyx.scipy.special.spherical_yn")(n, z[, derivative]) | Spherical Bessel function of the second kind or its derivative. |
| [`wright_bessel`](generated/cupyx.scipy.special.wright_bessel.html#cupyx.scipy.special.wright_bessel "cupyx.scipy.special.wright_bessel") | Wright's generalized Bessel function |

See also

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`bdtr`](generated/cupyx.scipy.special.bdtr.html#cupyx.scipy.special.bdtr "cupyx.scipy.special.bdtr") | Binomial distribution cumulative distribution function. |

| [`btdtr`](generated/cupyx.scipy.special.btdtr.html#cupyx.scipy.special.btdtr "cupyx.scipy.special.btdtr")(a, b, x[, out]) |  |

| [`pdtr`](generated/cupyx.scipy.special.pdtr.html#cupyx.scipy.special.pdtr "cupyx.scipy.special.pdtr") | Poisson cumulative distribution function. |

| [`chdtr`](generated/cupyx.scipy.special.chdtr.html#cupyx.scipy.special.chdtr "cupyx.scipy.special.chdtr") | Chi-square cumulative distribution function. |

# CuPy-specific functions[#](#cupy-specific-functions "Permalink to this heading")
CuPy-specific functions are placed under `cupyx` namespace.
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupyx.rsqrt`](generated/cupyx.rsqrt.html#cupyx.rsqrt "cupyx.rsqrt") | Returns the reciprocal square root. |
| [`cupyx.scatter_add`](generated/cupyx.scatter_add.html#cupyx.scatter_add "cupyx.scatter_add")(a, slices, value) | Adds given values to specified elements of an array. |
| [`cupyx.scatter_max`](generated/cupyx.scatter_max.html#cupyx.scatter_max "cupyx.scatter_max")(a, slices, value) | Stores a maximum value of elements specified by indices to an array. |
| [`cupyx.scatter_min`](generated/cupyx.scatter_min.html#cupyx.scatter_min "cupyx.scatter_min")(a, slices, value) | Stores a minimum value of elements specified by indices to an array. |
| [`cupyx.empty_pinned`](generated/cupyx.empty_pinned.html#cupyx.empty_pinned "cupyx.empty_pinned")(shape[, dtype, order]) | Returns a new, uninitialized NumPy array with the given shape and dtype. |
| [`cupyx.empty_like_pinned`](generated/cupyx.empty_like_pinned.html#cupyx.empty_like_pinned "cupyx.empty_like_pinned")(a[, dtype, order, ...]) | Returns a new, uninitialized NumPy array with the same shape and dtype as those of the given array. |
| [`cupyx.zeros_pinned`](generated/cupyx.zeros_pinned.html#cupyx.zeros_pinned "cupyx.zeros_pinned")(shape[, dtype, order]) | Returns a new, zero-initialized NumPy array with the given shape and dtype. |
| [`cupyx.zeros_like_pinned`](generated/cupyx.zeros_like_pinned.html#cupyx.zeros_like_pinned "cupyx.zeros_like_pinned")(a[, dtype, order, ...]) | Returns a new, zero-initialized NumPy array with the same shape and dtype as those of the given array. |
## non-SciPy compat Signal API[#](#non-scipy-compat-signal-api "Permalink to this heading")
The functions under cupyx.signal are non-SciPy compat signal API ported from cuSignal
through the courtesy of Nvidia cuSignal team.
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupyx.signal.channelize_poly`](generated/cupyx.signal.channelize_poly.html#cupyx.signal.channelize_poly "cupyx.signal.channelize_poly")(x, h, n\_chans) | Polyphase channelize signal into n channels |
| [`cupyx.signal.convolve1d3o`](generated/cupyx.signal.convolve1d3o.html#cupyx.signal.convolve1d3o "cupyx.signal.convolve1d3o")(in1, in2[, mode, ...]) | Convolve a 1-dimensional array with a 3rd order filter. |
| [`cupyx.signal.pulse_compression`](generated/cupyx.signal.pulse_compression.html#cupyx.signal.pulse_compression "cupyx.signal.pulse_compression")(x, template) | Pulse Compression is used to increase the range resolution and SNR by performing matched filtering of the transmitted pulse (template) with the received signal (x) |
| [`cupyx.signal.pulse_doppler`](generated/cupyx.signal.pulse_doppler.html#cupyx.signal.pulse_doppler "cupyx.signal.pulse_doppler")(x[, window, nfft]) | Pulse doppler processing yields a range/doppler data matrix that represents moving target data that's separated from clutter. |
| [`cupyx.signal.cfar_alpha`](generated/cupyx.signal.cfar_alpha.html#cupyx.signal.cfar_alpha "cupyx.signal.cfar_alpha")(pfa, N) | Computes the value of alpha corresponding to a given probability of false alarm and number of reference cells N. |
| [`cupyx.signal.ca_cfar`](generated/cupyx.signal.ca_cfar.html#cupyx.signal.ca_cfar "cupyx.signal.ca_cfar")(array, guard\_cells, ...) | Computes the cell-averaged constant false alarm rate (CA CFAR) detector threshold and returns for a given array. |
| [`cupyx.signal.freq_shift`](generated/cupyx.signal.freq_shift.html#cupyx.signal.freq_shift "cupyx.signal.freq_shift")(x, freq, fs) | Frequency shift signal by freq at fs sample rate |
## Profiling utilities[#](#profiling-utilities "Permalink to this heading")

| --- | --- |

## DLPack utilities[#](#dlpack-utilities "Permalink to this heading")
Below are helper functions for creating a [`cupy.ndarray`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray") from either a DLPack tensor
or any object supporting the DLPack data exchange protocol.
For further detail see [DLPack data exchange protocol](../user_guide/interoperability.html#dlpack).
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupy.from_dlpack`](generated/cupy.from_dlpack.html#cupy.from_dlpack "cupy.from_dlpack")(array, \*[, device, copy]) | Zero-copy conversion between array objects compliant with the DLPack data exchange protocol. |

# cupyx.scatter\_add[#](#cupyx-scatter-add "Permalink to this heading")
cupyx.scatter\_add(*a*, *slices*, *value*)[[source]](https://github.com/cupy/cupy/blob/v14.1.1/cupyx/_scatter.py#L4)[#](#cupyx.scatter_add "Permalink to this definition")
:   Adds given values to specified elements of an array.
    It adds `value` to the specified elements of `a`.
    If all of the indices target different locations, the operation of
    [`scatter_add()`](#cupyx.scatter_add "cupyx.scatter_add") is equivalent to `a[slices] = a[slices] + value`.
    If there are multiple elements targeting the same location,
    [`scatter_add()`](#cupyx.scatter_add "cupyx.scatter_add") uses all of these values for addition. On the other
    hand, `a[slices] = a[slices] + value` only adds the contribution from one
    of the indices targeting the same location.
    Note that just like an array indexing, negative indices are interpreted as
    counting from the end of an array.
    Also note that [`scatter_add()`](#cupyx.scatter_add "cupyx.scatter_add") behaves identically
    to `numpy.add.at()`.
    Example
    ```
    >>> import cupy
    >>> import cupyx
    >>> a = cupy.zeros((6,), dtype=cupy.float32)
    >>> i = cupy.array([1, 0, 1])
    >>> v = cupy.array([1., 1., 1.])
    >>> cupyx.scatter_add(a, i, v);
    >>> a
    array([1., 2., 0., 0., 0., 0.], dtype=float32)
    ```
    Parameters:
    :   * **a** ([*ndarray*](cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray")) – An array that gets added.
        * **slices** – It is integer, slices, ellipsis, numpy.newaxis,
          integer array-like, boolean array-like or tuple of them.
          It works for slices used for
          [`cupy.ndarray.__getitem__()`](cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray.__getitem__ "cupy.ndarray.__getitem__") and
          [`cupy.ndarray.__setitem__()`](cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray.__setitem__ "cupy.ndarray.__setitem__").
        * **v** (*array-like*) – Values to increment `a` at referenced locations.
    Note
    It only supports types that are supported by CUDA’s atomicAdd when
    an integer array is included in `slices`.
    The supported types are `numpy.float32`, `numpy.int32`,
    `numpy.uint32`, `numpy.uint64` and `numpy.ulonglong`.
    Note
    [`scatter_add()`](#cupyx.scatter_add "cupyx.scatter_add") does not raise an error when indices exceed size of
    axes. Instead, it wraps indices.
    See also
    [`numpy.ufunc.at()`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ufunc.at.html#numpy.ufunc.at "(in NumPy v2.4)").
[previous
cupyx.rsqrt](cupyx.rsqrt.html "previous page")
[next
cupyx.scatter\_max](cupyx.scatter_max.html "next page")
On this page
* [`scatter_add()`](#cupyx.scatter_add)

<!-- Source: https://docs.cupy.dev/en/stable/reference/generated/cupyx.scatter_max.html -->
# cupyx.scatter\_max[#](#cupyx-scatter-max "Permalink to this heading")
cupyx.scatter\_max(*a*, *slices*, *value*)[[source]](https://github.com/cupy/cupy/blob/v14.1.1/cupyx/_scatter.py#L57)[#](#cupyx.scatter_max "Permalink to this definition")
:   Stores a maximum value of elements specified by indices to an array.
    It stores the maximum value of elements in `value` array indexed by
    `slices` to `a`. If all of the indices target different locations,
    the operation of [`scatter_max()`](#cupyx.scatter_max "cupyx.scatter_max") is equivalent to
    `a[slices] = cupy.maximum(a[slices], value)`.
    If there are multiple elements targeting the same location,
    [`scatter_max()`](#cupyx.scatter_max "cupyx.scatter_max") stores the maximum of all of these values to the given
    index of `a`, the initial element of `a` is also taken in account.
    Note that just like an array indexing, negative indices are interpreted as
    counting from the end of an array.
    Also note that [`scatter_max()`](#cupyx.scatter_max "cupyx.scatter_max") behaves identically
    to `numpy.maximum.at()`.
    Example
    ```
    >>> import numpy
    >>> import cupy
    >>> a = cupy.zeros((6,), dtype=numpy.float32)
    >>> i = cupy.array([1, 0, 1, 2])
    >>> v = cupy.array([1., 2., 3., -1.])
    >>> cupyx.scatter_max(a, i, v);
    >>> a
    array([2., 3., 0., 0., 0., 0.], dtype=float32)
    ```
    Parameters:
    :   * **a** ([*ndarray*](cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray")) – An array to store the results.
        * **slices** – It is integer, slices, ellipsis, numpy.newaxis,
          integer array-like, boolean array-like or tuple of them.
          It works for slices used for
          [`cupy.ndarray.__getitem__()`](cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray.__getitem__ "cupy.ndarray.__getitem__") and
          [`cupy.ndarray.__setitem__()`](cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray.__setitem__ "cupy.ndarray.__setitem__").
        * **v** (*array-like*) – An array used for reference.

<!-- Source: https://docs.cupy.dev/en/stable/reference/generated/cupyx.scatter_min.html -->
# cupyx.scatter\_min[#](#cupyx-scatter-min "Permalink to this heading")
cupyx.scatter\_min(*a*, *slices*, *value*)[[source]](https://github.com/cupy/cupy/blob/v14.1.1/cupyx/_scatter.py#L97)[#](#cupyx.scatter_min "Permalink to this definition")
:   Stores a minimum value of elements specified by indices to an array.
    It stores the minimum value of elements in `value` array indexed by
    `slices` to `a`. If all of the indices target different locations,
    the operation of [`scatter_min()`](#cupyx.scatter_min "cupyx.scatter_min") is equivalent to
    `a[slices] = cupy.minimum(a[slices], value)`.
    If there are multiple elements targeting the same location,
    [`scatter_min()`](#cupyx.scatter_min "cupyx.scatter_min") stores the minimum of all of these values to the given
    index of `a`, the initial element of `a` is also taken in account.
    Note that just like an array indexing, negative indices are interpreted as
    counting from the end of an array.
    Also note that [`scatter_min()`](#cupyx.scatter_min "cupyx.scatter_min") behaves identically
    to `numpy.minimum.at()`.
    Example
    ```
    >>> import numpy
    >>> import cupy
    >>> a = cupy.zeros((6,), dtype=numpy.float32)
    >>> i = cupy.array([1, 0, 1, 2])
    >>> v = cupy.array([1., 2., 3., -1.])
    >>> cupyx.scatter_min(a, i, v);
    >>> a
    array([ 0.,  0., -1.,  0.,  0.,  0.], dtype=float32)
    ```
    Parameters:
    :   * **a** ([*ndarray*](cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray")) – An array to store the results.
        * **slices** – It is integer, slices, ellipsis, numpy.newaxis,
          integer array-like, boolean array-like or tuple of them.
          It works for slices used for
          [`cupy.ndarray.__getitem__()`](cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray.__getitem__ "cupy.ndarray.__getitem__") and
          [`cupy.ndarray.__setitem__()`](cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray.__setitem__ "cupy.ndarray.__setitem__").
        * **v** (*array-like*) – An array used for reference.
[previous
cupyx.scatter\_max](cupyx.scatter_max.html "previous page")
[next
cupyx.empty\_pinned](cupyx.empty_pinned.html "next page")
On this page
* [`scatter_min()`](#cupyx.scatter_min)
---
<!-- Source: https://docs.cupy.dev/en/stable/reference/generated/cupyx.empty_pinned.html -->
# cupyx.empty\_pinned[#](#cupyx-empty-pinned "Permalink to this heading")
cupyx.empty\_pinned(*shape*, *dtype=<class 'float'>*, *order='C'*)[[source]](https://github.com/cupy/cupy/blob/v14.1.1/cupyx/_pinned_array.py#L20)[#](#cupyx.empty_pinned "Permalink to this definition")
:   Returns a new, uninitialized NumPy array with the given shape
    and dtype.
    This is a convenience function which is just [`numpy.empty()`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.empty.html#numpy.empty "(in NumPy v2.4)"),
    except that the underlying memory is pinned/pagelocked.
    Parameters:
    :   * **shape** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *ints*) – Dimensionalities of the array.
        * **dtype** – Data type specifier.
        * **order** (*{'C'**,* *'F'}*) – Row-major (C-style) or column-major
          (Fortran-style) order.
    Returns:
    :   A new array with elements not initialized.
    Return type:
    :   [numpy.ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")
    See also
    [`numpy.empty()`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.empty.html#numpy.empty "(in NumPy v2.4)")
[previous
cupyx.scatter\_min](cupyx.scatter_min.html "previous page")
[next
cupyx.empty\_like\_pinned](cupyx.empty_like_pinned.html "next page")
On this page
* [`empty_pinned()`](#cupyx.empty_pinned)
---
<!-- Source: https://docs.cupy.dev/en/stable/reference/generated/cupyx.empty_like_pinned.html -->
# cupyx.empty\_like\_pinned[#](#cupyx-empty-like-pinned "Permalink to this heading")
cupyx.empty\_like\_pinned(*a*, *dtype=None*, *order='K'*, *subok=None*, *shape=None*)[[source]](https://github.com/cupy/cupy/blob/v14.1.1/cupyx/_pinned_array.py#L46)[#](#cupyx.empty_like_pinned "Permalink to this definition")
:   Returns a new, uninitialized NumPy array with the same shape and dtype
    as those of the given array.
    This is a convenience function which is just [`numpy.empty_like()`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.empty_like.html#numpy.empty_like "(in NumPy v2.4)"),
    except that the underlying memory is pinned/pagelocked.
    This function currently does not support `subok` option.
    Parameters:
    :   * **a** ([*numpy.ndarray*](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)") *or* [*cupy.ndarray*](cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray")) – Base array.
        * **dtype** – Data type specifier. The data type of `a` is used by default.
        * **order** (*{'C'**,* *'F'**,* *'A'**, or* *'K'}*) – Overrides the memory layout of the
          result. `'C'` means C-order, `'F'` means F-order, `'A'` means
          `'F'` if `a` is Fortran contiguous, `'C'` otherwise.
          `'K'` means match the layout of `a` as closely as possible.
        * **subok** – Not supported yet, must be None.
        * **shape** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *ints*) – Overrides the shape of the result. If
          `order='K'` and the number of dimensions is unchanged, will try
          to keep order, otherwise, `order='C'` is implied.
    Returns:
    :   A new array with same shape and dtype of `a` with
        elements not initialized.
    Return type:
    :   [numpy.ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")
    See also
    [`numpy.empty_like()`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.empty_like.html#numpy.empty_like "(in NumPy v2.4)")
[previous
cupyx.empty\_pinned](cupyx.empty_pinned.html "previous page")
[next
cupyx.zeros\_pinned](cupyx.zeros_pinned.html "next page")
On this page
* [`empty_like_pinned()`](#cupyx.empty_like_pinned)
---
<!-- Source: https://docs.cupy.dev/en/stable/reference/generated/cupyx.zeros_pinned.html -->
# cupyx.zeros\_pinned[#](#cupyx-zeros-pinned "Permalink to this heading")
cupyx.zeros\_pinned(*shape*, *dtype=<class 'float'>*, *order='C'*)[[source]](https://github.com/cupy/cupy/blob/v14.1.1/cupyx/_pinned_array.py#L90)[#](#cupyx.zeros_pinned "Permalink to this definition")
:   Returns a new, zero-initialized NumPy array with the given shape
    and dtype.
    This is a convenience function which is just [`numpy.zeros()`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.zeros.html#numpy.zeros "(in NumPy v2.4)"),
    except that the underlying memory is pinned/pagelocked.
    Parameters:
    :   * **shape** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *ints*) – Dimensionalities of the array.
        * **dtype** – Data type specifier.
        * **order** (*{'C'**,* *'F'}*) – Row-major (C-style) or column-major
          (Fortran-style) order.
    Returns:
    :   An array filled with zeros.
    Return type:
    :   [numpy.ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")
    See also
    [`numpy.zeros()`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.zeros.html#numpy.zeros "(in NumPy v2.4)")
[previous
cupyx.empty\_like\_pinned](cupyx.empty_like_pinned.html "previous page")

# cupyx.zeros\_like\_pinned[#](#cupyx-zeros-like-pinned "Permalink to this heading")
cupyx.zeros\_like\_pinned(*a*, *dtype=None*, *order='K'*, *subok=None*, *shape=None*)[[source]](https://github.com/cupy/cupy/blob/v14.1.1/cupyx/_pinned_array.py#L114)[#](#cupyx.zeros_like_pinned "Permalink to this definition")
:   Returns a new, zero-initialized NumPy array with the same shape and dtype
    as those of the given array.
    This is a convenience function which is just [`numpy.zeros_like()`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.zeros_like.html#numpy.zeros_like "(in NumPy v2.4)"),
    except that the underlying memory is pinned/pagelocked.
    This function currently does not support `subok` option.
    Parameters:
    :   * **a** ([*numpy.ndarray*](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)") *or* [*cupy.ndarray*](cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray")) – Base array.
        * **dtype** – Data type specifier. The dtype of `a` is used by default.
        * **order** (*{'C'**,* *'F'**,* *'A'**, or* *'K'}*) – Overrides the memory layout of the
          result. `'C'` means C-order, `'F'` means F-order, `'A'` means
          `'F'` if `a` is Fortran contiguous, `'C'` otherwise.
          `'K'` means match the layout of `a` as closely as possible.
        * **subok** – Not supported yet, must be None.
        * **shape** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *ints*) – Overrides the shape of the result. If
          `order='K'` and the number of dimensions is unchanged, will try
          to keep order, otherwise, `order='C'` is implied.
    Returns:
    :   An array filled with zeros.
    Return type:
    :   [numpy.ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")
    See also
    [`numpy.zeros_like()`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.zeros_like.html#numpy.zeros_like "(in NumPy v2.4)")

# CuPy-specific functions[#](#cupy-specific-functions "Permalink to this heading")
CuPy-specific functions are placed under `cupyx` namespace.
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupyx.rsqrt`](generated/cupyx.rsqrt.html#cupyx.rsqrt "cupyx.rsqrt") | Returns the reciprocal square root. |
| [`cupyx.scatter_add`](generated/cupyx.scatter_add.html#cupyx.scatter_add "cupyx.scatter_add")(a, slices, value) | Adds given values to specified elements of an array. |
| [`cupyx.scatter_max`](generated/cupyx.scatter_max.html#cupyx.scatter_max "cupyx.scatter_max")(a, slices, value) | Stores a maximum value of elements specified by indices to an array. |
| [`cupyx.scatter_min`](generated/cupyx.scatter_min.html#cupyx.scatter_min "cupyx.scatter_min")(a, slices, value) | Stores a minimum value of elements specified by indices to an array. |
| [`cupyx.empty_pinned`](generated/cupyx.empty_pinned.html#cupyx.empty_pinned "cupyx.empty_pinned")(shape[, dtype, order]) | Returns a new, uninitialized NumPy array with the given shape and dtype. |
| [`cupyx.empty_like_pinned`](generated/cupyx.empty_like_pinned.html#cupyx.empty_like_pinned "cupyx.empty_like_pinned")(a[, dtype, order, ...]) | Returns a new, uninitialized NumPy array with the same shape and dtype as those of the given array. |
| [`cupyx.zeros_pinned`](generated/cupyx.zeros_pinned.html#cupyx.zeros_pinned "cupyx.zeros_pinned")(shape[, dtype, order]) | Returns a new, zero-initialized NumPy array with the given shape and dtype. |
| [`cupyx.zeros_like_pinned`](generated/cupyx.zeros_like_pinned.html#cupyx.zeros_like_pinned "cupyx.zeros_like_pinned")(a[, dtype, order, ...]) | Returns a new, zero-initialized NumPy array with the same shape and dtype as those of the given array. |
## non-SciPy compat Signal API[#](#non-scipy-compat-signal-api "Permalink to this heading")
The functions under cupyx.signal are non-SciPy compat signal API ported from cuSignal
through the courtesy of Nvidia cuSignal team.
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupyx.signal.channelize_poly`](generated/cupyx.signal.channelize_poly.html#cupyx.signal.channelize_poly "cupyx.signal.channelize_poly")(x, h, n\_chans) | Polyphase channelize signal into n channels |
| [`cupyx.signal.convolve1d3o`](generated/cupyx.signal.convolve1d3o.html#cupyx.signal.convolve1d3o "cupyx.signal.convolve1d3o")(in1, in2[, mode, ...]) | Convolve a 1-dimensional array with a 3rd order filter. |
| [`cupyx.signal.pulse_compression`](generated/cupyx.signal.pulse_compression.html#cupyx.signal.pulse_compression "cupyx.signal.pulse_compression")(x, template) | Pulse Compression is used to increase the range resolution and SNR by performing matched filtering of the transmitted pulse (template) with the received signal (x) |
| [`cupyx.signal.pulse_doppler`](generated/cupyx.signal.pulse_doppler.html#cupyx.signal.pulse_doppler "cupyx.signal.pulse_doppler")(x[, window, nfft]) | Pulse doppler processing yields a range/doppler data matrix that represents moving target data that's separated from clutter. |
| [`cupyx.signal.cfar_alpha`](generated/cupyx.signal.cfar_alpha.html#cupyx.signal.cfar_alpha "cupyx.signal.cfar_alpha")(pfa, N) | Computes the value of alpha corresponding to a given probability of false alarm and number of reference cells N. |
| [`cupyx.signal.ca_cfar`](generated/cupyx.signal.ca_cfar.html#cupyx.signal.ca_cfar "cupyx.signal.ca_cfar")(array, guard\_cells, ...) | Computes the cell-averaged constant false alarm rate (CA CFAR) detector threshold and returns for a given array. |
| [`cupyx.signal.freq_shift`](generated/cupyx.signal.freq_shift.html#cupyx.signal.freq_shift "cupyx.signal.freq_shift")(x, freq, fs) | Frequency shift signal by freq at fs sample rate |
## Profiling utilities[#](#profiling-utilities "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupyx.profiler.benchmark`](generated/cupyx.profiler.benchmark.html#cupyx.profiler.benchmark "cupyx.profiler.benchmark")(func[, args, ...]) | Timing utility for measuring time spent by both CPU and GPU. |
| [`cupyx.profiler.time_range`](generated/cupyx.profiler.time_range.html#cupyx.profiler.time_range "cupyx.profiler.time_range")([message, ...]) | Mark function calls with ranges using NVTX/rocTX. |
| [`cupyx.profiler.profile`](generated/cupyx.profiler.profile.html#cupyx.profiler.profile "cupyx.profiler.profile")() | Enable CUDA profiling during with statement. |
## DLPack utilities[#](#dlpack-utilities "Permalink to this heading")
Below are helper functions for creating a [`cupy.ndarray`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray") from either a DLPack tensor
or any object supporting the DLPack data exchange protocol.
For further detail see [DLPack data exchange protocol](../user_guide/interoperability.html#dlpack).
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupy.from_dlpack`](generated/cupy.from_dlpack.html#cupy.from_dlpack "cupy.from_dlpack")(array, \*[, device, copy]) | Zero-copy conversion between array objects compliant with the DLPack data exchange protocol. |
## Automatic Kernel Parameters Optimizations ([`cupyx.optimizing`](#module-cupyx.optimizing "cupyx.optimizing"))[#](#module-cupyx.optimizing "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupyx.optimizing.optimize`](generated/cupyx.optimizing.optimize.html#cupyx.optimizing.optimize "cupyx.optimizing.optimize")(\*[, key, path, ...]) | Context manager that optimizes kernel launch parameters. |

CuPy-specific functions are placed under `cupyx` namespace.
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupyx.rsqrt`](generated/cupyx.rsqrt.html#cupyx.rsqrt "cupyx.rsqrt") | Returns the reciprocal square root. |
| [`cupyx.scatter_add`](generated/cupyx.scatter_add.html#cupyx.scatter_add "cupyx.scatter_add")(a, slices, value) | Adds given values to specified elements of an array. |
| [`cupyx.scatter_max`](generated/cupyx.scatter_max.html#cupyx.scatter_max "cupyx.scatter_max")(a, slices, value) | Stores a maximum value of elements specified by indices to an array. |
| [`cupyx.scatter_min`](generated/cupyx.scatter_min.html#cupyx.scatter_min "cupyx.scatter_min")(a, slices, value) | Stores a minimum value of elements specified by indices to an array. |
| [`cupyx.empty_pinned`](generated/cupyx.empty_pinned.html#cupyx.empty_pinned "cupyx.empty_pinned")(shape[, dtype, order]) | Returns a new, uninitialized NumPy array with the given shape and dtype. |
| [`cupyx.empty_like_pinned`](generated/cupyx.empty_like_pinned.html#cupyx.empty_like_pinned "cupyx.empty_like_pinned")(a[, dtype, order, ...]) | Returns a new, uninitialized NumPy array with the same shape and dtype as those of the given array. |
| [`cupyx.zeros_pinned`](generated/cupyx.zeros_pinned.html#cupyx.zeros_pinned "cupyx.zeros_pinned")(shape[, dtype, order]) | Returns a new, zero-initialized NumPy array with the given shape and dtype. |
| [`cupyx.zeros_like_pinned`](generated/cupyx.zeros_like_pinned.html#cupyx.zeros_like_pinned "cupyx.zeros_like_pinned")(a[, dtype, order, ...]) | Returns a new, zero-initialized NumPy array with the same shape and dtype as those of the given array. |
## non-SciPy compat Signal API[#](#non-scipy-compat-signal-api "Permalink to this heading")
The functions under cupyx.signal are non-SciPy compat signal API ported from cuSignal
through the courtesy of Nvidia cuSignal team.
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupyx.signal.channelize_poly`](generated/cupyx.signal.channelize_poly.html#cupyx.signal.channelize_poly "cupyx.signal.channelize_poly")(x, h, n\_chans) | Polyphase channelize signal into n channels |
| [`cupyx.signal.convolve1d3o`](generated/cupyx.signal.convolve1d3o.html#cupyx.signal.convolve1d3o "cupyx.signal.convolve1d3o")(in1, in2[, mode, ...]) | Convolve a 1-dimensional array with a 3rd order filter. |
| [`cupyx.signal.pulse_compression`](generated/cupyx.signal.pulse_compression.html#cupyx.signal.pulse_compression "cupyx.signal.pulse_compression")(x, template) | Pulse Compression is used to increase the range resolution and SNR by performing matched filtering of the transmitted pulse (template) with the received signal (x) |
| [`cupyx.signal.pulse_doppler`](generated/cupyx.signal.pulse_doppler.html#cupyx.signal.pulse_doppler "cupyx.signal.pulse_doppler")(x[, window, nfft]) | Pulse doppler processing yields a range/doppler data matrix that represents moving target data that's separated from clutter. |
| [`cupyx.signal.cfar_alpha`](generated/cupyx.signal.cfar_alpha.html#cupyx.signal.cfar_alpha "cupyx.signal.cfar_alpha")(pfa, N) | Computes the value of alpha corresponding to a given probability of false alarm and number of reference cells N. |
| [`cupyx.signal.ca_cfar`](generated/cupyx.signal.ca_cfar.html#cupyx.signal.ca_cfar "cupyx.signal.ca_cfar")(array, guard\_cells, ...) | Computes the cell-averaged constant false alarm rate (CA CFAR) detector threshold and returns for a given array. |
| [`cupyx.signal.freq_shift`](generated/cupyx.signal.freq_shift.html#cupyx.signal.freq_shift "cupyx.signal.freq_shift")(x, freq, fs) | Frequency shift signal by freq at fs sample rate |
## Profiling utilities[#](#profiling-utilities "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupyx.profiler.benchmark`](generated/cupyx.profiler.benchmark.html#cupyx.profiler.benchmark "cupyx.profiler.benchmark")(func[, args, ...]) | Timing utility for measuring time spent by both CPU and GPU. |
| [`cupyx.profiler.time_range`](generated/cupyx.profiler.time_range.html#cupyx.profiler.time_range "cupyx.profiler.time_range")([message, ...]) | Mark function calls with ranges using NVTX/rocTX. |
| [`cupyx.profiler.profile`](generated/cupyx.profiler.profile.html#cupyx.profiler.profile "cupyx.profiler.profile")() | Enable CUDA profiling during with statement. |
## DLPack utilities[#](#dlpack-utilities "Permalink to this heading")
Below are helper functions for creating a [`cupy.ndarray`](generated/cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray") from either a DLPack tensor
or any object supporting the DLPack data exchange protocol.
For further detail see [DLPack data exchange protocol](../user_guide/interoperability.html#dlpack).
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupy.from_dlpack`](generated/cupy.from_dlpack.html#cupy.from_dlpack "cupy.from_dlpack")(array, \*[, device, copy]) | Zero-copy conversion between array objects compliant with the DLPack data exchange protocol. |

| --- | --- |

# Low-level CUDA support[#](#low-level-cuda-support "Permalink to this heading")
## Device management[#](#device-management "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupy.is_available`](generated/cupy.is_available.html#cupy.is_available "cupy.is_available")() | Return True if GPU execution is available. |
| [`cupy.cuda.Device`](generated/cupy.cuda.Device.html#cupy.cuda.Device "cupy.cuda.Device")([device]) | Object that represents a CUDA device. |
## Memory management[#](#memory-management "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupy.get_default_memory_pool`](generated/cupy.get_default_memory_pool.html#cupy.get_default_memory_pool "cupy.get_default_memory_pool")() | Returns CuPy default memory pool for GPU memory. |
| [`cupy.get_default_pinned_memory_pool`](generated/cupy.get_default_pinned_memory_pool.html#cupy.get_default_pinned_memory_pool "cupy.get_default_pinned_memory_pool")() | Returns CuPy default memory pool for pinned memory. |
| [`cupy.cuda.Memory`](generated/cupy.cuda.Memory.html#cupy.cuda.Memory "cupy.cuda.Memory")(size\_t size) | Memory allocation on a CUDA device. |
| [`cupy.cuda.MemoryAsync`](generated/cupy.cuda.MemoryAsync.html#cupy.cuda.MemoryAsync "cupy.cuda.MemoryAsync")(size\_t size, stream) | Asynchronous memory allocation on a CUDA device. |
| [`cupy.cuda.ManagedMemory`](generated/cupy.cuda.ManagedMemory.html#cupy.cuda.ManagedMemory "cupy.cuda.ManagedMemory")(size\_t size) | Managed memory (Unified memory) allocation on a CUDA device. |
| [`cupy.cuda.UnownedMemory`](generated/cupy.cuda.UnownedMemory.html#cupy.cuda.UnownedMemory "cupy.cuda.UnownedMemory")(intptr\_t ptr, ...) | CUDA memory that is not owned by CuPy. |
| [`cupy.cuda.PinnedMemory`](generated/cupy.cuda.PinnedMemory.html#cupy.cuda.PinnedMemory "cupy.cuda.PinnedMemory")(size[, flags]) | Pinned memory allocation on host. |
| [`cupy.cuda.MemoryPointer`](generated/cupy.cuda.MemoryPointer.html#cupy.cuda.MemoryPointer "cupy.cuda.MemoryPointer")(BaseMemory mem, ...) | Pointer to a point on a device memory. |
| [`cupy.cuda.PinnedMemoryPointer`](generated/cupy.cuda.PinnedMemoryPointer.html#cupy.cuda.PinnedMemoryPointer "cupy.cuda.PinnedMemoryPointer")(mem, ...) | Pointer of a pinned memory. |
| [`cupy.cuda.malloc_managed`](generated/cupy.cuda.malloc_managed.html#cupy.cuda.malloc_managed "cupy.cuda.malloc_managed")(size\_t size) | Allocate managed memory (unified memory). |
| [`cupy.cuda.malloc_async`](generated/cupy.cuda.malloc_async.html#cupy.cuda.malloc_async "cupy.cuda.malloc_async")(size\_t size) | Allocate memory from Stream Ordered Memory Allocator. |
| [`cupy.cuda.alloc`](generated/cupy.cuda.alloc.html#cupy.cuda.alloc "cupy.cuda.alloc")(size) | Calls the current allocator. |
| [`cupy.cuda.alloc_pinned_memory`](generated/cupy.cuda.alloc_pinned_memory.html#cupy.cuda.alloc_pinned_memory "cupy.cuda.alloc_pinned_memory")(size\_t size) | Calls the current allocator. |
| [`cupy.cuda.get_allocator`](generated/cupy.cuda.get_allocator.html#cupy.cuda.get_allocator "cupy.cuda.get_allocator")() | Returns the current allocator for GPU memory. |
| [`cupy.cuda.set_allocator`](generated/cupy.cuda.set_allocator.html#cupy.cuda.set_allocator "cupy.cuda.set_allocator")([allocator]) | Sets the current allocator for GPU memory. |
| [`cupy.cuda.using_allocator`](generated/cupy.cuda.using_allocator.html#cupy.cuda.using_allocator "cupy.cuda.using_allocator")([allocator]) | Sets a thread-local allocator for GPU memory inside |
| [`cupy.cuda.set_pinned_memory_allocator`](generated/cupy.cuda.set_pinned_memory_allocator.html#cupy.cuda.set_pinned_memory_allocator "cupy.cuda.set_pinned_memory_allocator")([...]) | Sets the current allocator for the pinned memory. |
| [`cupy.cuda.MemoryPool`](generated/cupy.cuda.MemoryPool.html#cupy.cuda.MemoryPool "cupy.cuda.MemoryPool")([allocator]) | Memory pool for all GPU devices on the host. |
| [`cupy.cuda.MemoryAsyncPool`](generated/cupy.cuda.MemoryAsyncPool.html#cupy.cuda.MemoryAsyncPool "cupy.cuda.MemoryAsyncPool")([pool\_handles]) | CUDA memory pool for all GPU devices on the host. |
| [`cupy.cuda.PinnedMemoryPool`](generated/cupy.cuda.PinnedMemoryPool.html#cupy.cuda.PinnedMemoryPool "cupy.cuda.PinnedMemoryPool")([allocator]) | Memory pool for pinned memory on the host. |
| [`cupy.cuda.PythonFunctionAllocator`](generated/cupy.cuda.PythonFunctionAllocator.html#cupy.cuda.PythonFunctionAllocator "cupy.cuda.PythonFunctionAllocator")(...) | Allocator with python functions to perform memory allocation. |
| [`cupy.cuda.CFunctionAllocator`](generated/cupy.cuda.CFunctionAllocator.html#cupy.cuda.CFunctionAllocator "cupy.cuda.CFunctionAllocator")(intptr\_t param, ...) | Allocator with C function pointers to allocation routines. |

| [`cupy.cuda.nccl.get_unique_id`](generated/cupy.cuda.nccl.get_unique_id.html#cupy.cuda.nccl.get_unique_id "cupy.cuda.nccl.get_unique_id")() | Fetch the NCCL unique ID as a Python bytes object. |

| [`cupy.cuda.nccl.groupEnd`](generated/cupy.cuda.nccl.groupEnd.html#cupy.cuda.nccl.groupEnd "cupy.cuda.nccl.groupEnd")() | End a group of NCCL calls. |
## Version[#](#version "Permalink to this heading")
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupy.cuda.get_local_runtime_version`](generated/cupy.cuda.get_local_runtime_version.html#cupy.cuda.get_local_runtime_version "cupy.cuda.get_local_runtime_version")() | Returns the version of the CUDA Runtime installed in the environment. |
## Runtime API[#](#runtime-api "Permalink to this heading")
CuPy wraps CUDA Runtime APIs to provide the native CUDA operations.
Please check the [CUDA Runtime API documentation](https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-runtime-api/index.html)
to use these functions.
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion "cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion")() |  |

## Runtime API[#](#runtime-api "Permalink to this heading")
CuPy wraps CUDA Runtime APIs to provide the native CUDA operations.
Please check the [CUDA Runtime API documentation](https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-runtime-api/index.html)
to use these functions.
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion "cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion")() |  |

| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice "cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice")() |  |

|  |  |
| --- | --- |

|  |  |
| --- | --- |

## Runtime API[#](#runtime-api "Permalink to this heading")
CuPy wraps CUDA Runtime APIs to provide the native CUDA operations.
Please check the [CUDA Runtime API documentation](https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-runtime-api/index.html)
to use these functions.
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion "cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion")() |  |

| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice "cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice")() |  |

## Runtime API[#](#runtime-api "Permalink to this heading")
CuPy wraps CUDA Runtime APIs to provide the native CUDA operations.
Please check the [CUDA Runtime API documentation](https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-runtime-api/index.html)
to use these functions.
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion "cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion")() |  |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.runtimeGetVersion`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.runtimeGetVersion.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.runtimeGetVersion "cupy.cuda.runtime.runtimeGetVersion")() | Returns the version of the CUDA Runtime statically linked to CuPy. |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice "cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice")() |  |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.getDeviceProperties`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.getDeviceProperties.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.getDeviceProperties "cupy.cuda.runtime.getDeviceProperties")(int device) |  |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetAttribute`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetAttribute.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetAttribute "cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetAttribute")(...) |  |

## Runtime API[#](#runtime-api "Permalink to this heading")
CuPy wraps CUDA Runtime APIs to provide the native CUDA operations.
Please check the [CUDA Runtime API documentation](https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-runtime-api/index.html)
to use these functions.
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion "cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion")() |  |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.runtimeGetVersion`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.runtimeGetVersion.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.runtimeGetVersion "cupy.cuda.runtime.runtimeGetVersion")() | Returns the version of the CUDA Runtime statically linked to CuPy. |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice "cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice")() |  |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.getDeviceProperties`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.getDeviceProperties.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.getDeviceProperties "cupy.cuda.runtime.getDeviceProperties")(int device) |  |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetAttribute`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetAttribute.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetAttribute "cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetAttribute")(...) |  |

## Runtime API[#](#runtime-api "Permalink to this heading")
CuPy wraps CUDA Runtime APIs to provide the native CUDA operations.
Please check the [CUDA Runtime API documentation](https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-runtime-api/index.html)
to use these functions.
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion "cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion")() |  |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.runtimeGetVersion`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.runtimeGetVersion.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.runtimeGetVersion "cupy.cuda.runtime.runtimeGetVersion")() | Returns the version of the CUDA Runtime statically linked to CuPy. |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice "cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice")() |  |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.getDeviceProperties`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.getDeviceProperties.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.getDeviceProperties "cupy.cuda.runtime.getDeviceProperties")(int device) |  |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetAttribute`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetAttribute.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetAttribute "cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetAttribute")(...) |  |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetByPCIBusId`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetByPCIBusId.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetByPCIBusId "cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetByPCIBusId")(...) |  |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetPCIBusId`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetPCIBusId.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetPCIBusId "cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetPCIBusId")(int device) |  |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetDefaultMemPool`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetDefaultMemPool.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetDefaultMemPool "cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetDefaultMemPool")(...) | Get the default mempool on the current device. |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetMemPool`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetMemPool.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetMemPool "cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetMemPool")(int device) | Get the current mempool on the current device. |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceSetMemPool`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceSetMemPool.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceSetMemPool "cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceSetMemPool")(...) | Set the current mempool on the current device to pool. |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.memPoolCreate`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.memPoolCreate.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.memPoolCreate "cupy.cuda.runtime.memPoolCreate")(...) |  |

## Runtime API[#](#runtime-api "Permalink to this heading")
CuPy wraps CUDA Runtime APIs to provide the native CUDA operations.
Please check the [CUDA Runtime API documentation](https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-runtime-api/index.html)
to use these functions.
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion "cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion")() |  |

| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice "cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice")() |  |

## Runtime API[#](#runtime-api "Permalink to this heading")
CuPy wraps CUDA Runtime APIs to provide the native CUDA operations.
Please check the [CUDA Runtime API documentation](https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-runtime-api/index.html)
to use these functions.
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion "cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion")() |  |

| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice "cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice")() |  |

|  |  |
| --- | --- |

|  |  |
| --- | --- |

## Runtime API[#](#runtime-api "Permalink to this heading")
CuPy wraps CUDA Runtime APIs to provide the native CUDA operations.
Please check the [CUDA Runtime API documentation](https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-runtime-api/index.html)
to use these functions.
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion "cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion")() |  |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.runtimeGetVersion`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.runtimeGetVersion.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.runtimeGetVersion "cupy.cuda.runtime.runtimeGetVersion")() | Returns the version of the CUDA Runtime statically linked to CuPy. |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice "cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice")() |  |

## Runtime API[#](#runtime-api "Permalink to this heading")
CuPy wraps CUDA Runtime APIs to provide the native CUDA operations.
Please check the [CUDA Runtime API documentation](https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-runtime-api/index.html)
to use these functions.
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion "cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion")() |  |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.runtimeGetVersion`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.runtimeGetVersion.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.runtimeGetVersion "cupy.cuda.runtime.runtimeGetVersion")() | Returns the version of the CUDA Runtime statically linked to CuPy. |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice "cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice")() |  |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.getDeviceProperties`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.getDeviceProperties.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.getDeviceProperties "cupy.cuda.runtime.getDeviceProperties")(int device) |  |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetAttribute`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetAttribute.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetAttribute "cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetAttribute")(...) |  |

## Runtime API[#](#runtime-api "Permalink to this heading")
CuPy wraps CUDA Runtime APIs to provide the native CUDA operations.
Please check the [CUDA Runtime API documentation](https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-runtime-api/index.html)
to use these functions.
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion "cupy.cuda.runtime.driverGetVersion")() |  |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.runtimeGetVersion`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.runtimeGetVersion.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.runtimeGetVersion "cupy.cuda.runtime.runtimeGetVersion")() | Returns the version of the CUDA Runtime statically linked to CuPy. |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice "cupy.cuda.runtime.getDevice")() |  |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.getDeviceProperties`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.getDeviceProperties.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.getDeviceProperties "cupy.cuda.runtime.getDeviceProperties")(int device) |  |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetAttribute`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetAttribute.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetAttribute "cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetAttribute")(...) |  |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetByPCIBusId`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetByPCIBusId.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetByPCIBusId "cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetByPCIBusId")(...) |  |

| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetDefaultMemPool`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetDefaultMemPool.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetDefaultMemPool "cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetDefaultMemPool")(...) | Get the default mempool on the current device. |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetMemPool`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetMemPool.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetMemPool "cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceGetMemPool")(int device) | Get the current mempool on the current device. |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceSetMemPool`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceSetMemPool.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceSetMemPool "cupy.cuda.runtime.deviceSetMemPool")(...) | Set the current mempool on the current device to pool. |
| [`cupy.cuda.runtime.memPoolCreate`](generated/cupy.cuda.runtime.memPoolCreate.html#cupy.cuda.runtime.memPoolCreate "cupy.cuda.runtime.memPoolCreate")(...) |  |
