## JIT kernel definition[#](#jit-kernel-definition "Permalink to this heading")
Supported Python built-in functions include: [`range`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#range "(in Python v3.14)"), [`len()`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#len "(in Python v3.14)"), [`max()`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#max "(in Python v3.14)"), [`min()`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#min "(in Python v3.14)").
Note
If loop unrolling is needed, use [`cupyx.jit.range()`](generated/cupyx.jit.range.html#cupyx.jit.range "cupyx.jit.range") instead of the built-in [`range`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#range "(in Python v3.14)").
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupyx.jit.rawkernel`](generated/cupyx.jit.rawkernel.html#cupyx.jit.rawkernel "cupyx.jit.rawkernel")(\*[, mode, device]) | A decorator compiles a Python function into CUDA kernel. |

| [`cupyx.jit.laneid`](generated/cupyx.jit.laneid.html#cupyx.jit.laneid "cupyx.jit.laneid")() | Returns the lane ID of the calling thread, ranging in `[0, jit.warpsize)`. |
| [`cupyx.jit.warpsize`](generated/cupyx.jit.warpsize.html#cupyx.jit.warpsize "cupyx.jit.warpsize") | Returns the number of threads in a warp. |
| [`cupyx.jit.range`](generated/cupyx.jit.range.html#cupyx.jit.range "cupyx.jit.range")(\*args[, unroll]) | Range with loop unrolling support. |

<!-- Source: https://docs.cupy.dev/en/stable/reference/generated/cupy.ElementwiseKernel.html -->
# cupy.ElementwiseKernel[#](#cupy-elementwisekernel "Permalink to this heading")
*class* cupy.ElementwiseKernel(*in\_params*, *out\_params*, *operation*, *name='kernel'*, *reduce\_dims=True*, *preamble=''*, *no\_return=False*, *return\_tuple=False*, *\*\*kwargs*)[[source]](https://github.com/cupy/cupy/blob/v14.1.1/cupy/_core/_kernel.pyx)[#](#cupy.ElementwiseKernel "Permalink to this definition")
:   User-defined elementwise kernel.
    This class can be used to define an elementwise kernel with or without
    broadcasting.
    The kernel is compiled at an invocation of the
    [`__call__()`](#cupy.ElementwiseKernel.__call__ "cupy.ElementwiseKernel.__call__") method,
    which is cached for each device.
    The compiled binary is also cached into a file under the
    `$HOME/.cupy/kernel_cache/` directory with a hashed file name. The cached
    binary is reused by other processes.
    Parameters:
    :   * **in\_params** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Input argument list.
        * **out\_params** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Output argument list.
        * **operation** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – The body in the loop written in CUDA-C/C++.
        * **name** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Name of the kernel function. It should be set for
          readability of the performance profiling.
        * **reduce\_dims** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If `False`, the shapes of array arguments are
          kept within the kernel invocation. The shapes are reduced
          (i.e., the arrays are reshaped without copy to the minimum
          dimension) by default. It may make the kernel fast by reducing the
          index calculations.
        * **options** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")) – Compile options passed to NVRTC. For details, see
          <https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/nvrtc/index.html#group__options>.
        * **preamble** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Fragment of the CUDA-C/C++ code that is inserted at the
          top of the cu file.
        * **no\_return** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If `True`, \_\_call\_\_ returns `None`.
        * **return\_tuple** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If `True`, \_\_call\_\_ always returns tuple of
          array even if single value is returned.
        * **loop\_prep** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Fragment of the CUDA-C/C++ code that is inserted at
          the top of the kernel function definition and above the `for`
          loop.
        * **after\_loop** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Fragment of the CUDA-C/C++ code that is inserted at
          the bottom of the kernel function definition.
    Methods
    \_\_call\_\_(*self*, *\*args*, *\*\*kwargs*)[#](#cupy.ElementwiseKernel.__call__ "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Compiles and invokes the elementwise kernel.
        The compilation runs only if the kernel is not cached. Note that the
        kernels with different argument dtypes or dimensions are not
        compatible. It means that single ElementwiseKernel object may be
        compiled into multiple kernel binaries.
        Parameters:
        :   * **args** – Arguments of the kernel.
            * **size** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – Range size of the indices. By default, the range size
              is automatically determined from the result of broadcasting.
              This parameter must be specified if and only if all ndarrays
              are raw and the range size cannot be determined
              automatically.
            * **block\_size** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – Number of threads per block. By default, the
              value is set to 128.
        Returns:
        :   If `no_return` has not set, arrays are returned according to the
            `out_params` argument of the `__init__` method.
            If `no_return` has set, `None` is returned.
    \_\_eq\_\_(*value*, */*)[#](#cupy.ElementwiseKernel.__eq__ "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Return self==value.
    \_\_ne\_\_(*value*, */*)[#](#cupy.ElementwiseKernel.__ne__ "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Return self!=value.
    \_\_lt\_\_(*value*, */*)[#](#cupy.ElementwiseKernel.__lt__ "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Return self<value.
    \_\_le\_\_(*value*, */*)[#](#cupy.ElementwiseKernel.__le__ "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Return self<=value.
    \_\_gt\_\_(*value*, */*)[#](#cupy.ElementwiseKernel.__gt__ "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Return self>value.
    \_\_ge\_\_(*value*, */*)[#](#cupy.ElementwiseKernel.__ge__ "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Return self>=value.
    Attributes
    cached\_code[#](#cupy.ElementwiseKernel.cached_code "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Returns next(iter(self.cached\_codes.values())).
        This property method is for debugging purpose.
        The return value is not guaranteed to keep backward compatibility.
    cached\_codes[#](#cupy.ElementwiseKernel.cached_codes "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Returns a dict that has input types as keys and codes values.
        This property method is for debugging purpose.
        The return value is not guaranteed to keep backward compatibility.
    in\_params[#](#cupy.ElementwiseKernel.in_params "Permalink to this definition")
    kwargs[#](#cupy.ElementwiseKernel.kwargs "Permalink to this definition")
    name[#](#cupy.ElementwiseKernel.name "Permalink to this definition")
    nargs[#](#cupy.ElementwiseKernel.nargs "Permalink to this definition")
    nin[#](#cupy.ElementwiseKernel.nin "Permalink to this definition")
    no\_return[#](#cupy.ElementwiseKernel.no_return "Permalink to this definition")
    nout[#](#cupy.ElementwiseKernel.nout "Permalink to this definition")
    operation[#](#cupy.ElementwiseKernel.operation "Permalink to this definition")
    out\_params[#](#cupy.ElementwiseKernel.out_params "Permalink to this definition")
    params[#](#cupy.ElementwiseKernel.params "Permalink to this definition")
    preamble[#](#cupy.ElementwiseKernel.preamble "Permalink to this definition")
    reduce\_dims[#](#cupy.ElementwiseKernel.reduce_dims "Permalink to this definition")
    return\_tuple[#](#cupy.ElementwiseKernel.return_tuple "Permalink to this definition")

# cupy.ReductionKernel[#](#cupy-reductionkernel "Permalink to this heading")
*class* cupy.ReductionKernel(*str in\_params*, *str out\_params*, *map\_expr*, *reduce\_expr*, *post\_map\_expr*, *identity*, *name='reduce\_kernel'*, *reduce\_type=None*, *reduce\_dims=True*, *preamble=''*, *options=()*)[[source]](https://github.com/cupy/cupy/blob/v14.1.1/cupy/_core/_reduction.pyx)[#](#cupy.ReductionKernel "Permalink to this definition")
:   User-defined reduction kernel.
    This class can be used to define a reduction kernel with or without
    broadcasting.
    The kernel is compiled at an invocation of the
    [`__call__()`](#cupy.ReductionKernel.__call__ "cupy.ReductionKernel.__call__") method, which is cached for each device.
    The compiled binary is also cached into a file under the
    `$HOME/.cupy/kernel_cache/` directory with a hashed file name. The cached
    binary is reused by other processes.
    Parameters:
    :   * **in\_params** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Input argument list.
        * **out\_params** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Output argument list.
        * **map\_expr** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Mapping expression for input values.
        * **reduce\_expr** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Reduction expression.
        * **post\_map\_expr** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Mapping expression for reduced values.
        * **identity** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Identity value for starting the reduction.
        * **name** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Name of the kernel function. It should be set for
          readability of the performance profiling.
        * **reduce\_type** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Type of values to be used for reduction. This type
          is used to store the special variables `a`.
        * **reduce\_dims** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If `True`, input arrays are reshaped without copy
          to smaller dimensions for efficiency.
        * **preamble** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Fragment of the CUDA-C/C++ code that is inserted at the
          top of the cu file.
        * **options** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Additional compilation options.
    Methods
    \_\_call\_\_(*self*, *\*args*, *\*\*kwargs*)[#](#cupy.ReductionKernel.__call__ "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Compiles and invokes the reduction kernel.
        The compilation runs only if the kernel is not cached. Note that the
        kernels with different argument dtypes, ndims, or axis are not
        compatible. It means that single ReductionKernel object may be compiled
        into multiple kernel binaries.
        Parameters:
        :   * **args** – Arguments of the kernel.
            * **out** ([*cupy.ndarray*](cupy.ndarray.html#cupy.ndarray "cupy.ndarray")) – The output array. This can only be specified if
              `args` does not contain the output array.
            * **axis** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *ints*) – Axis or axes along which the
              reduction is performed.
            * **keepdims** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If `True`, the specified axes are remained as
              axes of length one.
            * **stream** ([*cupy.cuda.Stream*](cupy.cuda.Stream.html#cupy.cuda.Stream "cupy.cuda.Stream")*,* *optional*) – The CUDA stream to launch the
              kernel on. If not given, the current stream will be used.
        Returns:
        :   Arrays are returned according to the `out_params` argument of the
            `__init__` method.
    \_\_eq\_\_(*value*, */*)[#](#cupy.ReductionKernel.__eq__ "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Return self==value.
    \_\_ne\_\_(*value*, */*)[#](#cupy.ReductionKernel.__ne__ "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Return self!=value.
    \_\_lt\_\_(*value*, */*)[#](#cupy.ReductionKernel.__lt__ "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Return self<value.
    \_\_le\_\_(*value*, */*)[#](#cupy.ReductionKernel.__le__ "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Return self<=value.
    \_\_gt\_\_(*value*, */*)[#](#cupy.ReductionKernel.__gt__ "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Return self>value.
    \_\_ge\_\_(*value*, */*)[#](#cupy.ReductionKernel.__ge__ "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Return self>=value.
    Attributes
    cached\_code[#](#cupy.ReductionKernel.cached_code "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Returns next(iter(self.cached\_codes.values())).
        This property method is for debugging purpose.
        The return value is not guaranteed to keep backward compatibility.
    cached\_codes[#](#cupy.ReductionKernel.cached_codes "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Returns a dict that has input types as keys and codes values.
        This property method is for debugging purpose.
        The return value is not guaranteed to keep backward compatibility.
    identity[#](#cupy.ReductionKernel.identity "Permalink to this definition")
    :   str
        Type:
        :   identity
    in\_params[#](#cupy.ReductionKernel.in_params "Permalink to this definition")
    map\_expr[#](#cupy.ReductionKernel.map_expr "Permalink to this definition")
    name[#](#cupy.ReductionKernel.name "Permalink to this definition")
    nargs[#](#cupy.ReductionKernel.nargs "Permalink to this definition")
    nin[#](#cupy.ReductionKernel.nin "Permalink to this definition")
    nout[#](#cupy.ReductionKernel.nout "Permalink to this definition")
    options[#](#cupy.ReductionKernel.options "Permalink to this definition")
    out\_params[#](#cupy.ReductionKernel.out_params "Permalink to this definition")
    params[#](#cupy.ReductionKernel.params "Permalink to this definition")
    post\_map\_expr[#](#cupy.ReductionKernel.post_map_expr "Permalink to this definition")
    preamble[#](#cupy.ReductionKernel.preamble "Permalink to this definition")
    reduce\_dims[#](#cupy.ReductionKernel.reduce_dims "Permalink to this definition")
    reduce\_expr[#](#cupy.ReductionKernel.reduce_expr "Permalink to this definition")
    reduce\_type[#](#cupy.ReductionKernel.reduce_type "Permalink to this definition")

  + [`ReductionKernel.reduce_dims`](#cupy.ReductionKernel.reduce_dims)

  + [`ReductionKernel.reduce_type`](#cupy.ReductionKernel.reduce_type)

# cupy.RawKernel[#](#cupy-rawkernel "Permalink to this heading")
*class* cupy.RawKernel(*str code*, *str name*, *tuple options=()*, *str backend='nvrtc'*, *bool translate\_cucomplex=False*, *\**, *bool enable\_cooperative\_groups=False*, *jitify=None*)[[source]](https://github.com/cupy/cupy/blob/v14.1.1/cupy/_core/raw.pyx)[#](#cupy.RawKernel "Permalink to this definition")
:   User-defined custom kernel.
    This class can be used to define a custom kernel using raw CUDA source.
    The kernel is compiled at an invocation of the [`__call__()`](#cupy.RawKernel.__call__ "cupy.RawKernel.__call__")
    method, which is cached for each device.
    The compiled binary is also cached into a file under the
    `$HOME/.cupy/kernel_cache/` directory with a hashed file name. The cached
    binary is reused by other processes.
    Parameters:
    :   * **code** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – CUDA source code.
        * **name** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Name of the kernel function.
        * **options** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Compiler options passed to the backend (NVRTC
          or NVCC). For details, see
          <https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/nvrtc/index.html#group__options> or
          <https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-compiler-driver-nvcc/index.html#command-option-description>
        * **backend** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Either nvrtc or nvcc. Defaults to nvrtc
        * **translate\_cucomplex** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether the CUDA source includes the header
          cuComplex.h or not. If set to `True`, any code that uses the
          functions from cuComplex.h will be translated to its Thrust
          counterpart. Defaults to `False`.
        * **enable\_cooperative\_groups** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether to enable cooperative groups
          in the CUDA source. If set to `True`, compile options are
          configured properly and the kernel is launched with
          `cuLaunchCooperativeKernel` so that cooperative groups can be
          used from the CUDA source.
          This feature is only supported in CUDA 9 or later.
        * **jitify** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether or not to use [Jitify](https://github.com/NVIDIA/jitify) to assist NVRTC to
          compile C++ kernels. Defaults to `False`.
    Note
    Starting CuPy v14.0.0, RawKernel by default compiles with the C++17
    standard (`-std=c++17`) if it’s not specified in `options`.
    Note
    Unlike ElementwiseKernel, RawKernel ignores any views on
    CuPy arrays. You are responsible for handling strides manually.
    For example, passing matrix.T is treated as if you passed matrix.
    Methods
    \_\_call\_\_(*self*, *grid*, *block*, *args*, *\*\*kwargs*)[#](#cupy.RawKernel.__call__ "Permalink to this definition")
    :   \_\_call\_\_(self, grid, block, args, \*, shared\_mem=0)
        Compiles and invokes the kernel.
        The compilation runs only if the kernel is not cached.
        Parameters:
        :   * **grid** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")) – Size of grid in blocks.
            * **block** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")) – Dimensions of each thread block.
            * **args** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")) – Arguments of the kernel.
            * **shared\_mem** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – Dynamic shared-memory size per thread block in
              bytes.
    compile(*self*, *log\_stream=None*)[#](#cupy.RawKernel.compile "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Compile the current kernel.
        In general, you don’t have to call this method;
        kernels are compiled implicitly on the first call.
        Parameters:
        :   **log\_stream** ([*object*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#object "(in Python v3.14)")) – Pass either `sys.stdout` or a file object to
            which the compiler output will be written.
            Defaults to `None`.
    \_\_eq\_\_(*value*, */*)[#](#cupy.RawKernel.__eq__ "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Return self==value.
    \_\_ne\_\_(*value*, */*)[#](#cupy.RawKernel.__ne__ "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Return self!=value.
    \_\_lt\_\_(*value*, */*)[#](#cupy.RawKernel.__lt__ "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Return self<value.
    \_\_le\_\_(*value*, */*)[#](#cupy.RawKernel.__le__ "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Return self<=value.
    \_\_gt\_\_(*value*, */*)[#](#cupy.RawKernel.__gt__ "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Return self>value.
    \_\_ge\_\_(*value*, */*)[#](#cupy.RawKernel.__ge__ "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Return self>=value.
    Attributes
    attributes[#](#cupy.RawKernel.attributes "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Returns a dictionary containing runtime kernel attributes. This is
        a read-only property; to overwrite the attributes, use
        ```
        kernel = RawKernel(...)  # arguments omitted
        kernel.max_dynamic_shared_size_bytes = ...
        kernel.preferred_shared_memory_carveout = ...
        ```
        Note that the two attributes shown in the above example are the only
        two currently settable in CUDA.
        Any attribute not existing in the present CUDA toolkit version will
        have the value -1.
        Returns:
        :   A dictionary containing the kernel’s attributes.
        Return type:
        :   [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")
    backend[#](#cupy.RawKernel.backend "Permalink to this definition")
    binary\_version[#](#cupy.RawKernel.binary_version "Permalink to this definition")
    :   The binary architecture version that was used during compilation,
        in the format: 10\*major + minor.
    cache\_mode\_ca[#](#cupy.RawKernel.cache_mode_ca "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Indicates whether option “-Xptxas –dlcm=ca” was set during
        compilation.
    code[#](#cupy.RawKernel.code "Permalink to this definition")
    const\_size\_bytes[#](#cupy.RawKernel.const_size_bytes "Permalink to this definition")
    :   The size in bytes of constant memory used by the function.
    enable\_cooperative\_groups[#](#cupy.RawKernel.enable_cooperative_groups "Permalink to this definition")
    file\_path[#](#cupy.RawKernel.file_path "Permalink to this definition")
    kernel[#](#cupy.RawKernel.kernel "Permalink to this definition")
    local\_size\_bytes[#](#cupy.RawKernel.local_size_bytes "Permalink to this definition")
    :   The size in bytes of local memory used by the function.
    max\_dynamic\_shared\_size\_bytes[#](#cupy.RawKernel.max_dynamic_shared_size_bytes "Permalink to this definition")
    :   The maximum dynamically-allocated shared memory size in bytes that
        can be used by the function. Can be set.
    max\_threads\_per\_block[#](#cupy.RawKernel.max_threads_per_block "Permalink to this definition")
    :   The maximum number of threads per block that can successfully
        launch the function on the device.
    name[#](#cupy.RawKernel.name "Permalink to this definition")
    num\_regs[#](#cupy.RawKernel.num_regs "Permalink to this definition")
    :   The number of registers used by the function.
    options[#](#cupy.RawKernel.options "Permalink to this definition")
    preferred\_shared\_memory\_carveout[#](#cupy.RawKernel.preferred_shared_memory_carveout "Permalink to this definition")
    :   On devices that have a unified L1 cache and shared memory,
        indicates the fraction to be used for shared memory as a
        percentage of the total. If the fraction does not exactly equal a
        supported shared memory capacity, then the next larger supported
        capacity is used. Can be set.
    ptx\_version[#](#cupy.RawKernel.ptx_version "Permalink to this definition")
    :   The PTX virtual architecture version that was used during
        compilation, in the format: 10\*major + minor.
    shared\_size\_bytes[#](#cupy.RawKernel.shared_size_bytes "Permalink to this definition")
    :   The size in bytes of the statically-allocated shared memory
        used by the function. This is separate from any dynamically-allocated
        shared memory, which must be specified when the function is called.

<!-- Source: https://docs.cupy.dev/en/stable/reference/generated/cupy.RawModule.html -->
# cupy.RawModule[#](#cupy-rawmodule "Permalink to this heading")
*class* cupy.RawModule(*str code=None*, *\**, *str path=None*, *tuple options=()*, *str backend='nvrtc'*, *bool translate\_cucomplex=False*, *bool enable\_cooperative\_groups=False*, *name\_expressions=None*, *jitify=None*)[[source]](https://github.com/cupy/cupy/blob/v14.1.1/cupy/_core/raw.pyx)[#](#cupy.RawModule "Permalink to this definition")
:   User-defined custom module.
    This class can be used to either compile raw CUDA sources or load CUDA
    modules (\*.cubin, \*.ptx). This class is useful when a number of CUDA
    kernels in the same source need to be retrieved.
    For the former case, the CUDA source code is compiled when any method is
    called. For the latter case, an existing CUDA binary (\*.cubin) or a PTX
    file can be loaded by providing its path.
    CUDA kernels in a [`RawModule`](#cupy.RawModule "cupy.RawModule") can be retrieved by calling
    [`get_function()`](#cupy.RawModule.get_function "cupy.RawModule.get_function"), which will return an instance of [`RawKernel`](cupy.RawKernel.html#cupy.RawKernel "cupy.RawKernel").
    (Same as in [`RawKernel`](cupy.RawKernel.html#cupy.RawKernel "cupy.RawKernel"), the generated binary is also cached.)
    Parameters:
    :   * **code** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – CUDA source code. Mutually exclusive with `path`.
        * **path** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Path to cubin/ptx. Mutually exclusive with `code`.
        * **options** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Compiler options passed to the backend (NVRTC
          or NVCC). For details, see
          <https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/nvrtc/index.html#group__options> or
          <https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-compiler-driver-nvcc/index.html#command-option-description>.
        * **backend** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Either nvrtc or nvcc. Defaults to nvrtc
        * **translate\_cucomplex** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether the CUDA source includes the header
          cuComplex.h or not. If set to `True`, any code that uses the
          functions from cuComplex.h will be translated to its Thrust
          counterpart. Defaults to `False`.
        * **enable\_cooperative\_groups** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether to enable cooperative groups
          in the CUDA source. If set to `True`, compile options are
          configured properly and the kernel is launched with
          `cuLaunchCooperativeKernel` so that cooperative groups can be
          used from the CUDA source.
          This feature is only supported in CUDA 9 or later.
        * **name\_expressions** (*sequence* *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – A sequence (e.g. list) of strings
          referring to the names of C++ global/template kernels. For example,
          `name_expressions=['func1<int>', 'func1<double>', 'func2']` for
          the template kernel `func1<T>` and non-template kernel `func2`.
          Strings in this tuple must then be passed, one at a time, to
          [`get_function()`](#cupy.RawModule.get_function "cupy.RawModule.get_function") to retrieve the corresponding kernel.
        * **jitify** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether or not to use [Jitify](https://github.com/NVIDIA/jitify) to assist NVRTC to
          compile C++ kernels. Defaults to `False`.
    Note
    Starting CuPy v14.0.0, RawModule by default compiles with the C++17
    standard (`-std=c++17`) if it’s not specified in `options`.
    Note
    Each kernel in `RawModule` possesses independent function attributes.
    Note
    Before CuPy v8.0.0, the compilation happens at initialization. Now, it
    happens at the first time retrieving any object (kernels or pointers)
    from the module.
    Methods
    compile(*self*, *log\_stream=None*)[#](#cupy.RawModule.compile "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Compile the current module.
        In general, you don’t have to call this method;
        kernels are compiled implicitly on the first call.
        Parameters:
        :   **log\_stream** ([*object*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#object "(in Python v3.14)")) – Pass either `sys.stdout` or a file object to
            which the compiler output will be written.
            Defaults to `None`.
        Note
        Calling [`compile()`](#cupy.RawModule.compile "cupy.RawModule.compile") will reset the internal state of
        a [`RawKernel`](cupy.RawKernel.html#cupy.RawKernel "cupy.RawKernel").
    get\_function(*self*, *str name*)[#](#cupy.RawModule.get_function "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Retrieve a CUDA kernel by its name from the module.
        Parameters:
        :   **name** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Name of the kernel function. For C++ global/template
            kernels, `name` refers to one of the name expressions
            specified when initializing the present [`RawModule`](#cupy.RawModule "cupy.RawModule")
            instance.
        Returns:
        :   An `RawKernel` instance.
        Return type:
        :   [RawKernel](cupy.RawKernel.html#cupy.RawKernel "cupy.RawKernel")
        Note
        The following example shows how to retrieve one of the specialized
        C++ template kernels:
        ```
        code = r'''
        template<typename T>
        __global__ void func(T* in_arr) { /* do something */ }
        '''
        kers = ('func<int>', 'func<float>', 'func<double>')
        mod = cupy.RawModule(code=code, options=('--std=c++17',),
                             name_expressions=kers)
        // retrieve func<int>
        ker_int = mod.get_function(kers[0])
        ```
        See also
        `nvrtcAddNameExpression` and `nvrtcGetLoweredName` from
        [Accessing Lowered Names](https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/nvrtc/index.html#accessing-lowered-names) of the NVRTC documentation.
    get\_global(*self*, *name*)[#](#cupy.RawModule.get_global "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Retrieve a pointer to a global symbol by its name from the module.
        Parameters:
        :   **name** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Name of the global symbol.
        Returns:
        :   A handle to the global symbol.
        Return type:
        :   [*MemoryPointer*](cupy.cuda.MemoryPointer.html#cupy.cuda.MemoryPointer "cupy.cuda.MemoryPointer")
        Note
        This method can be used to access, for example, constant memory:
        ```
        # to get a pointer to "arr" declared in the source like this:
        # __constant__ float arr[10];
        memptr = mod.get_global("arr")
        # ...wrap it using cupy.ndarray with a known shape
        arr_ndarray = cp.ndarray((10,), cp.float32, memptr)
        # ...perform data transfer to initialize it
        arr_ndarray[...] = cp.random.random((10,), dtype=cp.float32)
        # ...and arr is ready to be accessed by RawKernels
        ```
    \_\_eq\_\_(*value*, */*)[#](#cupy.RawModule.__eq__ "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Return self==value.
    \_\_ne\_\_(*value*, */*)[#](#cupy.RawModule.__ne__ "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Return self!=value.
    \_\_lt\_\_(*value*, */*)[#](#cupy.RawModule.__lt__ "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Return self<value.
    \_\_le\_\_(*value*, */*)[#](#cupy.RawModule.__le__ "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Return self<=value.
    \_\_gt\_\_(*value*, */*)[#](#cupy.RawModule.__gt__ "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Return self>value.
    \_\_ge\_\_(*value*, */*)[#](#cupy.RawModule.__ge__ "Permalink to this definition")
    :   Return self>=value.
    Attributes
    backend[#](#cupy.RawModule.backend "Permalink to this definition")
    code[#](#cupy.RawModule.code "Permalink to this definition")
    enable\_cooperative\_groups[#](#cupy.RawModule.enable_cooperative_groups "Permalink to this definition")
    file\_path[#](#cupy.RawModule.file_path "Permalink to this definition")
    module[#](#cupy.RawModule.module "Permalink to this definition")
    name\_expressions[#](#cupy.RawModule.name_expressions "Permalink to this definition")
    options[#](#cupy.RawModule.options "Permalink to this definition")

<!-- Source: https://docs.cupy.dev/en/stable/reference/generated/cupy.fuse.html -->
# cupy.fuse[#](#cupy-fuse "Permalink to this heading")
cupy.fuse(*\*args*, *\*\*kwargs*)[#](#cupy.fuse "Permalink to this definition")
:   Decorator that fuses a function.
    This decorator can be used to define an elementwise or reduction kernel
    more easily than [`ElementwiseKernel`](cupy.ElementwiseKernel.html#cupy.ElementwiseKernel "cupy.ElementwiseKernel") or
    [`ReductionKernel`](cupy.ReductionKernel.html#cupy.ReductionKernel "cupy.ReductionKernel").
    Since the fused kernels are cached and reused, it is recommended to reuse
    the same decorated functions instead of e.g. decorating local functions
    that are defined multiple times.
    Parameters:
    :   **kernel\_name** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Name of the fused kernel function.
        If omitted, the name of the decorated function is used.
    Example
    ```
    >>> @cupy.fuse(kernel_name='squared_diff')
    ... def squared_diff(x, y):
    ...     return (x - y) * (x - y)
    ...
    >>> x = cupy.arange(10)
    >>> y = cupy.arange(10)[::-1]
    >>> squared_diff(x, y)
    array([81, 49, 25,  9,  1,  1,  9, 25, 49, 81])
    ```

cupy.RawModule](cupy.RawModule.html "previous page")

cupyx.jit.rawkernel](cupyx.jit.rawkernel.html "next page")

## JIT kernel definition[#](#jit-kernel-definition "Permalink to this heading")
Supported Python built-in functions include: [`range`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#range "(in Python v3.14)"), [`len()`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#len "(in Python v3.14)"), [`max()`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#max "(in Python v3.14)"), [`min()`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#min "(in Python v3.14)").
Note
If loop unrolling is needed, use [`cupyx.jit.range()`](generated/cupyx.jit.range.html#cupyx.jit.range "cupyx.jit.range") instead of the built-in [`range`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#range "(in Python v3.14)").
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`cupyx.jit.rawkernel`](generated/cupyx.jit.rawkernel.html#cupyx.jit.rawkernel "cupyx.jit.rawkernel")(\*[, mode, device]) | A decorator compiles a Python function into CUDA kernel. |
| [`cupyx.jit.threadIdx`](generated/cupyx.jit.threadIdx.html#cupyx.jit.threadIdx "cupyx.jit.threadIdx") | dim3 threadIdx |
| [`cupyx.jit.blockDim`](generated/cupyx.jit.blockDim.html#cupyx.jit.blockDim "cupyx.jit.blockDim") | dim3 blockDim |
| [`cupyx.jit.blockIdx`](generated/cupyx.jit.blockIdx.html#cupyx.jit.blockIdx "cupyx.jit.blockIdx") | dim3 blockIdx |
| [`cupyx.jit.gridDim`](generated/cupyx.jit.gridDim.html#cupyx.jit.gridDim "cupyx.jit.gridDim") | dim3 gridDim |
| [`cupyx.jit.grid`](generated/cupyx.jit.grid.html#cupyx.jit.grid "cupyx.jit.grid")(ndim) | Compute the thread index in the grid. |
| [`cupyx.jit.gridsize`](generated/cupyx.jit.gridsize.html#cupyx.jit.gridsize "cupyx.jit.gridsize")(ndim) | Compute the grid size. |
| [`cupyx.jit.laneid`](generated/cupyx.jit.laneid.html#cupyx.jit.laneid "cupyx.jit.laneid")() | Returns the lane ID of the calling thread, ranging in `[0, jit.warpsize)`. |
| [`cupyx.jit.warpsize`](generated/cupyx.jit.warpsize.html#cupyx.jit.warpsize "cupyx.jit.warpsize") | Returns the number of threads in a warp. |
| [`cupyx.jit.range`](generated/cupyx.jit.range.html#cupyx.jit.range "cupyx.jit.range")(\*args[, unroll]) | Range with loop unrolling support. |
| [`cupyx.jit.syncthreads`](generated/cupyx.jit.syncthreads.html#cupyx.jit.syncthreads "cupyx.jit.syncthreads")() | Calls `__syncthreads()`. |
| [`cupyx.jit.syncwarp`](generated/cupyx.jit.syncwarp.html#cupyx.jit.syncwarp "cupyx.jit.syncwarp")(\*[, mask]) | Calls `__syncwarp()`. |
| [`cupyx.jit.shfl_sync`](generated/cupyx.jit.shfl_sync.html#cupyx.jit.shfl_sync "cupyx.jit.shfl_sync")(mask, var, val\_id, \*[, ...]) | Calls the `__shfl_sync` function. |
| [`cupyx.jit.shfl_up_sync`](generated/cupyx.jit.shfl_up_sync.html#cupyx.jit.shfl_up_sync "cupyx.jit.shfl_up_sync")(mask, var, val\_id, \*) | Calls the `__shfl_up_sync` function. |
| [`cupyx.jit.shfl_down_sync`](generated/cupyx.jit.shfl_down_sync.html#cupyx.jit.shfl_down_sync "cupyx.jit.shfl_down_sync")(mask, var, val\_id, \*) | Calls the `__shfl_down_sync` function. |
| [`cupyx.jit.shfl_xor_sync`](generated/cupyx.jit.shfl_xor_sync.html#cupyx.jit.shfl_xor_sync "cupyx.jit.shfl_xor_sync")(mask, var, val\_id, \*) | Calls the `__shfl_xor_sync` function. |
| [`cupyx.jit.shared_memory`](generated/cupyx.jit.shared_memory.html#cupyx.jit.shared_memory "cupyx.jit.shared_memory")(dtype, size[, alignment]) | Allocates shared memory and returns it as a 1-D array. |
| [`cupyx.jit.atomic_add`](generated/cupyx.jit.atomic_add.html#cupyx.jit.atomic_add "cupyx.jit.atomic_add")(array, index, value[, ...]) | Calls the `atomicAdd` function to operate atomically on `array[index]`. |
| [`cupyx.jit.atomic_sub`](generated/cupyx.jit.atomic_sub.html#cupyx.jit.atomic_sub "cupyx.jit.atomic_sub")(array, index, value[, ...]) | Calls the `atomicSub` function to operate atomically on `array[index]`. |

| [`cupyx.jit.atomic_cas`](generated/cupyx.jit.atomic_cas.html#cupyx.jit.atomic_cas "cupyx.jit.atomic_cas")(array, index, value[, ...]) | Calls the `atomicCAS` function to operate atomically on `array[index]`. |
| [`cupyx.jit.atomic_and`](generated/cupyx.jit.atomic_and.html#cupyx.jit.atomic_and "cupyx.jit.atomic_and")(array, index, value[, ...]) | Calls the `atomicAnd` function to operate atomically on `array[index]`. |
| [`cupyx.jit.atomic_or`](generated/cupyx.jit.atomic_or.html#cupyx.jit.atomic_or "cupyx.jit.atomic_or")(array, index, value[, ...]) | Calls the `atomicOr` function to operate atomically on `array[index]`. |
| [`cupyx.jit.atomic_xor`](generated/cupyx.jit.atomic_xor.html#cupyx.jit.atomic_xor "cupyx.jit.atomic_xor")(array, index, value[, ...]) | Calls the `atomicXor` function to operate atomically on `array[index]`. |
| [`cupyx.jit.cg.this_grid`](generated/cupyx.jit.cg.this_grid.html#cupyx.jit.cg.this_grid "cupyx.jit.cg.this_grid")() | Returns the current grid group ([`_GridGroup`](generated/cupyx.jit.cg._GridGroup.html#cupyx.jit.cg._GridGroup "cupyx.jit.cg._GridGroup")). |

Supported Python built-in functions include: [`range`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#range "(in Python v3.14)"), [`len()`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#len "(in Python v3.14)"), [`max()`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#max "(in Python v3.14)"), [`min()`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#min "(in Python v3.14)").

| [`cupyx.jit.gridDim`](generated/cupyx.jit.gridDim.html#cupyx.jit.gridDim "cupyx.jit.gridDim") | dim3 gridDim |
| [`cupyx.jit.grid`](generated/cupyx.jit.grid.html#cupyx.jit.grid "cupyx.jit.grid")(ndim) | Compute the thread index in the grid. |
| [`cupyx.jit.gridsize`](generated/cupyx.jit.gridsize.html#cupyx.jit.gridsize "cupyx.jit.gridsize")(ndim) | Compute the grid size. |
| [`cupyx.jit.laneid`](generated/cupyx.jit.laneid.html#cupyx.jit.laneid "cupyx.jit.laneid")() | Returns the lane ID of the calling thread, ranging in `[0, jit.warpsize)`. |
| [`cupyx.jit.warpsize`](generated/cupyx.jit.warpsize.html#cupyx.jit.warpsize "cupyx.jit.warpsize") | Returns the number of threads in a warp. |
| [`cupyx.jit.range`](generated/cupyx.jit.range.html#cupyx.jit.range "cupyx.jit.range")(\*args[, unroll]) | Range with loop unrolling support. |
| [`cupyx.jit.syncthreads`](generated/cupyx.jit.syncthreads.html#cupyx.jit.syncthreads "cupyx.jit.syncthreads")() | Calls `__syncthreads()`. |
| [`cupyx.jit.syncwarp`](generated/cupyx.jit.syncwarp.html#cupyx.jit.syncwarp "cupyx.jit.syncwarp")(\*[, mask]) | Calls `__syncwarp()`. |
| [`cupyx.jit.shfl_sync`](generated/cupyx.jit.shfl_sync.html#cupyx.jit.shfl_sync "cupyx.jit.shfl_sync")(mask, var, val\_id, \*[, ...]) | Calls the `__shfl_sync` function. |
| [`cupyx.jit.shfl_up_sync`](generated/cupyx.jit.shfl_up_sync.html#cupyx.jit.shfl_up_sync "cupyx.jit.shfl_up_sync")(mask, var, val\_id, \*) | Calls the `__shfl_up_sync` function. |
| [`cupyx.jit.shfl_down_sync`](generated/cupyx.jit.shfl_down_sync.html#cupyx.jit.shfl_down_sync "cupyx.jit.shfl_down_sync")(mask, var, val\_id, \*) | Calls the `__shfl_down_sync` function. |
| [`cupyx.jit.shfl_xor_sync`](generated/cupyx.jit.shfl_xor_sync.html#cupyx.jit.shfl_xor_sync "cupyx.jit.shfl_xor_sync")(mask, var, val\_id, \*) | Calls the `__shfl_xor_sync` function. |
| [`cupyx.jit.shared_memory`](generated/cupyx.jit.shared_memory.html#cupyx.jit.shared_memory "cupyx.jit.shared_memory")(dtype, size[, alignment]) | Allocates shared memory and returns it as a 1-D array. |
| [`cupyx.jit.atomic_add`](generated/cupyx.jit.atomic_add.html#cupyx.jit.atomic_add "cupyx.jit.atomic_add")(array, index, value[, ...]) | Calls the `atomicAdd` function to operate atomically on `array[index]`. |
| [`cupyx.jit.atomic_sub`](generated/cupyx.jit.atomic_sub.html#cupyx.jit.atomic_sub "cupyx.jit.atomic_sub")(array, index, value[, ...]) | Calls the `atomicSub` function to operate atomically on `array[index]`. |
| [`cupyx.jit.atomic_exch`](generated/cupyx.jit.atomic_exch.html#cupyx.jit.atomic_exch "cupyx.jit.atomic_exch")(array, index, value[, ...]) | Calls the `atomicExch` function to operate atomically on `array[index]`. |
| [`cupyx.jit.atomic_min`](generated/cupyx.jit.atomic_min.html#cupyx.jit.atomic_min "cupyx.jit.atomic_min")(array, index, value[, ...]) | Calls the `atomicMin` function to operate atomically on `array[index]`. |
| [`cupyx.jit.atomic_max`](generated/cupyx.jit.atomic_max.html#cupyx.jit.atomic_max "cupyx.jit.atomic_max")(array, index, value[, ...]) | Calls the `atomicMax` function to operate atomically on `array[index]`. |
| [`cupyx.jit.atomic_inc`](generated/cupyx.jit.atomic_inc.html#cupyx.jit.atomic_inc "cupyx.jit.atomic_inc")(array, index, value[, ...]) | Calls the `atomicInc` function to operate atomically on `array[index]`. |
| [`cupyx.jit.atomic_dec`](generated/cupyx.jit.atomic_dec.html#cupyx.jit.atomic_dec "cupyx.jit.atomic_dec")(array, index, value[, ...]) | Calls the `atomicDec` function to operate atomically on `array[index]`. |
| [`cupyx.jit.atomic_cas`](generated/cupyx.jit.atomic_cas.html#cupyx.jit.atomic_cas "cupyx.jit.atomic_cas")(array, index, value[, ...]) | Calls the `atomicCAS` function to operate atomically on `array[index]`. |
| [`cupyx.jit.atomic_and`](generated/cupyx.jit.atomic_and.html#cupyx.jit.atomic_and "cupyx.jit.atomic_and")(array, index, value[, ...]) | Calls the `atomicAnd` function to operate atomically on `array[index]`. |
| [`cupyx.jit.atomic_or`](generated/cupyx.jit.atomic_or.html#cupyx.jit.atomic_or "cupyx.jit.atomic_or")(array, index, value[, ...]) | Calls the `atomicOr` function to operate atomically on `array[index]`. |
| [`cupyx.jit.atomic_xor`](generated/cupyx.jit.atomic_xor.html#cupyx.jit.atomic_xor "cupyx.jit.atomic_xor")(array, index, value[, ...]) | Calls the `atomicXor` function to operate atomically on `array[index]`. |
| [`cupyx.jit.cg.this_grid`](generated/cupyx.jit.cg.this_grid.html#cupyx.jit.cg.this_grid "cupyx.jit.cg.this_grid")() | Returns the current grid group ([`_GridGroup`](generated/cupyx.jit.cg._GridGroup.html#cupyx.jit.cg._GridGroup "cupyx.jit.cg._GridGroup")). |
| [`cupyx.jit.cg.this_thread_block`](generated/cupyx.jit.cg.this_thread_block.html#cupyx.jit.cg.this_thread_block "cupyx.jit.cg.this_thread_block")() | Returns the current thread block group ([`_ThreadBlockGroup`](generated/cupyx.jit.cg._ThreadBlockGroup.html#cupyx.jit.cg._ThreadBlockGroup "cupyx.jit.cg._ThreadBlockGroup")). |
| [`cupyx.jit.cg.sync`](generated/cupyx.jit.cg.sync.html#cupyx.jit.cg.sync "cupyx.jit.cg.sync")(group) | Calls `cg::sync()`. |
| [`cupyx.jit.cg.memcpy_async`](generated/cupyx.jit.cg.memcpy_async.html#cupyx.jit.cg.memcpy_async "cupyx.jit.cg.memcpy_async")(group, dst, ...[, ...]) | Calls `cg::memcpy_sync()`. |
| [`cupyx.jit.cg.wait`](generated/cupyx.jit.cg.wait.html#cupyx.jit.cg.wait "cupyx.jit.cg.wait")(group) | Calls `cg::wait()`. |
| [`cupyx.jit.cg.wait_prior`](generated/cupyx.jit.cg.wait_prior.html#cupyx.jit.cg.wait_prior "cupyx.jit.cg.wait_prior")(group) | Calls `cg::wait_prior<N>()`. |
| [`cupyx.jit._interface._JitRawKernel`](generated/cupyx.jit._interface._JitRawKernel.html#cupyx.jit._interface._JitRawKernel "cupyx.jit._interface._JitRawKernel")(func, ...) | JIT CUDA kernel object. |

<!-- Source: https://docs.cupy.dev/en/stable/reference/environment.html -->
# Environment variables[#](#environment-variables "Permalink to this heading")
## For runtime[#](#for-runtime "Permalink to this heading")
Here are the environment variables that CuPy uses at runtime.
CUDA\_PATH[#](#envvar-CUDA_PATH "Permalink to this definition")
:   Path to the directory containing CUDA.
    The parent of the directory containing `nvcc` is used as default.
    When `nvcc` is not found, `/usr/local/cuda` is used.
    See [Working with Custom CUDA Installation](../install.html#install-cuda) for details.
CUPY\_CACHE\_DIR[#](#envvar-CUPY_CACHE_DIR "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `${HOME}/.cupy/kernel_cache`
    Path to the directory to store kernel cache.
    See [Performance Best Practices](../user_guide/performance.html) for details.
CUPY\_CACHE\_SAVE\_CUDA\_SOURCE[#](#envvar-CUPY_CACHE_SAVE_CUDA_SOURCE "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0`
    If set to `1`, CUDA source file will be saved along with compiled binary in the cache directory for debug purpose.
    Note: the source file will not be saved if the compiled binary is already stored in the cache.
CUPY\_CACHE\_IN\_MEMORY[#](#envvar-CUPY_CACHE_IN_MEMORY "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0`
    If set to `1`, [`CUPY_CACHE_DIR`](#envvar-CUPY_CACHE_DIR) and [`CUPY_CACHE_SAVE_CUDA_SOURCE`](#envvar-CUPY_CACHE_SAVE_CUDA_SOURCE) will be ignored, and the cache is in memory.
    This environment variable allows reducing disk I/O, but is ignoed when `nvcc` is set to be the compiler backend.
CUPY\_NVRTC\_USE\_PCH[#](#envvar-CUPY_NVRTC_USE_PCH "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0`
    If set to `1`, precompiled headers (PCH) will be used to drastically speed up compilation when many kernels are compiled
    (e.g. because the CuPy kernel cache is empty or due to `CUPY_CACHE_IN_MEMORY`).
    I.e. if set, CuPy always passes the `--pch` option to all NVRTC kernel compilations.
    PCH does come at at the cost of temporary files which can be configured further, see:
    <https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/nvrtc/index.html#precompiled-header-pch-cuda-12-8>
    The option is only available for CUDA 12.8 or later.
CUPY\_DISABLE\_JITIFY\_CACHE[#](#envvar-CUPY_DISABLE_JITIFY_CACHE "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0`
    If set to `1`, headers loaded by Jitify would not be cached on disk (to [`CUPY_CACHE_DIR`](#envvar-CUPY_CACHE_DIR)). The default is to
    always cache.
CUPY\_DUMP\_CUDA\_SOURCE\_ON\_ERROR[#](#envvar-CUPY_DUMP_CUDA_SOURCE_ON_ERROR "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0`
    If set to `1`, when CUDA kernel compilation fails,
    CuPy dumps CUDA kernel code to standard error.
CUPY\_CUDA\_COMPILE\_WITH\_DEBUG[#](#envvar-CUPY_CUDA_COMPILE_WITH_DEBUG "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0`
    If set to `1`, CUDA kernel will be compiled with debug information (`--device-debug` and `--generate-line-info`).
CUPY\_GPU\_MEMORY\_LIMIT[#](#envvar-CUPY_GPU_MEMORY_LIMIT "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0` (unlimited)
    The amount of memory that can be allocated for each device.
    The value can be specified in absolute bytes or fraction (e.g., `"90%"`) of the total memory of each GPU.
    See [Memory Management](../user_guide/memory.html) for details.
CUPY\_SEED[#](#envvar-CUPY_SEED "Permalink to this definition")
:   Set the seed for random number generators.
CUPY\_EXPERIMENTAL\_SLICE\_COPY[#](#envvar-CUPY_EXPERIMENTAL_SLICE_COPY "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0`
    If set to `1`, the following syntax is enabled:
    ```
    cupy_ndarray[:] = numpy_ndarray
    ```
CUPY\_ACCELERATORS[#](#envvar-CUPY_ACCELERATORS "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `"cub"` (In ROCm HIP environment, the default value is `""`. i.e., no accelerators are used.)
    A comma-separated string of backend names (`cub`, `cutensor`, or `cutensornet`) which indicates the acceleration backends used in CuPy operations and its priority (in descending order).
    By default, all accelerators are disabled on HIP and only CUB is enabled on CUDA.
CUPY\_TF32[#](#envvar-CUPY_TF32 "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0`
    If set to `1`, it allows CUDA libraries to use Tensor Cores TF32 compute for 32-bit floating point compute.
CUPY\_CUDA\_ARRAY\_INTERFACE\_SYNC[#](#envvar-CUPY_CUDA_ARRAY_INTERFACE_SYNC "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `1`
    This controls CuPy’s behavior as a Consumer.
    If set to `0`, a stream synchronization will *not* be performed when a device array provided by an external library that implements the CUDA Array Interface is being consumed by CuPy.
    For more detail, see the [Synchronization](https://numba.readthedocs.io/en/latest/cuda/cuda_array_interface.html#synchronization) requirement in the CUDA Array Interface v3 documentation.
CUPY\_CUDA\_ARRAY\_INTERFACE\_EXPORT\_VERSION[#](#envvar-CUPY_CUDA_ARRAY_INTERFACE_EXPORT_VERSION "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `3`
    This controls CuPy’s behavior as a Producer.
    If set to `2`, the CuPy stream on which the data is being operated will not be exported and thus the Consumer (another library) will not perform any stream synchronization.
    For more detail, see the [Synchronization](https://numba.readthedocs.io/en/latest/cuda/cuda_array_interface.html#synchronization) requirement in the CUDA Array Interface v3 documentation.
CUPY\_DLPACK\_EXPORT\_VERSION[#](#envvar-CUPY_DLPACK_EXPORT_VERSION "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0.6`
    This controls CuPy’s DLPack support. Currently, setting a value smaller than 0.6 would disguise managed memory as normal device memory, which enables data exchanges with libraries that have not updated their DLPack support, whereas starting 0.6 CUDA managed memory can be correctly recognized as a valid device type.
NVCC[#](#envvar-NVCC "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `nvcc`
    Define the compiler to use when compiling CUDA source.
    Note that most CuPy kernels are built with NVRTC; this environment variable is only effective for [`RawKernel`](generated/cupy.RawKernel.html#cupy.RawKernel "cupy.RawKernel")/[`RawModule`](generated/cupy.RawModule.html#cupy.RawModule "cupy.RawModule") with the `nvcc` backend or when using `cub` as the accelerator.
CUPY\_CUDA\_PER\_THREAD\_DEFAULT\_STREAM[#](#envvar-CUPY_CUDA_PER_THREAD_DEFAULT_STREAM "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0`
    If set to `1`, CuPy will use the CUDA per-thread default stream, effectively causing each host thread to automatically execute in its own stream, unless the CUDA default (`null`) stream or a user-created stream is specified.
    If set to `0` (default), the CUDA default (`null`) stream is used, unless the per-thread default stream (`ptds`) or a user-created stream is specified.
CUPY\_COMPILE\_WITH\_PTX[#](#envvar-CUPY_COMPILE_WITH_PTX "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0`
    By default, CuPy directly compiles kernels into SASS (CUBIN) to support [CUDA Enhanced Compatibility](https://docs.nvidia.com/deploy/cuda-compatibility/)
    If set to `1`, CuPy instead compiles kernels into PTX and lets CUDA Driver assemble SASS from PTX.
    This option is only effective for CUDA 11.1 or later; CuPy always compiles into PTX on earlier CUDA versions. Also, this option only applies when NVRTC is selected as the compilation backend. NVCC backend always compiles into SASS (CUBIN).
CUDA Toolkit Environment Variables
:   In addition to the environment variables listed above, as in any CUDA programs, all of the CUDA environment variables listed in the [CUDA Toolkit Documentation](https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-c-programming-guide/index.html#env-vars) will also be honored.
Note
When [`CUPY_ACCELERATORS`](#envvar-CUPY_ACCELERATORS) or [`NVCC`](#envvar-NVCC) environment variables are set, g++-6 or later is required as the runtime host compiler.
Please refer to [Installing CuPy from Source](../install.html#install-cupy-from-source) for the details on how to install g++.
## For installation[#](#for-installation "Permalink to this heading")
These environment variables are used during installation (building CuPy from source).
CUTENSOR\_PATH[#](#envvar-CUTENSOR_PATH "Permalink to this definition")
:   Path to the cuTENSOR root directory that contains `lib` and `include` directories. (experimental)
CUPY\_INSTALL\_USE\_HIP[#](#envvar-CUPY_INSTALL_USE_HIP "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0`
    If set to `1`, CuPy is built for AMD ROCm Platform (experimental).
    For building the ROCm support, see [Installing Binary Packages](../install.html#install-hip) for further detail.
CUPY\_USE\_CUDA\_PYTHON[#](#envvar-CUPY_USE_CUDA_PYTHON "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0`
    If set to `1`, CuPy is built using [CUDA Python](https://github.com/NVIDIA/cuda-python).
CUPY\_NVCC\_GENERATE\_CODE[#](#envvar-CUPY_NVCC_GENERATE_CODE "Permalink to this definition")
:   Build CuPy for a particular CUDA architecture. For example:
    ```
    CUPY_NVCC_GENERATE_CODE="arch=compute_60,code=sm_60"
    ```
    For specifying multiple archs, concatenate the `arch=...` strings with semicolons (`;`).
    If `current` is specified, then it will automatically detect the currently installed GPU architectures in build time.
    When this is not set, the default is to support all architectures.
CUPY\_NUM\_BUILD\_JOBS[#](#envvar-CUPY_NUM_BUILD_JOBS "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `4`
    To enable or disable parallel build, sets the number of processes used to build the extensions in parallel.
CUPY\_NUM\_NVCC\_THREADS[#](#envvar-CUPY_NUM_NVCC_THREADS "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `2`
    To enable or disable nvcc parallel compilation, sets the number of threads used to compile files using nvcc.
Additionally, the environment variables [`CUDA_PATH`](#envvar-CUDA_PATH) and [`NVCC`](#envvar-NVCC) are also respected at build time.
[previous
cupyx.distributed.array.matmul](generated/cupyx.distributed.array.matmul.html "previous page")
[next
Comparison Table](comparison.html "next page")
On this page
* [For runtime](#for-runtime)
* [For installation](#for-installation)
---

# Environment variables[#](#environment-variables "Permalink to this heading")
## For runtime[#](#for-runtime "Permalink to this heading")
Here are the environment variables that CuPy uses at runtime.
CUDA\_PATH[#](#envvar-CUDA_PATH "Permalink to this definition")
:   Path to the directory containing CUDA.
    The parent of the directory containing `nvcc` is used as default.
    When `nvcc` is not found, `/usr/local/cuda` is used.
    See [Working with Custom CUDA Installation](../install.html#install-cuda) for details.
CUPY\_CACHE\_DIR[#](#envvar-CUPY_CACHE_DIR "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `${HOME}/.cupy/kernel_cache`
    Path to the directory to store kernel cache.
    See [Performance Best Practices](../user_guide/performance.html) for details.
CUPY\_CACHE\_SAVE\_CUDA\_SOURCE[#](#envvar-CUPY_CACHE_SAVE_CUDA_SOURCE "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0`
    If set to `1`, CUDA source file will be saved along with compiled binary in the cache directory for debug purpose.
    Note: the source file will not be saved if the compiled binary is already stored in the cache.
CUPY\_CACHE\_IN\_MEMORY[#](#envvar-CUPY_CACHE_IN_MEMORY "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0`
    If set to `1`, [`CUPY_CACHE_DIR`](#envvar-CUPY_CACHE_DIR) and [`CUPY_CACHE_SAVE_CUDA_SOURCE`](#envvar-CUPY_CACHE_SAVE_CUDA_SOURCE) will be ignored, and the cache is in memory.
    This environment variable allows reducing disk I/O, but is ignoed when `nvcc` is set to be the compiler backend.
CUPY\_NVRTC\_USE\_PCH[#](#envvar-CUPY_NVRTC_USE_PCH "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0`
    If set to `1`, precompiled headers (PCH) will be used to drastically speed up compilation when many kernels are compiled
    (e.g. because the CuPy kernel cache is empty or due to `CUPY_CACHE_IN_MEMORY`).
    I.e. if set, CuPy always passes the `--pch` option to all NVRTC kernel compilations.
    PCH does come at at the cost of temporary files which can be configured further, see:
    <https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/nvrtc/index.html#precompiled-header-pch-cuda-12-8>
    The option is only available for CUDA 12.8 or later.
CUPY\_DISABLE\_JITIFY\_CACHE[#](#envvar-CUPY_DISABLE_JITIFY_CACHE "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0`
    If set to `1`, headers loaded by Jitify would not be cached on disk (to [`CUPY_CACHE_DIR`](#envvar-CUPY_CACHE_DIR)). The default is to
    always cache.
CUPY\_DUMP\_CUDA\_SOURCE\_ON\_ERROR[#](#envvar-CUPY_DUMP_CUDA_SOURCE_ON_ERROR "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0`
    If set to `1`, when CUDA kernel compilation fails,
    CuPy dumps CUDA kernel code to standard error.
CUPY\_CUDA\_COMPILE\_WITH\_DEBUG[#](#envvar-CUPY_CUDA_COMPILE_WITH_DEBUG "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0`
    If set to `1`, CUDA kernel will be compiled with debug information (`--device-debug` and `--generate-line-info`).
CUPY\_GPU\_MEMORY\_LIMIT[#](#envvar-CUPY_GPU_MEMORY_LIMIT "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0` (unlimited)
    The amount of memory that can be allocated for each device.
    The value can be specified in absolute bytes or fraction (e.g., `"90%"`) of the total memory of each GPU.
    See [Memory Management](../user_guide/memory.html) for details.
CUPY\_SEED[#](#envvar-CUPY_SEED "Permalink to this definition")
:   Set the seed for random number generators.
CUPY\_EXPERIMENTAL\_SLICE\_COPY[#](#envvar-CUPY_EXPERIMENTAL_SLICE_COPY "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0`
    If set to `1`, the following syntax is enabled:
    ```
    cupy_ndarray[:] = numpy_ndarray
    ```
CUPY\_ACCELERATORS[#](#envvar-CUPY_ACCELERATORS "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `"cub"` (In ROCm HIP environment, the default value is `""`. i.e., no accelerators are used.)
    A comma-separated string of backend names (`cub`, `cutensor`, or `cutensornet`) which indicates the acceleration backends used in CuPy operations and its priority (in descending order).
    By default, all accelerators are disabled on HIP and only CUB is enabled on CUDA.
CUPY\_TF32[#](#envvar-CUPY_TF32 "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0`
    If set to `1`, it allows CUDA libraries to use Tensor Cores TF32 compute for 32-bit floating point compute.
CUPY\_CUDA\_ARRAY\_INTERFACE\_SYNC[#](#envvar-CUPY_CUDA_ARRAY_INTERFACE_SYNC "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `1`
    This controls CuPy’s behavior as a Consumer.
    If set to `0`, a stream synchronization will *not* be performed when a device array provided by an external library that implements the CUDA Array Interface is being consumed by CuPy.
    For more detail, see the [Synchronization](https://numba.readthedocs.io/en/latest/cuda/cuda_array_interface.html#synchronization) requirement in the CUDA Array Interface v3 documentation.
CUPY\_CUDA\_ARRAY\_INTERFACE\_EXPORT\_VERSION[#](#envvar-CUPY_CUDA_ARRAY_INTERFACE_EXPORT_VERSION "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `3`
    This controls CuPy’s behavior as a Producer.
    If set to `2`, the CuPy stream on which the data is being operated will not be exported and thus the Consumer (another library) will not perform any stream synchronization.
    For more detail, see the [Synchronization](https://numba.readthedocs.io/en/latest/cuda/cuda_array_interface.html#synchronization) requirement in the CUDA Array Interface v3 documentation.
CUPY\_DLPACK\_EXPORT\_VERSION[#](#envvar-CUPY_DLPACK_EXPORT_VERSION "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0.6`
    This controls CuPy’s DLPack support. Currently, setting a value smaller than 0.6 would disguise managed memory as normal device memory, which enables data exchanges with libraries that have not updated their DLPack support, whereas starting 0.6 CUDA managed memory can be correctly recognized as a valid device type.
NVCC[#](#envvar-NVCC "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `nvcc`
    Define the compiler to use when compiling CUDA source.
    Note that most CuPy kernels are built with NVRTC; this environment variable is only effective for [`RawKernel`](generated/cupy.RawKernel.html#cupy.RawKernel "cupy.RawKernel")/[`RawModule`](generated/cupy.RawModule.html#cupy.RawModule "cupy.RawModule") with the `nvcc` backend or when using `cub` as the accelerator.
CUPY\_CUDA\_PER\_THREAD\_DEFAULT\_STREAM[#](#envvar-CUPY_CUDA_PER_THREAD_DEFAULT_STREAM "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0`
    If set to `1`, CuPy will use the CUDA per-thread default stream, effectively causing each host thread to automatically execute in its own stream, unless the CUDA default (`null`) stream or a user-created stream is specified.
    If set to `0` (default), the CUDA default (`null`) stream is used, unless the per-thread default stream (`ptds`) or a user-created stream is specified.
CUPY\_COMPILE\_WITH\_PTX[#](#envvar-CUPY_COMPILE_WITH_PTX "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0`
    By default, CuPy directly compiles kernels into SASS (CUBIN) to support [CUDA Enhanced Compatibility](https://docs.nvidia.com/deploy/cuda-compatibility/)
    If set to `1`, CuPy instead compiles kernels into PTX and lets CUDA Driver assemble SASS from PTX.
    This option is only effective for CUDA 11.1 or later; CuPy always compiles into PTX on earlier CUDA versions. Also, this option only applies when NVRTC is selected as the compilation backend. NVCC backend always compiles into SASS (CUBIN).
CUDA Toolkit Environment Variables
:   In addition to the environment variables listed above, as in any CUDA programs, all of the CUDA environment variables listed in the [CUDA Toolkit Documentation](https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-c-programming-guide/index.html#env-vars) will also be honored.
Note
When [`CUPY_ACCELERATORS`](#envvar-CUPY_ACCELERATORS) or [`NVCC`](#envvar-NVCC) environment variables are set, g++-6 or later is required as the runtime host compiler.
Please refer to [Installing CuPy from Source](../install.html#install-cupy-from-source) for the details on how to install g++.
## For installation[#](#for-installation "Permalink to this heading")
These environment variables are used during installation (building CuPy from source).
CUTENSOR\_PATH[#](#envvar-CUTENSOR_PATH "Permalink to this definition")
:   Path to the cuTENSOR root directory that contains `lib` and `include` directories. (experimental)
CUPY\_INSTALL\_USE\_HIP[#](#envvar-CUPY_INSTALL_USE_HIP "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0`
    If set to `1`, CuPy is built for AMD ROCm Platform (experimental).
    For building the ROCm support, see [Installing Binary Packages](../install.html#install-hip) for further detail.
CUPY\_USE\_CUDA\_PYTHON[#](#envvar-CUPY_USE_CUDA_PYTHON "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0`
    If set to `1`, CuPy is built using [CUDA Python](https://github.com/NVIDIA/cuda-python).
CUPY\_NVCC\_GENERATE\_CODE[#](#envvar-CUPY_NVCC_GENERATE_CODE "Permalink to this definition")
:   Build CuPy for a particular CUDA architecture. For example:
    ```
    CUPY_NVCC_GENERATE_CODE="arch=compute_60,code=sm_60"
    ```
    For specifying multiple archs, concatenate the `arch=...` strings with semicolons (`;`).
    If `current` is specified, then it will automatically detect the currently installed GPU architectures in build time.
    When this is not set, the default is to support all architectures.
CUPY\_NUM\_BUILD\_JOBS[#](#envvar-CUPY_NUM_BUILD_JOBS "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `4`
    To enable or disable parallel build, sets the number of processes used to build the extensions in parallel.
CUPY\_NUM\_NVCC\_THREADS[#](#envvar-CUPY_NUM_NVCC_THREADS "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `2`
    To enable or disable nvcc parallel compilation, sets the number of threads used to compile files using nvcc.
Additionally, the environment variables [`CUDA_PATH`](#envvar-CUDA_PATH) and [`NVCC`](#envvar-NVCC) are also respected at build time.

# Environment variables[#](#environment-variables "Permalink to this heading")
## For runtime[#](#for-runtime "Permalink to this heading")
Here are the environment variables that CuPy uses at runtime.
CUDA\_PATH[#](#envvar-CUDA_PATH "Permalink to this definition")
:   Path to the directory containing CUDA.
    The parent of the directory containing `nvcc` is used as default.
    When `nvcc` is not found, `/usr/local/cuda` is used.
    See [Working with Custom CUDA Installation](../install.html#install-cuda) for details.
CUPY\_CACHE\_DIR[#](#envvar-CUPY_CACHE_DIR "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `${HOME}/.cupy/kernel_cache`
    Path to the directory to store kernel cache.
    See [Performance Best Practices](../user_guide/performance.html) for details.
CUPY\_CACHE\_SAVE\_CUDA\_SOURCE[#](#envvar-CUPY_CACHE_SAVE_CUDA_SOURCE "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0`
    If set to `1`, CUDA source file will be saved along with compiled binary in the cache directory for debug purpose.
    Note: the source file will not be saved if the compiled binary is already stored in the cache.
CUPY\_CACHE\_IN\_MEMORY[#](#envvar-CUPY_CACHE_IN_MEMORY "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0`
    If set to `1`, [`CUPY_CACHE_DIR`](#envvar-CUPY_CACHE_DIR) and [`CUPY_CACHE_SAVE_CUDA_SOURCE`](#envvar-CUPY_CACHE_SAVE_CUDA_SOURCE) will be ignored, and the cache is in memory.
    This environment variable allows reducing disk I/O, but is ignoed when `nvcc` is set to be the compiler backend.
CUPY\_NVRTC\_USE\_PCH[#](#envvar-CUPY_NVRTC_USE_PCH "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0`
    If set to `1`, precompiled headers (PCH) will be used to drastically speed up compilation when many kernels are compiled
    (e.g. because the CuPy kernel cache is empty or due to `CUPY_CACHE_IN_MEMORY`).
    I.e. if set, CuPy always passes the `--pch` option to all NVRTC kernel compilations.
    PCH does come at at the cost of temporary files which can be configured further, see:
    <https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/nvrtc/index.html#precompiled-header-pch-cuda-12-8>
    The option is only available for CUDA 12.8 or later.
CUPY\_DISABLE\_JITIFY\_CACHE[#](#envvar-CUPY_DISABLE_JITIFY_CACHE "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0`
    If set to `1`, headers loaded by Jitify would not be cached on disk (to [`CUPY_CACHE_DIR`](#envvar-CUPY_CACHE_DIR)). The default is to
    always cache.
CUPY\_DUMP\_CUDA\_SOURCE\_ON\_ERROR[#](#envvar-CUPY_DUMP_CUDA_SOURCE_ON_ERROR "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0`
    If set to `1`, when CUDA kernel compilation fails,
    CuPy dumps CUDA kernel code to standard error.
CUPY\_CUDA\_COMPILE\_WITH\_DEBUG[#](#envvar-CUPY_CUDA_COMPILE_WITH_DEBUG "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0`
    If set to `1`, CUDA kernel will be compiled with debug information (`--device-debug` and `--generate-line-info`).
CUPY\_GPU\_MEMORY\_LIMIT[#](#envvar-CUPY_GPU_MEMORY_LIMIT "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0` (unlimited)
    The amount of memory that can be allocated for each device.
    The value can be specified in absolute bytes or fraction (e.g., `"90%"`) of the total memory of each GPU.
    See [Memory Management](../user_guide/memory.html) for details.
CUPY\_SEED[#](#envvar-CUPY_SEED "Permalink to this definition")
:   Set the seed for random number generators.
CUPY\_EXPERIMENTAL\_SLICE\_COPY[#](#envvar-CUPY_EXPERIMENTAL_SLICE_COPY "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0`
    If set to `1`, the following syntax is enabled:
    ```
    cupy_ndarray[:] = numpy_ndarray
    ```
CUPY\_ACCELERATORS[#](#envvar-CUPY_ACCELERATORS "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `"cub"` (In ROCm HIP environment, the default value is `""`. i.e., no accelerators are used.)
    A comma-separated string of backend names (`cub`, `cutensor`, or `cutensornet`) which indicates the acceleration backends used in CuPy operations and its priority (in descending order).
    By default, all accelerators are disabled on HIP and only CUB is enabled on CUDA.
CUPY\_TF32[#](#envvar-CUPY_TF32 "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0`
    If set to `1`, it allows CUDA libraries to use Tensor Cores TF32 compute for 32-bit floating point compute.
CUPY\_CUDA\_ARRAY\_INTERFACE\_SYNC[#](#envvar-CUPY_CUDA_ARRAY_INTERFACE_SYNC "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `1`
    This controls CuPy’s behavior as a Consumer.
    If set to `0`, a stream synchronization will *not* be performed when a device array provided by an external library that implements the CUDA Array Interface is being consumed by CuPy.
    For more detail, see the [Synchronization](https://numba.readthedocs.io/en/latest/cuda/cuda_array_interface.html#synchronization) requirement in the CUDA Array Interface v3 documentation.
CUPY\_CUDA\_ARRAY\_INTERFACE\_EXPORT\_VERSION[#](#envvar-CUPY_CUDA_ARRAY_INTERFACE_EXPORT_VERSION "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `3`
    This controls CuPy’s behavior as a Producer.
    If set to `2`, the CuPy stream on which the data is being operated will not be exported and thus the Consumer (another library) will not perform any stream synchronization.
    For more detail, see the [Synchronization](https://numba.readthedocs.io/en/latest/cuda/cuda_array_interface.html#synchronization) requirement in the CUDA Array Interface v3 documentation.
CUPY\_DLPACK\_EXPORT\_VERSION[#](#envvar-CUPY_DLPACK_EXPORT_VERSION "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0.6`
    This controls CuPy’s DLPack support. Currently, setting a value smaller than 0.6 would disguise managed memory as normal device memory, which enables data exchanges with libraries that have not updated their DLPack support, whereas starting 0.6 CUDA managed memory can be correctly recognized as a valid device type.
NVCC[#](#envvar-NVCC "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `nvcc`
    Define the compiler to use when compiling CUDA source.
    Note that most CuPy kernels are built with NVRTC; this environment variable is only effective for [`RawKernel`](generated/cupy.RawKernel.html#cupy.RawKernel "cupy.RawKernel")/[`RawModule`](generated/cupy.RawModule.html#cupy.RawModule "cupy.RawModule") with the `nvcc` backend or when using `cub` as the accelerator.
CUPY\_CUDA\_PER\_THREAD\_DEFAULT\_STREAM[#](#envvar-CUPY_CUDA_PER_THREAD_DEFAULT_STREAM "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0`
    If set to `1`, CuPy will use the CUDA per-thread default stream, effectively causing each host thread to automatically execute in its own stream, unless the CUDA default (`null`) stream or a user-created stream is specified.
    If set to `0` (default), the CUDA default (`null`) stream is used, unless the per-thread default stream (`ptds`) or a user-created stream is specified.
CUPY\_COMPILE\_WITH\_PTX[#](#envvar-CUPY_COMPILE_WITH_PTX "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0`
    By default, CuPy directly compiles kernels into SASS (CUBIN) to support [CUDA Enhanced Compatibility](https://docs.nvidia.com/deploy/cuda-compatibility/)
    If set to `1`, CuPy instead compiles kernels into PTX and lets CUDA Driver assemble SASS from PTX.
    This option is only effective for CUDA 11.1 or later; CuPy always compiles into PTX on earlier CUDA versions. Also, this option only applies when NVRTC is selected as the compilation backend. NVCC backend always compiles into SASS (CUBIN).
CUDA Toolkit Environment Variables
:   In addition to the environment variables listed above, as in any CUDA programs, all of the CUDA environment variables listed in the [CUDA Toolkit Documentation](https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-c-programming-guide/index.html#env-vars) will also be honored.
Note
When [`CUPY_ACCELERATORS`](#envvar-CUPY_ACCELERATORS) or [`NVCC`](#envvar-NVCC) environment variables are set, g++-6 or later is required as the runtime host compiler.
Please refer to [Installing CuPy from Source](../install.html#install-cupy-from-source) for the details on how to install g++.
## For installation[#](#for-installation "Permalink to this heading")
These environment variables are used during installation (building CuPy from source).
CUTENSOR\_PATH[#](#envvar-CUTENSOR_PATH "Permalink to this definition")
:   Path to the cuTENSOR root directory that contains `lib` and `include` directories. (experimental)
CUPY\_INSTALL\_USE\_HIP[#](#envvar-CUPY_INSTALL_USE_HIP "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0`
    If set to `1`, CuPy is built for AMD ROCm Platform (experimental).
    For building the ROCm support, see [Installing Binary Packages](../install.html#install-hip) for further detail.
CUPY\_USE\_CUDA\_PYTHON[#](#envvar-CUPY_USE_CUDA_PYTHON "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `0`
    If set to `1`, CuPy is built using [CUDA Python](https://github.com/NVIDIA/cuda-python).
CUPY\_NVCC\_GENERATE\_CODE[#](#envvar-CUPY_NVCC_GENERATE_CODE "Permalink to this definition")
:   Build CuPy for a particular CUDA architecture. For example:
    ```
    CUPY_NVCC_GENERATE_CODE="arch=compute_60,code=sm_60"
    ```
    For specifying multiple archs, concatenate the `arch=...` strings with semicolons (`;`).
    If `current` is specified, then it will automatically detect the currently installed GPU architectures in build time.
    When this is not set, the default is to support all architectures.
CUPY\_NUM\_BUILD\_JOBS[#](#envvar-CUPY_NUM_BUILD_JOBS "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `4`
    To enable or disable parallel build, sets the number of processes used to build the extensions in parallel.
CUPY\_NUM\_NVCC\_THREADS[#](#envvar-CUPY_NUM_NVCC_THREADS "Permalink to this definition")
:   Default: `2`
    To enable or disable nvcc parallel compilation, sets the number of threads used to compile files using nvcc.
Additionally, the environment variables [`CUDA_PATH`](#envvar-CUDA_PATH) and [`NVCC`](#envvar-NVCC) are also respected at build time.

<!-- Source: https://docs.cupy.dev/en/stable/reference/comparison.html -->
# Comparison Table[#](#comparison-table "Permalink to this heading")
Here is a list of NumPy / SciPy APIs and its corresponding CuPy implementations.
`-` in CuPy column denotes that CuPy implementation is not provided yet.
We welcome contributions for these functions.
## NumPy / CuPy APIs[#](#numpy-cupy-apis "Permalink to this heading")
### Module-Level[#](#module-level "Permalink to this heading")
| NumPy | CuPy |
| --- | --- |
| `numpy.abs` | `cupy.abs` |
| [`numpy.absolute`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.absolute.html#numpy.absolute "(in NumPy v2.4)") | [`cupy.absolute`](generated/cupy.absolute.html#cupy.absolute "cupy.absolute") |
| [`numpy.acos`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.acos.html#numpy.acos "(in NumPy v2.4)") | [`cupy.acos`](generated/cupy.acos.html#cupy.acos "cupy.acos") |
| [`numpy.acosh`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.acosh.html#numpy.acosh "(in NumPy v2.4)") | [`cupy.acosh`](generated/cupy.acosh.html#cupy.acosh "cupy.acosh") |
| [`numpy.add`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.add.html#numpy.add "(in NumPy v2.4)") | [`cupy.add`](generated/cupy.add.html#cupy.add "cupy.add") |
| [`numpy.all`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.all.html#numpy.all "(in NumPy v2.4)") | [`cupy.all`](generated/cupy.all.html#cupy.all "cupy.all") |
| [`numpy.allclose`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.allclose.html#numpy.allclose "(in NumPy v2.4)") | [`cupy.allclose`](generated/cupy.allclose.html#cupy.allclose "cupy.allclose") |
| [`numpy.amax`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.amax.html#numpy.amax "(in NumPy v2.4)") | [`cupy.amax`](generated/cupy.amax.html#cupy.amax "cupy.amax") |
| [`numpy.amin`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.amin.html#numpy.amin "(in NumPy v2.4)") | [`cupy.amin`](generated/cupy.amin.html#cupy.amin "cupy.amin") |
| [`numpy.angle`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.angle.html#numpy.angle "(in NumPy v2.4)") | [`cupy.angle`](generated/cupy.angle.html#cupy.angle "cupy.angle") |
| [`numpy.any`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.any.html#numpy.any "(in NumPy v2.4)") | [`cupy.any`](generated/cupy.any.html#cupy.any "cupy.any") |
| [`numpy.append`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.append.html#numpy.append "(in NumPy v2.4)") | [`cupy.append`](generated/cupy.append.html#cupy.append "cupy.append") |
| [`numpy.apply_along_axis`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.apply_along_axis.html#numpy.apply_along_axis "(in NumPy v2.4)") | [`cupy.apply_along_axis`](generated/cupy.apply_along_axis.html#cupy.apply_along_axis "cupy.apply_along_axis") |
| [`numpy.apply_over_axes`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.apply_over_axes.html#numpy.apply_over_axes "(in NumPy v2.4)") | [`cupy.apply_over_axes`](generated/cupy.apply_over_axes.html#cupy.apply_over_axes "cupy.apply_over_axes") |
| [`numpy.arange`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.arange.html#numpy.arange "(in NumPy v2.4)") | [`cupy.arange`](generated/cupy.arange.html#cupy.arange "cupy.arange") |
| [`numpy.arccos`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.arccos.html#numpy.arccos "(in NumPy v2.4)") | [`cupy.arccos`](generated/cupy.arccos.html#cupy.arccos "cupy.arccos") |
| [`numpy.arccosh`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.arccosh.html#numpy.arccosh "(in NumPy v2.4)") | [`cupy.arccosh`](generated/cupy.arccosh.html#cupy.arccosh "cupy.arccosh") |
| [`numpy.arcsin`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.arcsin.html#numpy.arcsin "(in NumPy v2.4)") | [`cupy.arcsin`](generated/cupy.arcsin.html#cupy.arcsin "cupy.arcsin") |

### Masked Arrays[#](#masked-arrays "Permalink to this heading")
CuPy does not implement the NumPy masked array API. An alternative is [MArray](https://github.com/mdhaber/marray), which adds mask support to Python Array API Standard-compatible array backends, including CuPy.
